Use the smallest diaper to prevent hip abduction.
Monitor stimuli (e.g., light, noise, handling, and procedures) in the infant's environment and reduce when possible.
Dim the ambient light.
Protect the infant's eyes when using high-intensity lights.
Change ambient lighting to provide a diurnal rhythm.
Decrease ambient noise (e.g., turn off and promptly respond to monitor and telephone alarms, and converse away from the bedside).
Position the incubator away from sources of noise (e.g., sinks, doors, telephone, high activity, radio, and traffic).
Plan infant care and feeding close to sleep-wake cycles.
Gather and prepare necessary equipment away from the bedside.
Cluster care to promote the longest possible sleep interval and energy conservation.
Place the infant to sleep in the supine position supported on the parent's chest, if appropriate.
Provide a comfortable chair in a quiet area for feeding.
Use slow, gentle movements when handling, feeding, and caring for the infant.
Position and support throughout feeding, maintaining flexion and midline position (e.g., support shoulders and trunk, wrap feet, hold hands, use wraps or cloths).
Feed in an elevated supine position to promote tongue extension and swallowing.
Promote parental participation in feeding.
Support breastfeeding.
Monitor intake and output.
Use a pacifier during tube feeding and between feedings for non-nutritive sucking to promote physiological stability and nutritional status.
Facilitate state transition and calming during painful and stressful, yet necessary, procedures.
Establish a consistent and predictable routine to promote regular wake-sleep cycles.
Provide stimulation using recordings of musical instruments, toys, massage, rocking, and touch.
Monitor and manage oxygen needs.
Cover eyes and genitals with opaque protection for children receiving phototherapy.
Remove eye mask during feeding and regularly to monitor for secretions or corneal irritation.
Monitor hematocrit and administer blood transfusions when needed.
Inform parents about prevention measures for SIDS (sudden infant death syndrome).

definicao: Providing care to the child during the transition from birth to extrauterine life and the subsequent stabilization period.
atividades: Clear secretions from the nasal and oral passages.
Perform the Apgar test at 1 and 5 minutes after birth.
Measure and weigh the newborn.
Monitor the newborn's temperature.
Maintain adequate body temperature for the newborn (e.g., dry the infant immediately after birth, swaddle the newborn in

definicao: Assisting the patient in the correct use of contact lenses.
atividades: Monitor the eyes and surrounding area for open wounds and bruising.
Determine the patient's understanding of necessary lens care.
Determine the patient's physical and emotional ability to learn and perform necessary lens care.
Instruct the patient to perform hand hygiene before touching the lenses.
Instruct the patient on proper contact lens care according to type (e.g., rigid or flexible).
Instruct the patient to remove lenses at the appropriate interval (e.g., remove daily-wear lenses at night and do not use disposable lenses more than once).
Instruct the patient using extended-wear contact lenses about increased risks (e.g., corneal injuries and rashes due to infection).
Instruct the patient using rigid contact lenses about increased risks (e.g., corneal edema and corneal abrasions).
Instruct the patient on symptoms to report to the healthcare provider (e.g., eye and conjunctival redness, discomfort or pain, excessive tearing, and visual changes).
Instruct the patient to use recommended solutions for cleaning, wetting, rinsing, and disinfecting the lenses.
Instruct the patient to rub and rinse the lenses with recommended solution before and after storage.
Instruct the patient on the importance of monitoring and discontinuing use of recalled contact lens products.
Instruct the patient not to use saliva, tap water, or sterile saline found in healthcare institutions for washing or storing lenses.
Instruct the patient to avoid exposing eyes to tap water, pool water, or spa water when wearing lenses.
Instruct the patient to store lenses in the appropriate container with the recommended solution.
Instruct the patient on the care of the lens storage container (e.g., clean daily, leave open to air, and replace regularly).
Instruct the patient on how to examine lenses for damage.
Instruct the patient using eye cosmetics to be cautious in their choice and application (i.e., choose cosmetics without irritating properties and apply before lens placement).
Instruct the patient to avoid exposure to or use of environmental contaminants that may cause damage or irritation (e.g., dust, smoke, soaps, lotions, creams, and sprays).
Instruct the patient to carry proper identification regarding lens type and care.
Perform lens care for the patient unable to do so themselves (e.g., removal, cleaning, storage, and insertion).

definicao: Prevention of complications and promotion of wound healing.
atividades: Remove the covering and tape.
Shave hair around the affected area, if needed.
Monitor wound characteristics, including drainage, color, size, and odor.
Measure the wound bed, as appropriate.
Remove any foreign material embedded in the wound (e.g., splinter, stinger, glass, gravel, metal), if needed.
Cleanse with normal saline or non-toxic substance, as appropriate.
Soak the affected area, as appropriate.
Provide incision site care, if needed.
Perform skin wound care, if needed.
Apply appropriate ointment to the skin/wound, as appropriate.
Apply an appropriate covering for the wound type.
Reinforce the covering, if needed.
Maintain sterile technique during wound dressing, as appropriate.
Change the covering according to the amount of exudate and drainage.
Examine the wound at each dressing change.
Compare and regularly record all wound changes.
Position the patient to avoid tension on the wound, as appropriate.
Reposition the patient at least every 2 hours, as appropriate.
Encourage fluid intake, as appropriate.
Refer the patient to a wound specialist, as appropriate.
Refer the patient to a dietitian, as appropriate.
Apply a TENS unit (transcutaneous electrical nerve stimulation) to enhance wound healing, as appropriate.
Position pressure-relieving devices (i.e., low-air-loss, foam, or gel mattresses; elbow and heel protectors; chair cushion), as appropriate.
Assist the patient and family in obtaining necessary materials.
Instruct the patient and family on storage and disposal of dressings and materials.
Instruct the patient and family on wound care procedures.
Instruct the patient and family about signs and symptoms of infection.
Document wound location, size, and appearance.

definicao: Maintenance of a pressure drainage system at the wound site.
atividades: Gather necessary equipment and materials at the bedside (e.g., specimen container, absorbent pad, and gloves).
Assist the patient into a comfortable position.
Avoid transferring microorganisms (i.e., wash hands and put on clean disposable gloves).
Expose the drain insertion site and drainage system.
Check the pump and drain for patency, seal, and stability, taking care to avoid inadvertent suture removal, if any.
Monitor for signs of infection, inflammation, and discomfort around the drain.
Notify the appropriate healthcare professional if there are obstructed drains, signs of infection or discomfort, displaced drains, or saturated drainage system.
Remove the cap or disconnect tubes, depending on the type of drainage system (e.g., Hemovac® or Jackson-Pratt®).
Empty the drainage into the specimen container, avoiding contamination of the drain tube.
Clean the drain tube with an antiseptic swab.
Compress the drainage system and hold firmly while the cap is reinserted or the tube is connected.
Position the system appropriately (i.e., avoid twisting the tubing and secure it to the patient's clothing or linens, as appropriate).
Record the volume and characteristics of the drainage (e.g., color, consistency, and odor).
Compress the system to provide suction at regular time intervals according to institutional policies.
Number the collection devices if more than one is present.
Properly dispose of contaminated materials.

definicao: Care and prevention of complications of a malignant wound or other wound with no expectation of healing.
atividades: Provide adequate pain management (e.g., relaxation, distraction, analgesic therapy to be administered before and after dressing).
Agree to take breaks during wound care procedures.
Moisten gauze with normal saline before removal, as appropriate.
Describe wound characteristics, noting size, location, drainage, color, bleeding, pain, odor, and edema.
Record observed changes over the course of the wound.
Observe for signs and symptoms of wound infection.
Observe for signs of dermatitis on the skin around the wound and use barrier creams, as appropriate.
Irrigate the wound with water or normal saline, avoiding excessive pressure.
Avoid rubbing when cleaning.
Avoid the use of antiseptics.
Clean the wound, starting from the cleanest area and moving towards the dirtiest.
Carefully dry the skin around the wound.
Avoid chemical or mechanical removal of tissue.
Apply topical medication (cytostatic, antibiotic, analgesic) as needed.

definicao: Prevention of complications of burn injury and facilitation of its healing.
atividades: Cool the burn with lukewarm water (20°C) or normal saline at the time of injury, if possible.
Wash chemical injuries continuously for at least 30 minutes to ensure complete removal of the causative agent.
Determine the entry and exit areas of electrical burns to assess potentially involved organs.
Obtain an electrocardiogram (ECG) if the patient presents with electrical burns.
Increase the temperature of the patient who has sustained cold burns.
Maintain a clear airway to ensure ventilation.
Monitor the level of consciousness of patients with large burns.
Assess the patient's mouth and nasal passages for possible inhalation injury.
Assess the wound, examining its depth, extent, location, pain, causative agent, exudate, granulation or necrotic tissue, epithelialization, and signs of infection.
Administer tetanus vaccine, as appropriate.
Use physical isolation measures to prevent infection (e.g., mask, gown, sterile gloves, cap, and foot covers).
Inform the patient about the procedure to be followed for wound dressing.
Offer comfort measures before dressing change.
Use a sterile field and maintain maximum asepsis throughout the process.
Remove the outer dressing/bandage, cutting it and moistening it with normal saline or sterile water.
Perform wound debridement, as appropriate.
Apply topical agents to the wound, if needed.
Apply an occlusive dressing without applying compression.
Position the patient to preserve extremity and joint functionality and prevent contracture.
Provide adequate pain control with pharmacological and non-pharmacological measures.
Provide skin care at donor and graft sites.
Ensure adequate nutrient and fluid intake.
Administer gamma globulin to prevent fluid shift, if needed.
Assist the patient in determining the real extent of physical and functional changes.
Offer the patient options for cosmetic correction.
Recommend methods of protecting the affected part.
Assist the patient in accepting physical changes and adapting them to their lifestyle (e.g., sexual, family, professional, and social).
Provide emotional and acceptance support throughout the treatment.

definicao: Facilitation of healing of pressure injuries.
atividades: Describe wound characteristics at regular intervals, including size (length × width × depth), stage (I-IV), location, exudate, granulation or necrotic tissue, and epithelialization.
Monitor the color, temperature, edema, moisture, and appearance of surrounding skin.
Keep the wound moist to aid healing.
Apply moist heat to the wound to improve blood perfusion and oxygen supply to the area.
Clean the skin around the wound with mild soap and water.
Debride the wound as needed.
Clean the wound with the appropriate non-toxic solution, working in circular motions from the center.
Use a 19-gauge needle and a 35 cc syringe to clean deep wounds.
Note the characteristics of any drainage.
Apply a permeable adhesive membrane over the wound, as appropriate.
Apply normal saline compresses, as appropriate.
Apply ointments, as appropriate.
Apply dressings, as appropriate.
Administer oral medications, as appropriate.
Monitor for signs and symptoms of wound infection.
Position the patient every 1 to 2 hours to avoid prolonged pressure.
Use appropriate beds and mattresses, as appropriate.
Use equipment in the bed that protects the individual.
Ensure adequate nutrition.

definicao: Promotion of healthy, clean, attractive hair and scalp.
atividades: Monitor hair and scalp conditions, including abnormalities (e.g., dry, coarse, or brittle hair, pest infestation, dandruff, and nutritional deficiencies).
Offer treatment for abnormalities or notify the appropriate healthcare professional.
Prepare materials for hair cleansing (e.g., basin, shampoo board, waterproof pad, towel, shampoo, and conditioner).
Assist the patient into a comfortable position.
Use hydrogen peroxide or alcohol to dissolve dried blood, if present, before cleaning the hair.

Place a commercially prepared disposable cleansing cap on the patient's head and massage for the solution to work on the hair and scalp, ensuring the cap is used according to manufacturer's instructions.
Wash and condition the hair, massaging the shampoo and conditioner into the hair and scalp.
Avoid cooling during cleansing (i.e., adjust room temperature and provide warmed towels).
Dry the hair with a hair dryer on a low setting to prevent scalp burns.
Brush or comb the hair using a wide-tooth comb or pick as needed.
Apply a small amount of oil to dry or flaky areas of the scalp.
Style the hair.

Monitor the patient's response to hair loss, providing support (i.e., assist in choosing a hat, wig, or scarf, refer to a community agency, and discuss hair transplants and hair growth stimulating medications), if indicated.
Provide a barber or hairdresser for a haircut.
Prepare materials for shaving (e.g., shaving cream, towel, and a safety razor or electric shaver).
Shave body hair using an electric shaver for patients at risk of excessive bleeding, if desired.
Perform hair removal procedures using scissors, hair clippers, or chemical depilatories before surgical procedures, ensuring to consult institutional policies and medical orders.
Instruct the patient or significant others on hair care (e.g., cleansing the child's scalp and hair, preventing pest infestation).
Refer, as appropriate.

definicao: Insertion and maintenance of a peripherally inserted catheter for central circulation access.
atividades: Identify the use of the catheter to determine the type needed (e.g., vesicant or potentially irritant medications should be administered via central access, not peripheral).
Explain the purpose of the catheter, benefits, and risks associated with its use to the patient/family.
Obtain consent for the insertion procedure.
Choose the appropriate catheter size and type to meet patient needs.
Choose the most accessible and least used antecubital vein available (usually the basilic or cephalic vein of the dominant arm).

Ensure the patient is an appropriate candidate for insertion (e.g., veins must be visible or palpable; the skin over the site should show no evidence of infection or injury; the patient should not have sepsis, abnormal bleeding, history of non-cooperation with previous line placements, or lack of prior informed consent).
Determine the desired catheter tip placement (e.g., superior vena cava or brachiocephalic, axillary, or subclavian veins).
Instruct the patient that the dominant arm is used when placement is in the superior vena cava to increase blood flow and prevent edema.
Position the patient supine for insertion with the arm at a 90-degree angle to the body.
Measure arm circumference.
Measure the distance for catheter insertion.
Prepare the insertion site according to institutional protocol.
Instruct the patient to turn their head toward the arm to be cannulated and tuck their chin to the chest during insertion.
Insert the catheter using sterile technique according to manufacturer's instructions and institutional protocol.
Connect the extension tubing and aspirate to check for blood return.
Flush the catheter with normal saline and heparin, as appropriate and according to institutional protocol.
Secure the catheter and apply a sterile transparent dressing according to institutional protocol.
Record the date and time of dressing placement.
Verify the catheter tip by radiograph, as appropriate and according to institutional protocol.
Avoid using the affected arm for blood pressure measurement and phlebotomy.
Monitor for immediate complications such as bleeding, nerve or tendon injury, cardiac decompression, respiratory distress, or catheter embolism.
Monitor for signs of phlebitis (e.g., pain, erythema, warm skin, edema).
Use sterile technique for dressing change at the insertion site according to institutional protocol.
Instruct patient/family on dressing change technique.
Flush the line after each use with an appropriate solution according to institutional protocol.

definicao: Cleaning, monitoring, and promoting healing of a wound that is closed with sutures, clips, or staples.
atividades: Explain the procedure to the patient, using sensory preparation.
Inspect the incision site for redness, edema, or signs of dehiscence or evisceration.
Observe the characteristics of any drainage.
Monitor the healing process at the incision site.
Clean the area around the incision with an appropriate cleansing solution.
Wipe from the clean area to the less clean area.
Monitor the incision for signs and symptoms of infection.
Use sterile swabs for efficient cleaning of tight sutures with appropriately tightened threads, deep, narrow wounds, or wounds with pockets.
Clean the area around the site of any tube or drain.
Maintain the position of any drainage tube.
Apply closure strips, as appropriate.
Apply antiseptic ointment as ordered.
Remove sutures, staples, or clips, as indicated.
Change dressing at appropriate intervals.
Apply an appropriate dressing to protect the incision.
Facilitate visualization of the incision by the patient.
Instruct the patient on how to care for the incision during bathing or showering.
Teach the patient to minimize stress on the incision site.
Teach the patient and/or family how to care for the incision site, including signs and symptoms of infection.

definicao: Promoting comfort, safety, and prevention of complications for a patient unable to leave the bed.
atividades: Explain the reasons for the bed rest requirement.
Place an appropriate therapeutic mattress/bed.
Position proper body alignment.
Avoid using rough-textured linens.
Keep bed linens clean, dry, and wrinkle-free.
Place a footboard in the bed.
Use devices in the bed that protect the patient.
Apply devices to prevent foot drop.
Raise side rails as appropriate.
Place the bed positioning control device within easy reach of the patient.
Place the call bell within the patient's reach.
Place the bedside table within the patient's reach.
Attach a trapeze to the bed, as appropriate.
Reposition according to skin condition.
Reposition the immobilized patient at least every 2 hours according to a specific schedule.
Monitor skin condition.
Teach bed exercises, as appropriate.
Facilitate small movements for weight relief.
Perform passive and active range-of-motion exercises.
Assist with hygiene measures (e.g., use of deodorant or perfume).
Assist with activities of daily living.
Apply anti-embolism stockings.

Monitor for complications of bed rest (e.g., loss of muscle tone, back pain, constipation, increased stress, depression, confusion, sleep cycle changes, urinary tract infections, difficulty with urination, pneumonia).
Position in an upright posture intermittently for patients unable to get out of bed daily to protect against orthostatic intolerance.

definicao: Cleansing and inspection of the feet for relaxation, cleanliness, and skin health.
atividades: Examine the skin for irritation, cracks, lesions, areas of thick skin, calluses, deformities, or edema on the skin.
Examine the patient's shoes for proper fit.
Perform foot soaks as needed.
Dry carefully between the toes.
Apply creams.
Clean nails.
Apply moisture-absorbing powders as indicated.
Discuss with the patient their usual foot care routine.
Instruct patient/family on the importance of foot care.
Offer positive feedback on self-foot care activities.
Monitor the patient's gait and weight distribution on the feet.
Monitor the cleanliness and general condition of shoes and socks.
Instruct the patient to inspect for rough areas inside shoes.
Monitor the hydration level of the feet.
Monitor for arterial insufficiency in the legs.

definicao: Maintenance of elimination through a stoma and care of surrounding tissue.
atividades: Instruct patient/significant others on the use of equipment/ostomy care.
Ask patient/significant others to demonstrate the use of equipment.
Assist the patient in obtaining necessary equipment.
Properly fit the ostomy appliance as needed.
Monitor the incision/stoma healing.
Monitor for postoperative complications such as bowel obstruction, paralytic ileus, anastomotic leaks, or mucocutaneous separation, as appropriate.
Monitor stoma/surrounding tissue healing and adaptation to ostomy equipment.
Change/empty the ostomy bag appropriately.
Irrigate the ostomy appropriately.
Assist the patient with self-care.
Encourage patient/significant others to express feelings and concerns about changes in body image.
Explore the patient's ostomy care practices.
Explain to the patient how ostomy care will change their daily routine.
Assist the patient in planning time for the care routine.
Instruct the patient on how to monitor complications (e.g., mechanical failure, chemical failure, skin rash, leaks, dehydration, infection).
Instruct the patient on mechanisms to reduce odor.
Monitor elimination patterns.
Assist the patient in identifying factors affecting elimination patterns.
Instruct patient/significant others on appropriate diet and expected changes in elimination function.
Provide support and assistance as the patient develops skills in caring for the stoma and surrounding tissue.
Teach the patient to chew well, avoid foods that have caused digestive problems in the past, add new foods one at a time, and drink plenty of fluids.
Instruct on Kegel exercises if the patient has an ileoanal reservoir.
Instruct the patient to catheterize and drain the Indiana pouch whenever it feels full (every 4 to 6 hours).
Discuss concerns about sexual function, as appropriate.
Encourage visitation by support group members in the same situation.
Express confidence that the patient can return to a normal life with the ostomy.
Encourage participation in ostomy support groups after discharge.

definicao: Management of the patient who has traction and/or a stabilization device to immobilize and stabilize a body part.
atividades: Position in proper body alignment.
Maintain proper body alignment in bed to maximize traction.
Ensure that appropriate weights are applied (e.g., traction and countertraction measures).
Ensure that ropes and pulleys are functioning properly and are hanging freely.
Ensure that traction ropes and weights remain along the axis of the fractured bone.
Secure traction weights while moving the patient.
Maintain traction at all times.
Monitor the traction system at least once per shift.
Monitor self-care ability while on traction.
Monitor external fixation device.
Monitor pin insertion sites.
Remove traction devices from the skin at least daily for skin inspection and cleansing.
Monitor skin and bony prominences for signs of skin injury.
Monitor circulation, movement, and sensation of the affected extremity.
Monitor for complications of immobility (e.g., deep vein thrombosis, respiratory infection, muscle atrophy, foot drop).
Provide appropriate pressure relief measures.
Perform pin site care at least daily.
Perform appropriate skin care at friction points.
Provide a trapeze for bed mobility, as appropriate.
Instruct on orthosis care, as needed.
Instruct on external fixation device care, as needed.
Instruct on pin site care, as needed.
Instruct on the importance of adequate nutrition for bone healing.
Monitor for body image disturbances and need for counseling.

definicao: Prevention of wound complications and promotion of
Prevention of wound complications and promotion of healing of the graft site.
atividades: Ensure a complete history and physical examination have been obtained before skin graft surgery.
Apply cotton or gauze dressings to maintain adequate tension on the graft site according to institutional protocol.
Promote adequate pain control (e.g., medication, music therapy, distraction, massage).

Elevate the graft site until circulation develops in the graft on the host (about 1 week), then allow the graft site to remain in a dependent position for progressively longer periods according to institutional protocol.
Use needle aspiration to eliminate fluid beneath the graft to maintain close contact between the recipient bed and the graft during the postoperative revascularization period.
Avoid 'rolling' fluid blisters to the graft edge during the postoperative revascularization period.
Avoid friction and shearing forces on the new graft site.
Limit patient activity to bed rest until graft adherence.
Instruct the patient to keep the affected part as immobile as possible during healing.
Inspect the dressing daily according to institutional protocol.
Monitor graft color, warmth, capillary refill, and turgor if without dressing.
Monitor for signs of infection (e.g., fever, pain) and other postoperative complications.
Incorporate aggressive efforts to prevent the development of pneumonia, pulmonary emboli, and pressure injuries during the period of immobility.
Provide emotional support, understanding, and consideration to the patient and family in cases where the graft fails.
Support the patient in appropriately channeling anger, hostility, and frustration resulting from a failed graft.
Instruct the patient on methods of protecting the area against mechanical and thermal trauma (e.g., sun exposure, use of hot compresses).
Instruct the patient to use compression stockings, padding, or belts to protect the graft site.
Instruct patients to regularly apply artificial lubrication to the graft site as needed.
Explain to the patient that protection of the graft site may continue to be necessary years after graft transplantation.
Explain to the patient that smoking decreases blood supply to the graft recipient bed and increases the likelihood of graft failure and should therefore be avoided.

definicao: Application of topical substances or manipulation of devices to promote integrity and minimize skin breakdown.
atividades: Avoid using rough-textured linens.
Cleanse with antibacterial soap, as appropriate.
Dress the patient in loose-fitting clothing.
Cover the skin with medicated powder, as appropriate.
Remove adhesive tape and residue.
Provide support to edematous areas (e.g., pillow under arms and scrotal support), as appropriate.
Apply lubricant to moisturize lips and oral mucosa, as needed.
Administer back/neck massage, as appropriate.
Change the external urinary device (condom catheter), as appropriate.
Place diapers loosely, as appropriate.
Place incontinence pads, as appropriate.
Massage around the affected area.
Apply a properly fitted ostomy device, as needed.
Cover hands with protective gloves, as appropriate.
Promote perineal hygiene, as needed.
Avoid local heat applications.
Avoid using alkaline soap on the skin.
Immerse in a colloidal bath, as appropriate.
Keep bed linens clean, dry, and wrinkle-free.
Turn the immobilized patient at least every 2 hours according to a specific schedule.
Use devices in the bed that protect the patient (e.g., sheepskin).
Place heel protectors, as appropriate.
Apply powder to deep skin folds.
Initiate wound ostomy continence nurse consultation services, as needed.
Apply a clean occlusive dressing (e.g., Tegaderm® or Duoderm®), as needed.
Apply topical antibiotic to the affected area, as appropriate.
Apply topical anti-inflammatory agent to the affected area, as appropriate.

definicao: Provision of assessment and life-saving measures in urgent situations.
atividades: Activate the emergency medical system.
Request the automated external defibrillator (AED) or ensure someone obtains it, if possible and appropriate.
Initiate rescue procedures for the most critically ill patients in the case of multiple victims.
Assess unresponsive patients to determine appropriate action.
Assess signs and symptoms of cardiac arrest.
Call for help if the patient is not breathing or breathing is abnormal and unresponsive.
Direct others to call for help, if needed.
Employ precautionary measures to reduce the risk of infection while providing care.
Apply the AED and implement specified actions, as appropriate.
Ensure immediate defibrillation, as appropriate.
Perform cardiopulmonary resuscitation focusing on chest compressions in adults and compressions with ventilations in children, as appropriate.

Initiate 30 chest compressions at the indicated rate and depth, allowing full chest recoil between compressions, minimizing interruptions between compressions, and avoiding excessive ventilation, as appropriate.
Minimize the interval between the pause in compressions and shock, if indicated.
Adapt rescue procedures to the most likely cause of the arrest (e.g., cardiac or respiratory).
Create or maintain a clear airway.
Assess signs and symptoms of severely compromised breathing (e.g., pneumothorax or flail chest).
Perform two rescue breaths after completing the initial 30 compressions, as appropriate.
Perform the Heimlich maneuver, as appropriate.
Provide age-appropriate treatment for the elderly and children.
Assess signs and symptoms of severely compromised hemodynamic status (e.g., trauma or arterial rupture).
Institute measures (e.g., apply pressure, pressure dressing, positioning) to reduce or minimize hemorrhage.
Institute measures for shock control (e.g., positioning for optimal perfusion, use of pneumatic antishock garment), as needed.
Monitor the amount and nature of blood loss.
Monitor vital signs, if possible and appropriate.
Assess signs and symptoms of compromised neurological conditions (e.g., paralysis, paresthesias, fecal or urinary incontinence).

Immobilize the patient with suspected cranial or spinal trauma using appropriate devices and techniques (e.g., apply cervical collar, logroll the patient, and transport the patient supine on a backboard).

Position the patient's body part or the body as a block in the appropriate position (e.g., body part affected by an insect bite lower than heart level and left lateral decubitus for ingestion of poison or alcohol and substance intoxication).
Immobilize fractures, large wounds, and any part that has been injured.
Move the patient using only proper technique and body mechanics when needed.
Monitor signs and symptoms of hypoglycemia (e.g., tremors, tachycardia, chills, clammy skin, drowsiness, dizziness, blurred vision, confusion).
Monitor level of consciousness.
Remove the patient from a cold environment.
Remove wet clothing from the patient.
Remove the patient with fever from direct sunlight and heat source.
Cool the patient with a fan and offer cold drinks PO, if needed.
Check for medical alert tags.
Administer medication (e.g., nitroglycerin, bronchodilator, activated charcoal, insulin, epinephrine, and antidote) as needed.
Determine the history of the accident through reports from the patient and others in the area.
Determine what type of vehicle was involved in the accident and the use of restraints, if appropriate.
Determine the exact nature of the trauma sustained, if appropriate.
Determine if there was intoxication by illicit substances or other substances.
Determine if toxic or poisonous substances were used.
Send substances suspected of being used along with the patient to the care facility, as appropriate.
Contact the poison control center, following treatment as indicated.
Do not leave a suicidal patient alone.
Reassure and provide emotional support to the patient or family.
Assist with ongoing treatment by providing information about the life-threatening situation to other healthcare professionals.
Coordinate medical transport, as appropriate.
Transport using a backboard, as appropriate.

definicao: Monitoring and management of stages one and two of the birthing process.
atividades: Determine if the patient is in labor.
Determine if membranes have ruptured.
Admit to the labor and delivery area.
Determine the patient's preparation and goals for the birth of the child.

intervencao: Labor and Delivery Care: High-Risk Delivery
definicao: Assistance with vaginal delivery of multiple or malpositioned fetuses.
atividades: Inform the patient and support persons about the procedures and team to prepare them during the birth process.
Communicate changes in maternal or fetal status to the attending physician appropriately.
Prepare appropriate equipment, including electronic fetal monitor, ultrasound, anesthesia equipment, neonatal resuscitation equipment (Piper), and extra blankets for neonates.
Notify additional assistants to assist with delivery (e.g., neonatologist, NICU nurses, anesthesiologist).
Assist in donning surgical gowns and gloves for the obstetric team.
Continue electronic monitoring.
Coach during pushing efforts in the second stage.
Alert the attending physician to abnormalities in maternal vital signs or fetal heart rate tracing(s).
Encourage the support person to assist with comfort measures.
Use universal precautions.
Perform perineal scrub.
Perform or assist with manual rotation of the fetal head from occiput to anterior position, as appropriate.
Record the time of delivery of the first twin or of the fetal presentation to the level of the umbilicus.
Assist with amniotomy of additional amniotic membranes as needed.
Continue to monitor the heart rate of the second or third fetus.
Perform fetal ultrasound to locate fetal position, as appropriate.
Guide the fetal head with the hand to promote flexion during breech delivery, as determined by the physician.
Perform McRoberts' maneuver, suprapubic pressure as requested by the physician or obstetric nurse.
Support the body as the physician performs the delivery after the head emerges.

definicao: Provision of short-term care to provide relief to the family caregiver.
atividades: Establish a therapeutic relationship with the patient and family.
Monitor caregiver endurance.
Inform the patient and family about available state funds for home caregivers.
Arrange for substitute home caregivers.
Coordinate volunteers for home services, as appropriate.
Coordinate community support services (i.e., meals, day care, summer camps).
Arrange for a substitute caregiver.
Monitor the skill level of the substitute caregiver.
Follow the usual care routine.
Provide care, such as exercises, ambulation, and hygiene, as appropriate.
Provide a possible activity program, as appropriate.
Obtain emergency telephone numbers.
Determine how to contact the usual caregiver.
Provide emergency care as needed.
Maintain the normal home environment.
Provide a report to the usual caregiver upon their return.

definicao: Facilitating the patient's entry into a healthcare institution.
atividades: Introduce yourself and inform of your role in providing care.
Orient patient/family/significant other(s) to care expectations.
Provide appropriate privacy for the patient/family/significant other(s).
Orient the patient/family/significant other(s) to the environment.
Orient the patient/family/significant other(s) to the institution's facilities.
Obtain admission history, including past illnesses, medications, and allergies.
Perform the admission physical examination, as appropriate.
Conduct admission financial data collection, as appropriate.
Conduct admission psychosocial data collection, as appropriate.
Conduct admission religious data collection, as appropriate.
Conduct admission risk screening (e.g., fall risk, tuberculosis test, skin assessment).
Provide the patient with the 'Patient's Bill of Rights.'
Obtain information about advanced care directives (e.g., living will and healthcare proxy).
Document pertinent information.
Maintain patient data confidentiality.
Identify patients at risk for readmission.
Develop the patient's care plan, nursing diagnoses, outcomes, and interventions.
Initiate discharge planning.
Implement safety precautions, as appropriate.
Identify the patient's chart, room door, and/or bedside, as indicated.
Notify the physician of admission and patient status.
Obtain medical orders for patient care.

definicao: Promotion of physical and psychological recovery before and after amputation of a body part.
atividades: Encourage the patient to participate in the decision to amputate, when possible.
Review informed consent with the patient.
Provide information and support before and after surgery.
Facilitate the use of a pressure-relief mattress.
Position the stump in proper body alignment.
Position the below-knee stump in an extended position.
Avoid placing the stump in a dependent position to reduce edema and vascular stasis.
Avoid removing the stump dressing immediately after surgery, as long as there is no drainage or sign of infection.
Wrap the stump, as needed.
Facilitate the shaping of a soft, cone-shaped stump through wrapping for proper prosthetic fit.
Monitor the amount of edema present in the stump.
Monitor phantom limb pain (i.e., check for burning, cramping, throbbing, crushing sensation, or tingling where the limb was).
Explain that phantom limb pain may begin several weeks after surgery and be triggered by pressure in other areas.
Administer pharmacological and non-pharmacological analgesia (e.g.,

definicao: Management of a patient with occlusion of peripheral circulation.
atividades: Obtain a detailed patient health history to plan current and future preventive care.

Assess changes in cardiac and pulmonary status (e.g., onset of wheezing, hemoptysis, dyspnea, tachypnea, tachycardia, syncope), as patients with deep vein thrombosis (DVT) are at increased risk of recurrence and peripheral embolism (PE).
Assess any complaints of chest, shoulder, back, or pleuritic pain (i.e., assess intensity, location, radiation, duration, and precipitating and relieving factors).
Perform a comprehensive assessment of peripheral circulation (i.e., check peripheral pulses, edema, capillary refill, color, and temperature of extremities).
Monitor pain in the affected area.

Monitor signs of decreased venous circulation in the affected extremity (e.g., increased extremity circumference; painful edema and tenderness; increased pain in dependent position, pain persisting with use of the extremity; hard, palpable vein; increased superficial veins; severe cramps; rubor and heat; numbness and tingling; skin color change; fever).
Administer anticoagulant medication.
Elevate any extremity suspected of being affected 20 degrees or more above heart level to improve venous return.
Apply the Wells prediction score to assist in DVT diagnosis.

Instruct the patient and family on diagnostic procedures (e.g., plethysmography; computed tomography plethysmography; venography; D-dimer assay; multidetector computed tomography; magnetic resonance imaging, ultrasounds), as appropriate.
Apply graduated elastic compression stockings or sleeves to reduce the risk of post-thrombotic syndrome or DVT recurrence.
Remove graduated elastic compression stockings or sleeves for 15 to 20 minutes every 8 hours or per institutional policy and protocol.
Avoid antecubital intravenous access and instruct radiology and laboratory staff to limit access to antecubital veins for tests, if possible.
Administer promethazine intravenously only in 25 to 50 mL of normal saline at a slow infusion rate and avoid diluting in less than 10 mL.
Assist the patient with passive or active range-of-motion exercises, as appropriate.
Maintain the patient on bed rest and change position every 2 hours.
Provide early ambulation and exercises under the direction and supervision of a physical therapist.
Monitor neurological status.
Provide pain relief and comfort measures.
Elevate the sheets using a bed cradle over the affected extremity, as appropriate.
Avoid massaging or compressing the muscles of the affected extremity.
Instruct the patient not to massage or compress the affected area.
Monitor the patient's prothrombin time (PT) and partial thromboplastin time (PTT) to maintain one to two times normal, as appropriate.
Monitor side effects of anticoagulants.
Keep protamine sulfate and vitamin K available for emergencies.
Administer antacids and analgesics, as appropriate.
Instruct the patient not to cross their legs and avoid sitting for long periods with legs dangling.
Instruct the patient to avoid activities that result in Valsalva maneuver (e.g., straining during defecation).
Administer medications that prevent Valsalva maneuver episodes (e.g., stool softeners and antiemetics), as appropriate.
Instruct the patient and family on appropriate precautions (e.g.,

definicao: Management of a patient with occlusion of pulmonary circulation.
atividades: Prepare for thrombolytic therapy (e.g., streptokinase, urokinase, activase), as indicated.
Obtain a detailed patient health history to plan current and future preventive care.

Assess changes in cardiac and pulmonary status (e.g., onset of wheezing, hemoptysis, dyspnea, tachypnea, tachycardia, syncope), as patients with peripheral embolism (PE) or deep vein thrombosis (DVT) have a higher risk of recurrence.
Assess any complaints of chest, shoulder, back, or pleuritic pain (i.e., assess intensity, location, radiation, duration, and precipitating and relieving factors).

Assist with diagnostic tests and evaluations to rule out conditions with similar signs and symptoms (e.g., acute myocardial infarction; pericarditis; aortic dissection; pneumonia; pneumothorax; anxiety accompanied by hyperventilation; asthma; heart failure; pericardial tamponade; and gastrointestinal abnormalities such as peptic ulcer, esophageal rupture, gastritis).
Instruct the patient and/or family regarding diagnostic procedures (e.g., ventilation-perfusion (V/Q) scan, D-dimer assay, spiral multidetector CT, ultrasounds), as appropriate.
Auscultate lung sounds for crackles or other adventitious sounds.
Check arterial blood gas levels as indicated.
Monitor determinants of tissue oxygen delivery (e.g., PaO2, SaO2, hemoglobin levels, and cardiac output).
Monitor symptoms of inadequate tissue oxygenation (e.g., pallor, cyanosis, and slow capillary refill).
Monitor symptoms of respiratory failure (e.g., low PaO2 and elevated PaCO2 levels and respiratory muscle fatigue).
Initiate the appropriate thromboprophylaxis regimen immediately according to institutional policy and protocol.
Administer low-dose prophylactic anticoagulant and/or antiplatelet (e.g., heparin, clopidogrel, warfarin, acetylsalicylic acid, dipyridamole, dextran) according to institutional policy and protocol.
Elevate any limb suspected of being affected 20 degrees or more above heart level to increase venous return.
Apply graduated elastic compression stockings or sleeves (GECS) to reduce the risk of DVT or DVT recurrence according to institutional policy and protocol.
Maintain graduated elastic compression stockings or sleeves to prevent the development of post-thrombotic syndrome (PTS).
Apply an intermittent pneumatic compression device according to institutional policy and protocol.
Remove the graduated elastic compression stockings or sleeves and intermittent pneumatic compression device for 15 to 20 minutes every 8 hours according to institutional policy and protocol.
Avoid antecubital intravenous access and instruct radiology and laboratory staff to limit access to antecubital veins for tests, if possible.
Assist the patient with passive or active range-of-motion exercises, as appropriate.
Encourage the patient to flex and extend the foot and legs at least 10 times every hour.
Change the patient's position every 2 hours, encourage early mobilization or ambulation as tolerated.
Encourage good ventilation (e.g., incentive spirometry, cough and deep breathe every 2 hours).
Monitor changes in laboratory test results for changes in oxygenation or acid-base balance, as appropriate.
Instruct the patient and/or family about any planned treatment to remove the embolus (e.g., fibrinolysis, catheter embolectomy, or surgical pulmonary embolectomy).
Encourage the patient to relax.
Monitor side effects of anticoagulants.
Avoid excessive catheter inflation in the pulmonary artery to prevent pulmonary artery rupture, as appropriate.
Monitor the pulmonary artery catheter tracing for spontaneous catheter wedging, if appropriate.
Reposition the spontaneously wedged pulmonary artery catheter, as appropriate.
Maintain thromboprophylaxis after an embolism.
Instruct the patient and family on the need for anticoagulation for at least 3 months after an embolism.
Provide detailed guidance to the patient and family regarding prevention of future emboli and thrombosis.

definicao: Identification and management of a high-risk pregnancy to promote healthy outcomes for mother and baby.
atividades: Determine the presence of medical factors related to poor pregnancy outcomes (e.g., diabetes, thyroid disease, hypertension, obesity, thrombophilias or history of deep vein thrombosis, autoimmune conditions, herpes, hepatitis, HIV, cardiac conditions or history of cardiac surgery, chronic pain conditions controlled with opioid analgesics, use of opioid replacement therapies, seizure disorders).

Review obstetric history for risk factors related to pregnancy (e.g., recurrent loss, prematurity, post-maturity, pre-eclampsia, multifetal pregnancy, intrauterine growth restriction, placental abruption, placenta previa, hyperemesis, Rh sensitization, premature rupture of membranes, family history of genetic disorders).

Recognize demographic and social factors related to poor pregnancy outcomes (e.g., low or advanced maternal age, race, ethnicity, low socioeconomic status, late or absent prenatal care, physical abuse, substance abuse).
Determine the patient's understanding of the identified risk factors.
Encourage expression of feelings and fears about lifestyle changes of pregnancy and parenting, fetal well-being, financial changes, family functioning, and personal safety.
Provide educational material addressing risk factors and routine examinations and common procedures.

Instruct the patient on self-care techniques to increase the chance of a healthy outcome (e.g., hydration, diet, activity changes, importance of regular prenatal assessments, blood sugar normalization, sexual precautions).
Instruct on alternative methods of sexual satisfaction and intimacy.

Refer, as appropriate, to specific programs (e.g., smoking cessation, substance abuse treatment, diabetes education, preterm birth prevention and education; shelter for victims of abuse, pain management by caregivers, genetic counseling, sexually transmitted infection clinic).
Instruct the patient on the use of prescribed medication (e.g., insulin, tocolytic, antihypertensives, antibiotics, anticoagulants, anticonvulsants).
Instruct the patient on self-monitoring skills (e.g., vital signs, blood glucose testing, uterine activity monitoring), as appropriate.

Provide written instructions to address signs and symptoms requiring immediate medical attention (e.g., bright red vaginal bleeding, amniotic fluid leakage, unusual vaginal discharge, decreased fetal movements, four or more contractions per hour before 37 weeks gestation, headache, visual disturbances, epigastric pain, rapid weight gain with facial edema).
Discuss the fetal risks associated with preterm birth at various gestational ages.
Visit the neonatal intensive care unit if preterm birth is anticipated (e.g., multiple gestation).
Conduct tests to assess fetal status and placental function (e.g., non-stress test, biophysical profiles, ultrasound examination).
Obtain cervical specimens, as appropriate.
Assist with fetal diagnostic procedures (e.g., blood sampling, amniocentesis, chorionic villus sampling, percutaneous umbilical blood sampling, Doppler blood flow studies).
Assist with fetal therapy procedures (e.g., fetal transfusions, fetal surgery, selective reduction, termination procedure).
Interpret medical explanations for test and procedure results.
Administer Rh0(D) immune globulin (e.g., RhoGAM® or Gamulin® Rh) to prevent Rh sensitization after invasive procedures, as appropriate.
Establish a plan for clinical follow-up.
Provide anticipatory guidance for probable treatments during the labor process.
Encourage early enrollment in prenatal classes or provide childbirth education materials for patients on bed rest.

Provide anticipatory guidance for common experiences that high-risk mothers have during the postpartum period (e.g., exhaustion, depression, chronic stress, disillusionment with baby care, loss of income, discord with partner, sexual dysfunction).
Refer to a high-risk mothers' support group, if needed.
Refer to home care agencies (e.g., specialized perinatal nursing services, perinatal case management, public health nursing).
Closely monitor the physical and psychosocial status throughout the pregnancy.
Immediately report deviations from normal for the mother and fetus to the physician or obstetric nurse.
Document patient education, laboratory results, fetal test results, and patient responses.

definicao: Promotion of bowel continence and maintenance of perianal skin integrity.
atividades: Determine the physical or psychological cause of fecal incontinence.
Determine the onset and type of incontinence, frequency of episodes, and any changes related to bowel function or stool consistency.
Explain the etiology of the problem and the rationale for actions.
Eliminate the cause of incontinence (e.g., medication, infection, fecal impaction), if possible.
Determine the goals of the bowel control program with the patient/family.
Discuss the procedures and expected outcomes with the patient.
Instruct the patient or family to record fecal elimination, as appropriate.
Wash the perianal area with soap and water and dry completely after each bowel movement.
Use a non-ionic detergent preparation (e.g., Peri-Wash®) for cleansing, as appropriate.
Protect the skin from excess moisture from urine, feces, or perspiration using a moisture barrier cream (e.g., petrolatum, lanolin, dimethicone), as needed.
Use powder and creams on the perianal area with caution.
Monitor the perianal skin for the development of pressure injury and infection.
Keep the bed and clothing clean.
Implement the bowel training program, as appropriate.
Schedule perineal hygiene with a commode or bedpan at

definicao: Promotion of bowel continence in children.
atividades: Gather information on toilet training history, duration of encopresis, and attempts made to eliminate the problem.
Determine the cause of fecal leakage (e.g., constipation and fecal impaction), as appropriate.
Order tests to detect physical causes (e.g., endoscopy, radiographic procedures, and stool analysis).
Prepare the child and family for diagnostic tests.
Perform rectal examination, as appropriate.
Instruct the family on the physiology of normal stool elimination and sphincter control.
Develop a bowel retraining plan with the family (e.g., mineral oil, high-fiber diet, regular toilet routine).
Encourage the child to sit on the toilet 10 to 15 minutes after meals for 10-minute intervals.
Place a footstool under the feet to relax the abdomen.
Provide positive reinforcement (e.g., stickers, praise, special activities) to encourage the child to participate in bowel training.
Conduct a psychosocial assessment of the family, including caregiver responses and the child's self-esteem.
Discuss the psychosocial dynamics of encopresis with parents (e.g., family patterns, separation from family, self-esteem issues, and self-limiting characteristics).
Use play therapy to help the child process feelings.
Investigate family communication patterns, strengths, and coping skills.
Encourage parents to promote security by eliminating anxiety associated with perineal hygiene.
Encourage parents to demonstrate love and acceptance at home to neutralize peer ridicule.
Refer for family therapy, as appropriate.

definicao: Assistance in promoting continence and maintaining perineal skin integrity.
atividades: Identify multifactorial causes of incontinence (e.g., urinary output, voiding pattern, cognitive function, pre-existing urinary problems, post-void residual, and medications).

Use the acronym DIAPPERS (delirium, infection, atrophic urethritis or vaginitis, pharmacology, psychosocial disorders, endocrine disorders, mobility restriction, stool impaction) to identify possible causes of transient incontinence.
Provide privacy for elimination.
Explain the etiology of the problem and the rationale for your actions.
Include family members in management strategies.
Monitor urinary elimination, including frequency, consistency, odor, volume, and color.
Collect urine for culture and sensitivity, if needed.
Discuss the procedures and expected outcomes with the patient.
Assist in developing/maintaining a sense of hope.
Modify clothing and environment for easy access to the toilet.
Assist in selecting appropriate incontinence clothing/protection for short-term management while more definitive treatment is planned.
Provide protective clothing when needed.
Cleanse the skin of the genital area at regular intervals.
Provide positive feedback for any decrease in incontinence episodes.
Limit fluids 2 to 3 hours before bedtime, as appropriate.
Schedule diuretic administration to have the least impact on lifestyle.
Instruct patient/family to record urine output and pattern, as appropriate.
Implement scheduled voiding programs (offer to help use the toilet every 2 hours while awake) or prompted voiding (encourage the patient to ask for help when feeling urgency).
Instruct the patient to drink a minimum of 1,500 mL of fluids per day.
Instruct on ways to avoid constipation or fecal impaction.
Inform that up to 5 to 10% weight loss can help symptoms.
Instruct on how to perform Kegel exercises to strengthen pelvic floor muscles.
Limit intake of bladder irritants (cola drinks, coffee, tea, chocolate, artificial sweeteners, spicy foods, alcohol).
Monitor the effectiveness of surgical, medical, pharmacological, and self-prescribed treatments.
Monitor bowel habits.
Determine the need for placement and duration of an indwelling catheter, as complications increase the longer the catheter is in place.
Refer to a urinary continence specialist, as appropriate.

definicao: Promotion of urinary continence in children who
Management of the physical and psychological needs of a woman undergoing a spontaneous or elective abortion.
atividades: Prepare the patient physically and psychologically for the abortion procedure.
Explain the sensations the patient may experience.
Instruct on signs to report (e.g., increased bleeding, increased cramping intensity, and passage of clots or tissue).
Administer analgesics or antiemetics, as appropriate.
Perform back massage, if needed.
Administer medications to terminate the pregnancy as per protocol.
Monitor the patient for bleeding and cramping.
Initiate intravenous infusion, as appropriate.
Observe signs of spontaneous abortion (e.g., cessation of cramping, increased pelvic pressure, loss of amniotic fluid).
Perform vaginal examination, as appropriate.
Assist with labor, as appropriate, depending on the gestational age of the fetus.
Assess blood loss, as appropriate.
Monitor vital signs.
Observe for signs of shock.
Save all expelled tissue.
Administer oxytocics after labor, as appropriate.
Instruct the patient on the procedures (e.g., suction curettage, dilation and curettage, and uterine evacuation).
Administer Rho(D) immune globulin for Rh-negative status.
Instruct the patient on post-abortion self-care and monitoring for adverse effects.
Provide anticipatory guidance on the grief reaction to fetal death.
Encourage a close person to support the patient before, during, or after the abortion if desired or with patient consent.
Provide information for referral to support groups (e.g., Resolve through Sharing, Compassionate Friends, SHARE Pregnancy and Infant Loss Support).
Refer for psychological support or counseling, as needed.
Consider cultural and religious beliefs and values.
Refer for religious support, as desired.
Inform about the 'anniversary phenomenon.'
Complete the birth registration and death report, as appropriate.
Obtain samples for genetic studies or autopsy, as appropriate.

definicao: Assistance in relieving bladder distension.
atividades: Determine the amount and characteristics of urinary output (e.g., voiding pattern, cognitive function, pre-existing urinary problems).
Monitor the use and effects of pharmacological treatments that could alter bladder emptying.
Provide privacy for elimination.
Use the power of suggestion by running water from the tap or flushing the toilet.
Stimulate the reflex bladder by applying cold to the abdomen, percussing the medial thigh, or running water.
Allow sufficient time for bladder emptying (10 minutes).

definicao: Providing care to a patient giving birth via abdominal incision into the uterus.
atividades: Orient the patient to the unit.
Review prenatal history.
Explain the reasons for surgery.
Discuss the patient's feelings, questions, and concerns about surgery.
Obtain or confirm informed consent.
Obtain necessary blood tests and document results.
Monitor vital signs.
Monitor fetal heart rate.
Prepare the abdomen for surgery.
Puncture intravenous access.
Insert an indwelling urinary catheter.
Administer medications.
Encourage the intimate partner or support person to be present during the birth.
Provide information about events occurring and sensations the patient may experience during surgery.
Provide information about the newborn.
Transfer the patient to the recovery room or delivery room.
Monitor physiological aspects of recovery (e.g., pain, uterine changes, airway patency, and lochia).
Inspect the condition of the surgical incision and dressing.
Assist with lower extremity exercises, position changes, coughing, and deep breathing.
Encourage the mother to breastfeed, if appropriate.
Provide appropriate breastfeeding guidance and support, if appropriate (demonstrate positioning adaptations for the baby according to mobility limitations).

Facilitate family bonding by minimizing separation between mother and newborn (e.g., position the baby face-to-face with the mother, provide unobstructed view of the baby, facilitate skin-to-skin contact, and transfer mother and baby together).

definicao: Provision of healthcare during the course of pregnancy.
atividades: Identify the individual's needs, concerns, and preferences, encouraging involvement in decision-making, and identifying and addressing barriers to treatment.
Discuss the importance of participating in prenatal care throughout pregnancy, encouraging the involvement of the patient's partner or other family members.
Encourage the patient to attend a prenatal care class.
Monitor weight gain.
Monitor for hypertensive disorders (e.g., blood pressure, edematous ankles, hands, and face, proteinuria).
Monitor fetal heart sounds.
Measure fundal height and compare with gestational age.
Monitor fetal movement.
Instruct the patient on the importance of monitoring fetal activity.
Monitor fetal presentation.
Review with the patient changes observed in fetal growth and status.
Instruct the patient on warning signs that require immediate communication.
Discuss nutritional needs and concerns (e.g., balanced diet, folic acid supplements, food safety, and other supplements).
Instruct the patient on the effects of exposure to or ingestion of hazardous substances (e.g., alcohol, illicit substances, teratogens, medications, herbs, and tobacco).
Discuss activity level with the patient (e.g., appropriate exercise and activities to avoid, importance of rest).
Provide genetic counseling and testing if indicated.
Instruct the patient on routine laboratory tests that will occur during pregnancy (e.g., urine tests, hemoglobin level, ultrasounds, gestational diabetes, HIV).
Instruct the patient on non-routine tests and treatments (e.g., stress tests, biophysical profile, Rh immune globulin, and membrane sampling), if needed.
Review test results with the patient.
Discuss dental care.
Discuss sexuality.
Monitor the patient's and partner's psychosocial status.
Monitor for risk factors affecting the patient's or fetus's health status (e.g., mental disorder and partner violence).
Offer support and guidance to a patient presenting with an unplanned or unwanted pregnancy.
Offer anticipatory guidance on psychological and physiological changes and discomforts (e.g., nausea, vomiting, musculoskeletal changes, fears, and breast tenderness).

definicao: Promotion of physical comfort and psychological peace in the final phase of life.
atividades: Identify the patient's care priorities.
Communicate willingness to discuss death.
Encourage the patient and family to share feelings about death.
Assist the patient and family in identifying a shared meaning about death.
Seek to understand the patient's actions, feelings, and attitudes.
Monitor the patient's anxiety.
Stay physically close to the frightened patient.
Monitor deterioration of physical and/or mental capacities.
Reduce demands on cognitive functioning when the patient is feeling unwell or fatigued.
Monitor mood changes.
Respect specific care requests of the patient and family.
Include the family in care decisions and activities as desired.
Support the patient and family in stages of grief.
Monitor pain.
Minimize discomfort when possible.
Medicate via alternative routes when there are swallowing problems.
Postpone feeding when the patient is fatigued.
Offer fluids and soft foods frequently.
Offer culturally appropriate foods.
Provide frequent rest periods.
Assist with basic care as needed.
Respect the need for privacy.
Modify the environment based on patient needs and desires.
Support the family's efforts to remain at the bedside.
Facilitate obtaining spiritual support for the patient and family.
Facilitate third-party care as appropriate.
Facilitate referral to a long-term care facility as desired.
Facilitate discussion of funeral arrangements.

definicao: Prevention or reduction of threats to ocular or visual integrity.
atividades: Monitor for hyperemia, exudate, or ulceration.
Instruct the patient not to touch the eye.
Monitor the corneal reflex.
Remove contact lenses, as appropriate.
Apply an eye shield, as appropriate.
Place eye patches as needed.
Alternate the patch between eyes for diplopia.
Apply lubricating eye drops, as appropriate.
Apply lubricating ointment, as appropriate.
Keep eyelids closed with tape, as appropriate.
Apply eye protectors with a moisture chamber, as appropriate.

definicao: Maintenance of perineal skin integrity and relief of perineal discomfort.
atividades: Assist with hygiene.
Keep the perineum dry.
Place a padded protector on chairs, as appropriate.
Inspect the condition of incision or tear(s) (e.g., episiotomy, laceration, circumcision).
Apply cold compress, as appropriate.
Apply warm compress or heat source, as appropriate.
Instruct the patient on the reason and use of sitz baths.
Provide and assist with sitz baths as needed.
Clean the perineum carefully at regular intervals.
Maintain the patient in a comfortable position.
Apply absorbent materials to absorb drainage, as appropriate.
Apply protective barrier (e.g., zinc oxide, petrolatum), as appropriate.
Apply prescribed medication (e.g., antibacterials, antifungals), as appropriate.
Document characteristics of drained secretions, as appropriate.
Provide scrotal support, as appropriate.
Provide pain medications, as appropriate.
Instruct the patient or significant others on perineal inspection for pathologies (e.g., infection, resolution of

definicao: Monitoring and management of the patient recently subjected to general or local anesthesia.
atividades: Review the patient's allergies, including latex allergy.
Determine the adequacy of airway and circulation.
Administer oxygen as appropriate.
Monitor oxygenation.
Ventilate as appropriate.
Monitor the quality and rate of respirations.
Encourage the patient to breathe deeply and cough.
Obtain a report from the operating room nurse and anesthesiologist.
Clarify postoperative orders to be initiated in the post-anesthesia care unit.
Monitor and record vital signs, including pain assessment, every 15 minutes or more frequently, as appropriate.
Monitor temperature.
Administer measures to warm the patient (warm blankets, convective blanket) as needed.
Monitor urine output.
Provide non-pharmacological and pharmacological pain relief measures as needed.
Administer antiemetic as prescribed.
Administer narcotic antagonists per institutional protocol.
Contact the anesthesiologist as appropriate.
Monitor the level of intrathecal analgesia.
Monitor return of sensorium and motor function.
Monitor neurological status.
Monitor level of consciousness.
Attend to patient safety needs.
Provide warm blankets as appropriate.
Interpret diagnostic tests as appropriate.
Check the patient's hospital record to determine baseline vital signs, as appropriate.
Compare current status with previous status to detect improvements and deterioration in the patient's condition.
Offer verbal or tactile stimulation as appropriate.
Administer IV medication to control shivering per institutional protocol.
Monitor the surgical site as appropriate.
Restrain the patient as appropriate.
Adjust the bed as appropriate.
Provide privacy as appropriate.
Provide emotional support to the patient and family as appropriate.
Determine the patient's conditions for discharge.
Provide the patient's chart to the postoperative care unit.
Transfer the patient to the next level of care.

definicao: Care for the deceased patient and their family.
atividades: Remove external objects from the body (e.g., clothing, IVs, monitors) as needed.
Clean the body.
Place an incontinence pad under the buttocks and between the legs.
Elevate the head slightly to prevent fluid pooling in the head or face.
Place dentures in the mouth, if possible.
Close the eyes.
Maintain proper body alignment.
Notify various departments and people according to institutional policy.
Label and place personal belongings in a safe place.
Notify a religious agent if requested by the family.
Avoid restricting the number of visitors.
Provide photographs.
Facilitate and support the family in viewing the body.
Respect the family's religious beliefs and rituals.
Provide privacy and support to family members.
Answer questions related to organ donation.
Answer questions related to autopsy.
Identify the body according to institutional policy and after the family has left.
Transfer the body to the morgue.
Notify the funeral director.
Notify the medical examiner as appropriate.

definicao: Providing care to the woman during the 6-week period beginning immediately after birth.
atividades: Monitor vital signs.
Monitor lochia for color, amount, odor, and presence of clots.
Have the patient void before postpartum check and void frequently thereafter.
Monitor the location of the uterine fundus, height, and tone, ensuring to support the lower uterine segment during palpation.
Gently massage the uterine fundus as needed.
Monitor the perineum or surgical incision and surrounding tissues (i.e., monitor for redness, edema, ecchymosis, drainage, and wound edge approximation).
Encourage early and frequent ambulation, assisting the patient when needed.
Encourage the postoperative patient to perform breathing exercises, assisting when needed.
Monitor patient pain.
Provide comfort to the patient experiencing chills (e.g., offer blankets, drinks).
Administer analgesics when needed.
Instruct the patient on non-pharmacological pain relief (e.g., sitz baths, ambulation, massage, distraction, ice packs, herbal baths, distraction).
Instruct the patient on perineal care to prevent infection and reduce discomfort.
Perform or assist with perineal care (e.g., apply an ice pack, encourage the patient to take sitz baths, apply non-moist local heat).
Monitor the breasts for temperature and color, as well as nipple condition.
Instruct the patient on changes that will occur in the breasts.
Monitor the bladder, including fluid intake and output (e.g., bladder emptying, palpability, color, odor).
Facilitate the return of normal urinary function (i.e., assist with local baths, promote hydration, moisten the perineum with warm water, encourage ambulation).
Monitor bowel function (e.g., date and time of last bowel movement, bowel sounds, presence of flatus).
Facilitate the return to normal bowel function (i.e., administer stool softeners or laxatives, instruct the patient to increase fluid and fiber intake, encourage ambulation).
Provide measures to decrease the likelihood of deep vein thrombosis (e.g., lower extremity exercises and application of compression boots).
Monitor the lower extremities for Homans' sign and provide other tests if needed.
Monitor the patient's emotional status.
Encourage the mother to discuss the labor and birth experience.
Offer encouragement to the patient about their ability for self-care and caring for the child.
Provide information on mood changes (e.g., sadness, postpartum depression, and psychosis).
Monitor for symptoms of postpartum depression or psychosis.
Provide anticipatory guidance on physiological and psychological changes and how to address them.
Discuss activity and rest needs.
Discuss sexuality and contraceptive options.
Monitor parent-infant interaction behaviors.
Facilitate optimal parent-infant interaction.
Instruct the patient on nutritional needs, including the importance of a balanced diet and use of supplements, if indicated.
Instruct the patient on the infant's nutritional needs.
Provide appropriate guidance and support regarding the chosen feeding method.
Refer the patient to a lactation consultant if indicated.
Instruct the patient on warning signs that need immediate reporting (e.g., fever, depression).
Administer Rh immune globulin and rubella vaccine, if indicated.
Assist parents in scheduling newborn exams and postpartum check-ups.
Refer to appropriate resources for community support or medical follow-up.

definicao: Use of body movements accompanied by a musical rhythm to facilitate specific physical, mental, emotional, or spiritual therapeutic change.
atividades: Assess the individual's interest in dance therapy.
Instruct the individual on the purpose of the dance experience.
Discuss with the individual the desired goals (e.g., relaxation, stimulation, concentration, pain reduction).
Assess the individual's capacity for movement.
Identify preference for music type and dance related to desired goals.
Determine session duration.
Determine the time intervals between each session.
Recommend wearing comfortable and appropriate clothing and footwear for dancing.
Limit distracting visual and auditory stimuli.
Choose a suitable location and necessary equipment (e.g., sound equipment, music selection, mirrors, chairs), as appropriate.
Plan sessions according to progress in movement execution.
Encourage expression of emotions.
Provide time for breaks and interruptions, as appropriate.
Instruct the individual to perform dance therapy at home, as appropriate.
Incorporate family participation in sessions, as appropriate.
Continuously monitor the achievement of objectives and goals.

definicao: Transfer of responsibility for the performance of patient care, while maintaining accountability for outcomes.
atividades: Determine the patient care that needs to be performed.
Identify the potential for harm.
Assess the complexity of the care to be delegated.
Determine the problem-solving and innovative skills required.
Consider the predictability of the outcome.
Estimate the effect on the nurse's time with the patient and subsequent development of a trusting relationship.
Assess the competence and training of the healthcare professional.
Familiarize yourself with the scope of practice of the healthcare professional who will assume the tasks, determined by state law and institutional guidelines, as appropriate.
Explain the task to the healthcare professional.
Ask questions to understand and clarify the other's understanding.

definicao: Provision of recorded and sworn testimony for legal purposes based on knowledge of the case.
atividades: Contact your employer and error author, as appropriate, when subpoenaed to testify or give a deposition.
Arrange for an attorney to represent you individually, as needed.
Discuss the case only with the attorney(s) representing you in the deposition.
Avoid discussing the case with colleagues, physicians, and others involved without your attorney present.
Ask your attorney to explain the deposition process.
Prepare by reviewing the clinical record, reading or re-reading any documents to be presented during the deposition.
Avoid taking any notes, documents, or reports to the deposition unless instructed by your attorney.
Prepare to admit any errors that occurred.
Listen carefully to the entire question and understand it before attempting to answer.
Listen to the questions and answer them directly and truthfully.
Ask for clarification if the question is unclear.
Avoid assumptions or trying to find pitfalls in the questions.
Answer a question only if the person asking has finished; do not interject while the question is being asked.
Answer questions based solely on personal and professional knowledge; do not guess or speculate.
Answer 'I don't remember' or 'I don't recall' if you do not remember a fact.
Answer only the questions asked and do not volunteer any unsolicited information.
Answer questions with 'yes' or 'no' if possible.
Provide an explanation only if your attorney asks you to.
Testify only about documents you have read.
Avoid answering a question that your attorney has objected to.
Correct the other party's attorney, particularly if facts are reported incorrectly.
Be respectful, courteous, and polite, and do not argue with the other attorney.
Speak calmly, clearly, and confidently, but without being pedantic or arrogant.
Spell out unusual words after enunciating them clearly, if requested.
Ask to speak to your attorney privately if needed.
Avoid offering documents; let your attorney take the lead.
Communicate with the other party's attorney or the other party only with your attorney present.
Speak loudly and clearly so that everything is heard.
If tired, ask to stand and take a break.
Avoid picking up any document marked as evidence.

definicao: Assisting community members in identifying community health concerns, mobilizing resources, and implementing solutions.
atividades: Identify health problems, strengths, and priorities with community partners.
Offer opportunities for participation from all segments of the community.
Assist community members in becoming aware of health problems and concerns.
Engage in dialogue to define community health problems and develop action plans.
Facilitate the implementation and review of community plans.
Assist community members with development and fundraising.
Enhance community support networks.
Identify and develop potential community leaders.
Maintain open communication with community members and institutions.
Strengthen contacts between individuals and groups to discuss common and competing interests.
Provide an organizational structure through which people can improve communication and negotiation skills.
Provide an environment by creating situations where individuals and groups feel safe expressing their views.
Develop strategies for conflict management.
Unify community members under a common mission.
Ensure that community members maintain control over decision-making.
Build commitment within the community by demonstrating how participation will influence individuals' lives and improve outcomes.
Develop mechanisms for member involvement in local, state, and national activities related to community health problems.

definicao: Development and use of a sequenced program of care activities to improve desired patient outcomes in a cost-effective manner.
atividades: Perform chart audits to determine current patterns of care for the patient population.

definicao: Development, maintenance, and monitoring of staff competence.
atividades: Identify staff learning needs (e.g., new or changing policies and procedures, new hires, transfers within the company, new job requirements, cross-training, equipment updates, emerging trends, skills training).
Identify learner characteristics (e.g., education, language, educational background, previous experience, age, motivation, attitude).
Identify performance problem(s) (e.g., knowledge deficit, skill deficit, motivational deficit), if needed.
Identify instructional goal(s) (e.g., inform staff of changes, provide knowledge and skills, improve proficiency, enhance competence).
Identify learning achievement standard(s) (e.g., psychomotor, interpersonal, critical thinking).
Determine instructional learning objectives.
Identify instructional content.
Identify constraints to instruction (e.g., time, cost, equipment availability).
Identify resources supporting instruction (e.g., specialist consultation, learning materials, time, financial resources).
Identify appropriate individual(s) to deliver the instruction.
Organize and develop instructional content.
Develop teaching and learning activities.
Create pre- and post-data collection/evaluation methods.
Deliver the instructional program (e.g., self-directed learning packages, classroom presentation, small group presentation, on-the-job training).
Evaluate the effectiveness of the instruction.
Provide feedback on instruction outcomes for staff development to appropriate individuals.
Monitor staff skill competence.
Conduct periodic competency reviews.
Determine the required frequency of staff development instruction to maintain competence.
Provide financial assistance and time off for participation in job-required educational programs.
Encourage participation in peer review.
Encourage reading of professional journals.
Encourage participation in professional organizations.

definicao: Planning, implementation, and evaluation of a coordinated set of activities aimed at increasing well-being or preventing, reducing, or eliminating one or more health problems of a group or community.
atividades: Assist the group or community in identifying significant health needs or problems.
Prioritize health needs among identified problems.
Form a task force, including appropriate community members, to examine the priority need or problem.
Educate planning group members about the planning process as appropriate.
Identify alternative approaches to meeting needs or solving problems.
Evaluate alternative approaches, detailing cost, resources, feasibility, and activities required.
Select the most appropriate approach.
Describe objectives and goals to meet needs or solve problems.
