Assess whether the leave goals were achieved upon return.
Verify that medications were taken as instructed upon return.

definicao: Facilitating a relaxed internal state of expanded awareness in the present moment, focusing attention on a sound, object, visual image, breath, or movement.
atividades: Discuss the patient's previous experience with meditation.
Discuss the patient's desire to learn meditation.
Explain that during meditation, the person remains calm, yet alert.
Provide quiet time free from interruptions.

definicao: Facilitating family presence in supporting individuals undergoing cardiopulmonary resuscitation and/or invasive procedures.
atividades: Introduce yourself to the team treating the patient and family.
Determine the suitability of the physical location for family presence.
Obtain team consensus for family presence and the period they may remain present.
Notify the treatment team of the family's emotional reaction to the patient's condition, as appropriate.
Obtain information regarding the patient's status, response to treatment, and identified needs.
Introduce yourself and other support team members to the family and patient.
Communicate information regarding the patient's current status in a timely manner.
Assure the family that the patient is receiving the best possible care.
Use the patient's name when speaking with the family.
Determine the emotional, physical, psychosocial, and spiritual support needs of the patient and family and initiate measures to meet those needs.
Determine the psychological burden of the prognosis for the family.
Promote realistic hope, as appropriate.
Be a mediator for the family, as appropriate.
Prepare the family to ensure they are informed about what to expect, what they will see, hear, and/or smell.
Inform the family about expectations and limits of behavior.
Provide a dedicated staff person to ensure family members are never left unattended at the bedside.
Accompany the family to the resuscitation or treatment area and announce their presence to the treatment team each time the family enters the treatment area.
Provide information and explanations about interventions, medical/nursing jargon, and expectations about the patient's response to treatment.
Accompany the family out of the room when requested by the team providing direct patient care.
Provide the family with the opportunity to ask questions and see, touch, and speak with the patient before transfers.
Assist the patient or family members in making phone calls, if needed.
Offer and provide comfort and support measures, including appropriate referrals, as needed.
Participate in assessing the emotional needs of the team and oneself.
Assist in identifying the need for critical incident stress planning, individual stress reduction, etc., as appropriate.
Participate in, initiate, and/or coordinate family grief follow-up at regular intervals, as appropriate.

definicao: Promoting beneficial visits from family and friends.
atividades: Determine the patient's preferences regarding visiting and information release.
Consider ethical and legal implications regarding the patient, family visiting, and the right to information.
Determine the need to limit visits in the case of excessive numbers of people, restless or tired patient, or due to physical status.
Determine the need for a greater number of family and friend visits.
Identify specific problems with visits, if any.
Establish flexible, patient-centered visiting policies, as appropriate.
Prepare the environment for the visit.
Discuss visiting policies with family/significant other(s).
Discuss the overnight stay policy for family/significant other(s).
Discuss the family's understanding of the patient's condition.
Negotiate with family/significant other(s) responsibilities and activities that assist the patient, such as feeding.
Establish ideal times for visits by family/significant other(s).
Explain the reason for limited visiting time.
Periodically evaluate with the family and patient whether visiting practices are meeting the patient/family needs and review accordingly.
Inform visitors, including children, about what to expect to see and hear before the first hospital visit, when appropriate.
Explain the procedure performed.
Encourage family members to use touch as well as verbal communication, as appropriate.
Place a chair next to the bed.
Be flexible with visiting, maintaining rest periods.

definicao: Fostering the growth of the patient's capacity to identify, connect with, and draw upon the source of meaning, purpose, comfort, strength, and hope in their lives.
atividades: Demonstrate caring presence and comfort by spending time with the patient, as well as with the patient's family and other significant person(s).
Encourage dialogue that helps the patient resolve their spiritual issues.
Model healthy reasoning and relationship skills.
Assist the patient in identifying barriers and attitudes that hinder growth or self-discovery.
Offer prayer support individually or in groups, as appropriate.
Encourage participation in devotional services, retreats, and special prayer/study programs.
Promote relationships with others for friendship and work.
Encourage the use of spiritual rituals and celebrations.
Encourage the patient's examination of their degree of spiritual commitment based on beliefs and values.
Provide an environment that fosters a meditative/contemplative attitude for self-reflection.
Assist the patient in exploring beliefs related to healing of body, mind, and spirit.
Refer to support groups, mutual self-help, or other spiritually based programs, as appropriate.
Refer to pastoral care or primary spiritual caregiver when concerns warrant.
Refer for additional body-mind-spirit connection guidance and support, as needed.

definicao: Assisting an individual's desire to replace feelings of anger and resentment towards another, oneself, or a higher power with charity, empathy, and humility.
atividades: Identify the patient's beliefs that may hinder/help in 'letting go' of a problem.
Acknowledge when anger and resentment are justified.
Identify the source of anger and resentment when possible.
Listen empathetically, without moral judgment or offering platitudes.
Explain that forgiveness is a process.
Assist the patient in exploring feelings of anger, bitterness, and resentment.
Use presence, touch, and empathy to facilitate the process, as appropriate.
Explore possibilities for making amends and reconciling with oneself, others, and/or a higher power.
Assist the patient in examining the health and healing dimensions of forgiveness.
Assist the patient in overcoming blocks to healing using spiritual practices (e.g., prayers, guidance, and discernment; healing, touch, healing visualization, and giving thanks), as appropriate.
Teach the art of emotional release and relaxation.
Assist the patient in seeking an arbiter (objective party) to facilitate the process of individual or group interest.
Encourage the use of traditional faith rituals, as appropriate (e.g., anointing, confession, reconciliation).
Communicate higher power/God or forgiveness to the inner self through prayer, scripture, other readings, as appropriate.
Communicate acceptance of the individual's level of progress.

definicao: Assisting another to cope with painful feelings of real or perceived responsibility.
atividades: Guide the patient/family in identifying painful feelings of guilt.
Assist the patient/family in identifying and analyzing the situations in which these feelings are experienced or generated.
Assist the patient/family in identifying their behaviors in the guilt situation.

definicao: Assisting in the resolution of a significant loss.
atividades: Identify the loss.
Assist the patient in identifying the nature of the attachment to the lost object or person.
Assist the patient in identifying the initial reaction to the loss.
Encourage expression of feelings about the loss.
Listen to expressions of grief.
Encourage discussion of previous loss experiences.
Encourage the patient to verbalize memories of the loss, both past and present.
Make empathic and sensitive statements about grief.
Encourage identification of the greatest fears related to the loss.
Instruct on the stages of the grieving process, as appropriate.
Support progression through personal stages of the grieving process.
Include other significant people in discussions and decisions, as appropriate.
Assist in identifying personal coping strategies.
Encourage the patient to implement cultural, religious, and social customs associated with the loss.
Communicate acceptance of discussing the loss.
Respond to children's questions associated with the loss.
Use clear words such as 'dead' or 'died' instead of euphemisms.
Encourage children to discuss their feelings.
Encourage expression of feelings in a manner comfortable for the child, such as writing, drawing, or playing.
Assist the child in clarifying misconceptions.
Identify community support groups.
Support previous conflict resolution efforts, as appropriate.
Reinforce progress made in the grieving process.
Assist in identifying necessary lifestyle modifications.

definicao: Assisting in the resolution of a perinatal loss.
atividades: Discuss with parents and family what to expect if loss is imminent or likely.
Encourage participation in decisions about withdrawing life support.
Assist in keeping the infant alive until the parents arrive.
Baptize the infant, if appropriate.
Encourage parents to hold the infant as they die, as appropriate.
Support parents and family whatever the bond they may or may not have with the pregnancy and the fetus.
Determine how and when the fetal or infant death was diagnosed.
Discuss plans that have been made (e.g., burial, funeral, baby's name).
Discuss decisions that need to be made about funeral arrangements, autopsy, genetic counseling, and family participation.
Describe mementos that will be obtained, including footprints and handprints, photos, hats, clothes, blankets, diapers, and blood pressure cuffs, as appropriate.
Discuss available support groups, as appropriate.
Discuss differences between male and female grief patterns, as appropriate.
Obtain footprints, handprints, length, and weight of the infant, as needed.
Prepare the infant to be viewed by bathing and dressing them, including parents in the activities, as appropriate.
Encourage family members to see and hold the infant for as long as they wish.
Discuss the infant's appearance based on gestational age and time of death.
Focus on the infant's normal characteristics when sensitively discussing anomalies.
Encourage the family to have time alone with the infant, as desired.
Provide referrals to chaplain, social work, grief counselor, and genetic counselor, as appropriate.
Create mementos and offer to the family before burial, as appropriate.
Offer to complete a commemorative birth certificate if state law does not require one at the gestational age of delivery.
Discuss normal and abnormal grief characteristics, including triggers that precipitate feelings of sadness.
Notify the laboratory or funeral agency for body disposition, as appropriate.
Transfer the infant to the morgue or prepare the body for transport by the family to the funeral agency.

definicao: Encouraging the patient to assume greater responsibility for their own behavior.
atividades: Hold the patient accountable for their own behavior.
Discuss with the patient the extent of responsibility for their current condition.
Determine if the patient has adequate knowledge about their health condition.
Encourage verbalization of feelings, perceptions, and fears about assuming responsibility.
Assist patients in identifying areas where they could readily assume more responsibility.
Encourage goal setting.
Facilitate the patient and family making choices regarding their own care, when appropriate.
Encourage independence, but assist the patient when unable to perform the task or function.
Ensure that patients and families have appropriate resources to assume more responsibility.
Discuss the consequences of not handling one's own responsibilities.
Provide opportunities for self-assessment and self-reflection.
Monitor the level of responsibility the patient assumes.
Provide constructive positive feedback for accepting additional responsibility for behavior change.
Encourage admission of mistakes when appropriate.
Set limits on manipulative behaviors.
Avoid arguing or bargaining with the patient about the limits set.
Encourage the patient to assume as much responsibility as possible for their self-care.
Assist parents in identifying age-appropriate tasks for which a child could be responsible.
Encourage parents to clearly communicate expectations for responsible behavior in the child, when appropriate.
Encourage parents to persist in expectations for responsible behavior in the child, when appropriate.
Facilitate family support for a new level of responsibility desired or achieved by the patient.
Assist with creating a schedule to guide increasing responsibility in the future.

definicao: Assisting the patient in mobilizing airway secretions through percussion, vibration, and postural drainage.
atividades: Check for contraindications to respiratory physiotherapy use (e.g., acute exacerbation of COPD, pneumonia without evidence of excess mucus production, osteoporosis, lung cancer, and cerebral edema).
Perform respiratory physiotherapy at least 2 hours after eating.
Explain to the patient the purpose and procedures used during respiratory physiotherapy.
Position any necessary equipment nearby (e.g., suction equipment, secretion collection container, and tissues).
Monitor respiratory and cardiac status (e.g., rate, rhythm, breath sounds, and depth of breathing).
Monitor the amount and character of secretions.
Identify the pulmonary segment(s) containing excessive secretions.

Position the patient with the pulmonary segment to be drained facing up, making modifications for patients unable to tolerate the prescribed position (i.e., avoid placing patients with COPD, acute head trauma, and cardiac problems in Trendelenburg position, as this increases shortness of breath, intracranial pressure, and stress, respectively).
Use pillows to support the patient in the determined position.

Percuss the chest rhythmically and in rapid succession with cupped hands over the area(s) to be drained for 3 to 5 minutes, avoiding percussion over the spine, kidneys, female breasts, incisions, and fractured ribs.
Apply pneumatic, acoustic, or electric chest percussors.
Vibrate the hands rapidly and vigorously, keeping shoulders and arms straight and wrists rigid over the area(s) to be drained while the patient exhales or coughs 3 to 4 times.
Instruct the patient to expectorate loosened secretions through deep breathing.
Stimulate coughing during and after the procedure.
Suction loosened secretions.
Monitor the patient's tolerance during and after the procedure (e.g., pulse oximetry, vital signs, and reported comfort level).

definicao: Use of active ingredients or substances contained in medicinal plants to maintain health or for disease treatment.
atividades: Use health history to determine the appropriateness of herbal or flower essence therapy.
Check health history for allergies.
Instruct the patient on the therapeutic use of herbs or flower essences (history, philosophy, modes of action, contraindications).
Select various appropriate plants related to the individual's health status.
Obtain medicinal substances from a reputable source.
Consider the use of flower essences in prepared stocks, such as Bach's collection of 38 flower essences.
Prepare medicinal herbs or flower essences (e.g., harvest, wash, cut, cook), as indicated.
Determine the form in which the medicinal plant or plant part is prepared (e.g., infusion, decoction, maceration).
Store preparations away from direct sunlight in a moderate environment.
Determine the dose and guidelines for administration of herbal products.
Assist the patient in the correct use of herbal products (e.g., preparation, administration time, method of administration).
Instruct on the effects of interaction with other prescribed medications, if needed.

definicao: Assisting the patient in increasing their personal judgment of their own worth.
atividades: Monitor the patient's statements of self-worth.
Determine the patient's locus of control.
Determine the patient's confidence in their own judgment.
Encourage the patient to identify strengths.
Assist the patient in finding self-acceptance.
Encourage eye contact during communication with others.
Reinforce positive personal points recognized by the patient.
Encourage the patient to talk to themselves and verbalize positive affirmations about themselves daily.
Provide experiences that increase patient autonomy, as appropriate.
Assist the patient in identifying positive responses from others.
Avoid negative criticism.
Assist the patient in overcoming bullying or teasing.
Convey confidence in the patient's ability to handle situations.
Assist in setting realistic goals to achieve greater self-esteem.
Assist the patient in accepting dependence on others, as appropriate.
Assist the patient in re-evaluating negative self-perceptions.
Encourage greater responsibility for oneself, as appropriate.
Assist the patient in identifying the impact of the peer group on feelings of self-worth.
Explore achieved accomplishments.
Explore reasons for self-criticism or guilt.
Encourage the patient to evaluate their own behavior.
Encourage the patient to accept new challenges.
Reward or praise the patient's progress toward achieving goals.
Facilitate an environment and activities that enhance self-esteem.
Assist the patient in identifying the importance of culture, religion, race, gender, and age on self-esteem.
Clarify to parents the importance of their interest and support for their children to develop a positive self-esteem concept.
Instruct parents to establish clear expectations and set limits for children.
Instruct parents to recognize their children's achievements.
Monitor the frequency of negative self-verbalizations.
Monitor for failure to follow through in achieving goals.
Monitor self-esteem levels over time, as appropriate.
Make positive affirmations about the patient.

definicao: Use of light therapy to reduce bilirubin levels in newborns.
atividades: Review maternal and infant history for risk factors for hyperbilirubinemia (e.g., Rh or AB0 incompatibility, polycythemia, sepsis, prematurity, inappropriate presentation).
Observe for signs of jaundice.
Request verification of bilirubin levels, as appropriate, according to protocol or responsible professional's request.
Report laboratory values to the responsible professional.
Place the infant in an incubator.
Instruct the family on phototherapy procedures and care.
Apply eye protectors to cover both eyes, avoiding excessive pressure.
Remove eye protectors every 4 hours or when lights are off for parental contact or feeding.
Monitor eyes for edema, drainage, and discoloration.
Place phototherapy lights above the infant at an appropriate height.
Check light intensity daily.
Monitor vital signs according to protocol or when needed.
Change the infant's position every 4 hours or according to protocol.
Monitor serum bilirubin levels according to protocol or medical order.
Assess neurological status every 4 hours or according to protocol.
Observe for signs of dehydration (e.g., depressed fontanels, poor skin turgor, weight loss).
Weigh daily.
Encourage eight feedings per day.
Encourage the family to participate in phototherapy.
Instruct the family on home phototherapy, as appropriate.

definicao: Administration of a dose of bright light to elevate mood and/or normalize the biological clock.
atividades: Obtain a medical prescription for phototherapy (e.g., frequency, distance, intensity, and duration of phototherapy), as appropriate.
Instruct patient/significant other about the treatment (e.g., indications for use, treatment procedure).
Assist the patient in obtaining the appropriate light source for treatment.
Assist the patient in adjusting the light source as prescribed in preparation for treatment.
Assist the patient in using the treatment.
Supervise the patient as needed during treatment.
Monitor for adverse effects of treatment (e.g., headaches, eye strain, nausea, insomnia, hyperactivity).
Terminate treatment if the patient develops adverse effects.
Notify the attending physician of adverse effects.
Modify the treatment as prescribed to decrease/eliminate adverse effects.
Document the treatment and patient response.

definicao: Coordination of care and support for specific individuals and patient populations across different settings to reduce costs and resource use, improve quality of healthcare, and achieve desired outcomes.
atividades: Identify individuals or patient populations that would benefit from case management (e.g., high cost, high volume, and/or high risk).
Identify the payer source for case management services.
Explain the role of the case manager to the patient and family.
Explain the cost of the service to the patient and/or family before providing care.
Obtain permission from the patient or family for enrollment in a case management program, as appropriate.
Develop a relationship with the patient, family, and other healthcare providers, as needed.
Use effective communication skills with the patient, family, and other healthcare providers.
Treat the patient and family with dignity and respect.
Maintain patient and family confidentiality and privacy.
Assess the patient's physical health status, mental state, and functional capacity, formal and informal support systems, financial resources, and environmental conditions, as needed.
Determine the treatment plan with patient and/or family participation.
Explain the sequence of activities to the patient and family.
Individualize the sequence of activities for the patient.
Determine outcomes to be achieved with patient and/or family participation.
Discuss the care plan and desired outcomes with the patient's physician.
Negotiate work schedules with the nurse manager (head nurse) to participate in weekly group practice meetings, as needed.
Integrate case management information and reviewed interventions (processes) into shift reports and group practice meetings, as needed.
Assess progress toward established goals continuously.
Review interventions and goals to meet patient needs as needed.
Identify needed resources and/or services.
Coordinate provision of needed resources or services.
Coordinate care with other relevant healthcare professionals (e.g., other nurses, physicians, social workers, health plan administration, physical therapists).
Provide direct care as needed.
Instruct the patient and/or family on the importance of self-care.
Encourage appropriate decision-making activities by the patient and/or family.
Document all case management activities.
Monitor the plan for quality, quantity, timeliness, and effectiveness of services.
Facilitate access to necessary health and social services.
Assist the patient and/or family in accessing the healthcare delivery system.
Instruct the patient and/or family about the healthcare delivery system.
Assist the patient and/or family in making informed decisions regarding healthcare.
Advocate for the patient as needed.
Recognize the need to integrate patient, clinical, and financial concerns.
Notify the patient and/or family of service change, service interruption, and discharge from the case management program.
Promote efficient use of resources.
Monitor cost-effectiveness of care.
Modify care for better cost-effectiveness as needed.
Establish a quality improvement program to evaluate case management activities.
Document cost-effectiveness of case management.
Report outcomes to health plans and other third parties.
Publicize services to individuals, families, insurers, and employers.

definicao: Obtaining and guiding the use of resources.
Coordination of emergency measures to sustain life.
atividades: Assess patient responsiveness to determine appropriate actions.
Call for help if there is no breathing or breathing is abnormal and the patient does not respond.
Activate the code according to institutional standards when obtaining the automated external defibrillator (AED) or ensure someone requests the automated external defibrillator.
Ensure the patient's airway is clear.
Perform cardiopulmonary resuscitation (CPR) focusing on chest compressions in adults and chest compressions and ventilation for children.
Perform cardioversion or defibrillation as soon as possible.
Minimize the interval between interruption of chest compressions and shock delivery, if indicated.
Bring the emergency cart near the bed.
Monitor the quality of CPR provided.
Install the cardiac monitor and determine the rhythm, ensuring defibrillations will not be interrupted.
Ensure someone is ventilating the patient and assisting with intubation as indicated.
Obtain IV access and administer IV fluids as indicated.
Ensure someone is: (1) preparing the medications; (2) administering the medications; (3) interpreting the ECG and performing cardioversion/defibrillation as needed; and (4) documenting care.
Remind the team about current Advanced Cardiac Life Support protocols, as appropriate.

Ensure special resuscitation protocols are instituted (e.g., asthma, anaphylaxis, pregnancy, morbid obesity, pulmonary embolism, electrolyte imbalance, toxic substance ingestion, trauma, accidental hypothermia, avalanche, drowning, electrical shock or lightning strikes, percutaneous coronary interventions, cardiac tamponade, cardiac surgery), when appropriate.
Offer family members and significant others opportunities to be present during resuscitation when in the patient's best interest.

Provide support to family members present during resuscitation (i.e., ensure a safe environment, provide explanations and commentary, enable appropriate communication with the patient, continuously assess needs, offer opportunities to reflect on resuscitation efforts after the event).
Ensure someone is coordinating care for other patients on the nursing unit.
Terminate care as indicated by the patient's condition.

Ensure organized post-cardiac arrest care (e.g., cardiorespiratory and neurological support, therapeutic hypothermia, gradual weaning of inspired oxygen concentration to avoid harmful hyperoxia, avoid hyperventilation).
Implement appropriate procedures for possible tissue and organ donation that are timely, effective, and according to the wishes of family and patient.
Review actions after care to identify strengths and areas for improvement.
Provide opportunities for team members to engage in discussion or reflection on resuscitation efforts after the event.
Perform emergency cart checks according to institutional protocol.

definicao: Use of a group environment to offer emotional support and health-related information to members.
atividades: Determine the level and adequacy of the patient's current support system.
Use a support group during transitional stages to assist the patient in adapting to a new lifestyle.
Determine the purpose of the group and the nature of the group process.
Determine the most appropriate location for the group meeting (e.g., face-to-face or online).
Identify faith-based groups as available options for patients, as appropriate.
Create a relaxing and accepting atmosphere.
Clarify in advance the group's goals and the responsibilities of members and leaders.
Use a co-leader, as appropriate.
Use a written contract if deemed appropriate.
Choose members who can contribute to and benefit from group interaction.
Form a group of optimal size (e.g., 5 to 12 members).
Address the issue of mandatory attendance.
Address the issue of whether new members can join the group at any time.
Establish the time and place for group meetings.

definicao: Assisting the patient in achieving a state of focused attention and concentration with suspension of part of peripheral awareness to create changes in sensations, thoughts, or behavior.
atividades: Obtain a history of the problems to be treated by hypnosis.
Determine the goals for hypnosis with the patient.
Determine the patient's receptivity to the use of hypnosis.
Correct myths and misconceptions about hypnosis.
Ensure the patient has accepted the treatment.
Assess patient acceptance by assessing their hypnotic suggestibility.
Determine the patient's history of trance states, such as daydreaming and 'highway hypnosis.'
Confirm the presence of a trusting relationship.
Prepare a quiet, comfortable environment.
Take precautions to prevent interruptions.
Instruct the patient on the purpose of the intervention.
Instruct the patient that they will induce the trance state and maintain control.
Sit comfortably, facing the patient's profile, when appropriate.
Discuss hypnotic suggestions to be used with the patient before induction.

Select an induction technique (e.g., Chevreul's pendulum illusion, relaxation, imagining descending stairs, eye closure, arm levitation, simple muscle relaxation, visualization exercises, attention to breathing, repetition of key words/phrases, and others).
Use the patient's language as much as possible.
Provide a small number of suggestions in an assertive manner.
Combine suggestions with naturally occurring events.
Use a permissive attitude to assist in trance induction.
Use a rhythmic, soft, monotone voice during patient induction.
Synchronize statements with the patient's breathing.
Encourage the patient to take deep breaths to intensify the relaxation state and decrease tension.
Assist the patient in escaping to a pleasant place using guided imagery.
Assist the patient in identifying appropriate deepening techniques (e.g., moving the hand to the face, imaginary scaling technique, fractionation, and others).
Avoid guessing what the patient is thinking.
Assist the patient in using all senses during the process.
Determine when to use direct or indirect imagery with the patient, as appropriate.
Facilitate rapid induction through specific signaling (e.g., verbal or visual) with experience.
Instruct the patient that the trance level is not important for successful hypnosis.
Facilitate the patient's emergence from trance by counting to a pre-agreed number, as appropriate.
Assist the patient in emerging from trance at their own pace, as appropriate.
Provide positive feedback to the patient after each episode.
Encourage the patient to use self-induction independently of whether the nurse controls a problem under treatment.
Identify situations, such as painful procedures, when the patient needs additional support staff for effective induction.

definicao: Facilitating the patient to perceive, appreciate, and express what is funny, amusing, or absurd, to establish relationships, relieve tension, release anger, facilitate learning, or cope with painful feelings.
atividades: Determine the types of humor appreciated by the patient.
Determine the patient's typical humor responses (e.g., laughter or smiles).
Determine the time of day when the patient is most receptive.
Avoid areas of content about which the patient is sensitive.
Discuss the advantages of laughter with the patient.
Select humor materials that create moderate excitement in the individual.
Make available a selection of humor games, cartoons.

definicao: Analysis of potential risk factors, determination of health risks, and prioritization of risk reduction strategies for an individual or group.
atividades: Review health history and previous documents for evidence of previous medical and nursing diagnoses and treatments.
Review data derived from routine risk assessment measures.
Determine the availability and quality of resources (e.g., psychological, financial, education level, family, community).
Identify available resources to assist in decreasing risk factors.
Maintain accurate records and statistics.
Identify biological, behavioral, and environmental risks and their interrelationships.
Identify typical coping strategies for problems.
Determine previous and current level of functioning.
Determine the status of basic life needs.
Determine appropriate community resources for basic life and health needs.
Determine adherence to medical treatments and nursing approaches.
Instruct on risk factors and plan risk reduction.
Use mutual goal-setting situations as appropriate.

Consider useful criteria in prioritizing areas for risk reduction (e.g., awareness and motivation level, effectiveness, cost, availability, preference, equity, stigmatization, and severity of outcomes if risks remain unaddressed).
Discuss and plan risk reduction activities in collaboration with an individual or group.
Implement risk reduction activities.
Initiate referrals to healthcare professionals or institutions as appropriate.
Plan long-term monitoring for health risks.
Plan long-term follow-up of risk reduction strategies and activities.

definicao: Identification and analysis of potential genetic risk factors in individuals, family, or group.
atividades: Ensure privacy and confidentiality.

Obtain or review a complete health history, including prenatal and obstetric history, developmental history, and current and past health status related to the confirmed or suspected genetic condition.
Obtain or review environmental history (e.g., potential teratogen and carcinogen exposures) and lifestyle (e.g., tobacco, alcohol, substance exposure).
Determine the presence and quality of family support, other support systems, and previous coping skills.
Obtain or review comprehensive family history and construct at least a three-generation pedigree.
Obtain documented diagnosis of affected family members.

Review diagnostic testing options that may confirm or predict the presence of a genetic disorder, such as biochemical or radiographic studies, chromosomal analysis, linkage analysis, or direct DNA testing.
Provide information on diagnostic procedures.
Discuss advantages, risks, and financial costs of diagnostic options.
Discuss insurance aspects and possible workplace discrimination as relevant.
Discuss aspects of testing with other family members as relevant.
Initiate genetic counseling intervention based on risk identification, as appropriate.
Refer to genetic healthcare specialists for genetic counseling as needed.
Provide the patient with a written summary of the risk identification counseling, as indicated.

definicao: Identification of individuals or families at potential risk for parenting difficulties and prioritization of strategies to prevent parenting problems.
Positive verification of a patient's identity.
atividades: Explain to the patient the importance of proper identification throughout their stay in the healthcare institution.
Ask the patient's first and last name, as well as date of birth.

Verify that the information provided by the patient matches the information on the identification device (e.g., wristband, bed label, fingerprint recognition software, palm vein scan) and the medical record.
Choose the most appropriate location for placement of the identification device.
Ensure the identification device is placed in an appropriate location.
Have multiple wristbands and an easy process for personalization and placement of a new wristband if the previous one is lost or needs to be removed.
Standardize the wristband format throughout the healthcare institution.

Compare the information provided by the patient with the information on the patient identification before each care administration (e.g., medication administration, performance of invasive procedures, conducting diagnostic tests, patient transfer).
Use at least two patient identifiers when obtaining samples for laboratory tests or when administering medications or blood products.
Conduct patient verification at multiple times during the process when the procedure is complex and involves multiple stages.
Use identification by a family member or close friend when the patient cannot provide the information.
Institute a 'stop the line' policy if an identification error is suspected (e.g., do not continue with an action until positive identification is made).
Instruct the patient about the risks related to incorrect identification.

definicao: Intentional use of imagination to achieve a specific state, outcome, or action, or to divert attention from undesirable sensations.
atividades: Assess for severe emotional problems, history of psychiatric illness, or hallucinations.
Assess for decreased current energy level, inability to concentrate, or other symptoms that interfere with cognitive ability to create focus on mental images.
Describe the rationale, benefits, limitations, and types of guided imagery techniques available.
Identify information about past coping experiences to determine if guided imagery may be useful.
Discuss the ability to create and experience mental images as if they were real.
Determine the ability to do guided imagery without the nurse (e.g., alone or with a tape).
Encourage the individual to choose from a variety of guided imagery techniques (e.g., nurse-guided, with a tape).
Suggest that the individual be in a comfortable position, with comfortable clothing and eyes closed.
Provide a comfortable, interruption-free environment (e.g., using headphones), as possible.
Discuss an image the patient has experienced that is pleasant and relaxing, such as lying on a beach, watching a new snowfall, floating on a raft, or watching the sunset.
Individualize chosen images, considering religious or spiritual beliefs, artistic interest, or other individual preferences.
Describe the scene using as many of the five senses as possible.
Make suggestions to induce relaxation (e.g., images of peace, pleasant sensations, or rhythmic breathing), as appropriate.
Use a modulated voice when guiding the imagery.
Mentally guide the patient to the scene and assist in describing details.
Use permissive guidance and suggestions when guiding imagery, such as 'maybe,' 'if you want,' or 'you might like.'
Guide the patient slowly through the scene; what does it look like? smell? sound? feel? taste?
Use words or phrases that convey pleasant images, such as floating, melting, releasing, and so on.
Develop a process of clearing or releasing the images (e.g., all the pain appears as red dust and washes away in a stream as you enter).
Assist the patient in developing a method to end the imagery technique, such as slow counting while breathing deeply, and slow movements and thoughts of being relaxed, rested, and alert.
Encourage the patient to express thoughts and feelings about the experience.
Prepare the patient for unexpected (but often therapeutic) experiences, such as crying.
Instruct the patient to practice the imagery if possible.
Record the imagery experience on tape if helpful.
Plan with the patient an appropriate time to do guided imagery.
Use imagery techniques preventively.
Plan a follow-up to evaluate the effects of imagery and any resulting change in sensation and perception.
Use guided imagery as an adjunctive strategy to pain medications or in conjunction with other measures, as appropriate.
Assess and document the response to guided imagery.

definicao: Stabilization, immobilization, and/or protection of an injured body part with a support device.
atividades: Monitor circulation (e.g., pulse, capillary refill, and sensation) in the injured body part.
Monitor movement distal to the injury site.
Monitor bleeding at the injury site.
Cover the open wound with a dressing and control bleeding before applying the immobilizer.
Minimize patient movement, especially of the injured body part.
Identify the most appropriate immobilization material (e.g., rigid, soft, anatomical, or with traction).
Pad rigid immobilizers.
Immobilize the joint above and the joint below the injury site.
Support the feet using a footboard.
Place the injured hand or wrist in a functional position.

Apply the immobilizer in the position in which the injured body part is found, using hands to support the injury site, minimizing movement, and, when possible, using assistance from another team member.
Apply the immobilizer as appropriate.
Monitor skin integrity under the support device.
Encourage isometric exercises, as appropriate.
Instruct the patient or family members on caring for the immobilizer.

definicao: Achievement and maintenance of core body temperature below 35°C and monitoring of side effects and/or prevention of complications.
atividades: Monitor vital signs, as appropriate.
Monitor patient temperature using a continuous core temperature monitoring device.
Place the patient on a cardiac monitor.
Institute active external cooling measures (e.g., ice packs, cooled water blanket, cooled water circulating blankets), as appropriate.

definicao: Initiation or augmentation of labor by mechanical or pharmacological methods.
atividades: Determine medical and obstetric indications for induction.

Review obstetric history for pertinent information that may influence induction (e.g., gestational age, duration of previous labor, placenta previa, structural pelvic deformities, previous uterine rupture, active genital herpes, umbilical cord prolapse, transverse presentation, Category III fetal heart rate [FHR]).
Determine the Bishop score to assess cervical readiness for induction (e.g., a score of 8 or more is more favorable for vaginal delivery).
Monitor maternal and fetal vital signs before induction.
Perform or assist with the application of mechanical or pharmacological agents at appropriate intervals to improve cervical readiness, if needed.
Assist with membrane stripping if the patient is 39 weeks gestation or more.
Monitor side effects of procedures used to prepare the cervix.
Reassess cervical status and verify presentation before initiating induction measures.
Perform or assist with amniotomy if cervical dilation is adequate and the vertex is engaged.
Determine fetal heart rate by auscultation or electronic monitoring post-amniotomy and per protocol.
Encourage ambulation if there are no contraindications for the mother and fetus.
Observe the appearance or change in uterine activity.
Initiate IV medication (e.g., oxytocin) to stimulate uterine activity according to protocol, if needed.
Regulate the uterine stimulant until delivery is imminent, as needed or per protocol.
Closely monitor labor progress, being alert for signs of abnormal labor progress.
Avoid uterine tachysystole by using a low-dose protocol to infuse oxytocin to achieve adequate contraction frequency, duration, and relaxation.
Monitor data provided by the intrauterine pressure catheter.
Observe for signs of uteroplacental insufficiency (e.g., late decelerations and Category III fetal heart rate changes) during the induction process.
Monitor for fluid retention during oxytocin administration.

definicao: Description in concrete, objective terms of the typical sensory experiences and events associated with an upcoming stressful health procedure/treatment.
atividades: Identify the sequence of events and describe the environment associated with the procedure/treatment.
Identify typical sensations (e.g., what will be seen, felt, smelled, tasted, heard) that most patients describe as associated with each aspect of the procedure/treatment.
Describe sensations in concrete, objective terms, using the patient's descriptive words but omitting evaluative adjectives that reflect the degree of sensation or emotional response to a sensation.
Present sensations and events of the procedure/treatment in the most likely sequence to be experienced.
Relate sensations to their cause when not automatically evident.
Describe how long sensations and events in the procedure can be expected or when they can be expected to change.
Personalize the information using personal pronouns.
Offer the patient the opportunity to ask questions and clarify misunderstandings.

definicao: Infusion of hematopoietic stem cells and monitoring of patient response.
atividades: Ensure the product to be infused has been prepared, labeled, and classified according to institutional protocol.
Explain the procedure and purpose of hematopoietic stem cell infusion to patients and caregivers.

Inform the patient and family about possible adverse reactions (e.g., transfusion reaction, volume overload, pulmonary embolism, vital sign changes, and nausea/vomiting) that may arise during infusion.
Use peppermint oil or mint candy to neutralize unpleasant odor and taste.
Prepare filterless infusion equipment and other necessary materials (e.g., 0.9% normal saline, measurement systems for

definicao: Insertion or assistance with insertion and stabilization of an artificial airway.
atividades: Perform hand hygiene.
Use personal protective equipment (PPE) (gloves, eye protection, and mask), as appropriate.
Select the correct size and type of oropharyngeal or nasopharyngeal airway.
Position the patient and head, as appropriate.
Suction the mouth and oropharynx.
Insert the oronasal/pharyngeal airway, ensuring it reaches the base of the tongue, providing support in the forward-projected position.
Secure the oronasal/pharyngeal airway in place.
Monitor for dyspnea, snoring, or inspiratory stridor when the oronasal/pharyngeal airway is positioned.
Change the oronasal/pharyngeal airway fixation site daily and examine the mucosa.
Insert a laryngeal mask airway (LMA), as appropriate.
Insert an esophageal obturator airway (EOA), as appropriate.
Auscultate for bilateral breath sounds before inflating the EOA cuff.
Collaborate with the physician in choosing the correct size and type of endotracheal tube (ETT) or tracheostomy tube.
Select artificial airways with high-volume, low-pressure cuffs.
Limit insertion of endotracheal and tracheostomy tubes to credentialed and qualified professionals.
Encourage physicians to place ETTs via the oropharyngeal route, as appropriate.

Assist with ETT insertion by gathering necessary intubation and emergency equipment, positioning the patient, administering medications as requested, and monitoring the patient for complications during insertion.
Assist with emergency tracheostomy procedure by preparing appropriate support equipment, administering medications, ensuring sterility during the procedure, and monitoring changes in patient status.
Instruct the patient and family about the intubation procedure.
Hyperoxygenate with 100% oxygen for 3 to 5 minutes, as appropriate.
Auscultate the chest after intubation.
Observe systematic chest wall movement.
Monitor oxygen saturation (SpO2) via non-invasive pulse oximetry and CO2 detection.
Monitor respiratory status, as appropriate.
Inflate the endotracheal/tracheostomy cuff using the minimal occlusive volume technique or the minimal leak technique.
Secure the endotracheal/tracheostomy tube with tape, ties, or a commercially available stabilization device.
Mark the endotracheal tube at the lips or nares using the centimeter markings on the ETT and document.
Verify tube placement with chest radiograph, ensuring intubation of the trachea 2 to 4 cm above the carina.

Minimize elevation and traction of the artificial airway by suspending the ventilator circuit from a support, using flexible and swivel mounts, and supporting the tube during position changes, suctioning, and reconnection/disconnection from the ventilator.

definicao: Deliberate use of culturally competent strategies to bridge or mediate between the patient's culture and the biomedical healthcare system.
Critical analysis of patient laboratory data to assist in clinical decision-making.
atividades: Familiarize yourself with abbreviations accepted by the specific institution.
Use the reference values of the laboratory performing the specific test(s).
Recognize physiological factors that may affect laboratory values, including ethnicity, gender, age, pregnancy, menstrual cycle, diet (especially hydration), time of day, activity level, and stress.
Recognize the effects of drugs on laboratory values, including prescription medications as well as non-prescription medications.
Note the time and location of sample collection, as appropriate.
Use peak drug levels when testing for toxicity.
Recognize which drug levels are useful to demonstrate a satisfactory therapeutic level.
Consider pharmacokinetic influences (e.g., half-life, peak, protein binding, and excretion) when evaluating toxic and therapeutic drug levels.
Consider that several test changes are more likely to be significant than individual test abnormalities.
Compare test results with another laboratory and/or diagnostic test.
Compare results with values obtained previously when the patient was not ill (if available) to determine baseline values.
Monitor sequential test results for trends or macroscopic changes.
Consult appropriate references/texts for clinical implications of unknown tests.
Recognize that incorrect test results mostly result from clerical errors.
Carefully confirm grossly abnormal test results, sample identification, sample condition, and timely delivery to the laboratory.
Report laboratory test results to the patient, as appropriate.
Send split samples to the laboratory for result verification, if appropriate.
Immediately report sudden changes in laboratory values to the physician.
Immediately report critical values (as determined by the institution) to the physician.
Analyze whether the results obtained are consistent with the patient's clinical behavior and status.

definicao: Use of short-term counseling to assist the patient in coping with a crisis and returning to a functional state comparable to or better than the pre-crisis state.
atividades: Provide a supportive atmosphere.
Avoid giving false reassurance.
Offer an atmosphere of safety.
Determine if the patient is at safety risk for self or others.
Initiate necessary precautions to protect the patient or others at risk of physical harm.
Encourage expression of feelings in a non-destructive manner.
Assist in identifying the precipitants and dynamics of the crisis.
Encourage the patient to focus on one implication at a time.
Assist in identifying personal strengths and skills that can be used in crisis resolution.
Assist in identifying past/present coping skills and their effectiveness.
Assist in developing new coping and problem-solving skills as needed.
Assist in identifying available support systems.
Link the patient and family to community resources as needed.
Provide guidance on how to develop and maintain support system(s).
Introduce the patient to people (or groups) who have gone through the

definicao: Flushing of the wound with solution.
atividades: Gather necessary equipment and materials at the bedside (e.g., irrigation set, sterile field, sterile basin, sterile irrigation solution, sterile gloves, and dressing change materials).
Identify any allergies related to the products to be used.
Explain the procedure to the patient.
Administer analgesics before wound care, if needed.
Assist the patient into a comfortable position, ensuring the solution will flow by gravity from the least contaminated area to the most contaminated and will collect in the basin.
Place a sterile field and towels under the patient.
Perform hand hygiene.
Put on mask, eye protection, and apron, if needed.
Remove the dressing and inspect the wound and adjacent tissue, reporting abnormalities to the appropriate healthcare professional (e.g., infection and necrosis).
Place the prescribed irrigation solution in the sterile irrigation bottle, ensuring the solution is warmed to room temperature.
Use sterile gloves.
Open the irrigation syringe and attach it to the bottle containing the solution.
Place the sterile basin at the distal end of the wound.
Fill the irrigation syringe with the solution.
Avoid aspirating the solution back into the syringe.

Gently spray the solution into the wound until the solution flowing into the basin becomes clear, ensuring the syringe tip is held 2.5 cm above the wound and washing from the least contaminated to the most contaminated area.
Attach a sterile silicone or latex catheter to the filled syringe when needed (e.g., to irrigate deep wounds).
Avoid applying pressure with the catheter on an abdominal wound to prevent bowel perforation.
Refill the irrigation syringe, maintaining sterility (i.e., when using a catheter, disconnect it, fill the syringe, and reconnect it).
Open the solution package if used for irrigation and follow the instructions.
Clean and dry the surrounding skin after the procedure.
Perform appropriate wound or burn care.
Apply a sterile dressing.
Protect the wound with the appropriate type of sterile dressing.
Monitor the patient's pain levels, tolerance, comfort, and anxiety during the procedure.

Maintain a sterile field during the procedure, as appropriate (e.g., use aids to prevent children from moving and contaminating the wound, or use a sterile field and teach children not to touch the wound).

Instruct the patient or family members on how to perform the procedure at home using the proper technique and with necessary modifications (e.g., emphasize the importance of washing hands before and after irrigation when sterile technique is not used).
Properly dispose of materials.

definicao: Improving nasal mucosal function using saline lavage.
atividades: Consider contraindications before the procedure (e.g., patients with incompletely healed facial trauma and patients with musculoskeletal or neurological problems that increase the risk of aspiration).
Prepare the irrigation solution using pre-prepared bags or household ingredients.

Mix one teaspoon of salt with ½ teaspoon of baking soda with half a liter of warm potable water, adjusting the salinity and temperature of the solution according to institutional guidelines or patient preference, if needed.
Use salt that does not contain iodine, anti-caking agents, or preservatives to avoid nasal mucosal irritation (i.e., use kosher, canned, or pickling salt).

Aspirate the solution using a low positive pressure device (e.g., bulb syringe or spray bottle) or a gravity-based pressure device (e.g., nasal irrigator or other commercially available saline rinse product).
Position the patient over the sink or basin with the head tilted back and turned so that one nostril is higher than the other.
Gently insert the applicator tip or nozzle into the higher nostril until a comfortable seal is formed, avoiding pressing the tip or nozzle towards the septum.
Instill approximately half of the prepared solution into the nostril.
Instruct the patient to breathe normally through the mouth.
Monitor the return of fluid from the nostril (e.g., amount, color, consistency).
Repeat the procedure in the other nostril.
Adjust head position to avoid draining the solution into the back of the throat or ears.
Encourage the patient to gently blow their nose.
Irrigate the nasal passages 1 to 3 times per day or as prescribed.
Clean the irrigation device after each use.
Prepare the solution daily.
Discontinue if the patient experiences pain, epistaxis, or other problems.
Instruct the patient on self-irrigation techniques.
Administer nasal medication if needed.
Offer referral to a healthcare professional.

definicao: Instillation of solution into the bladder for cleansing or medication.
atividades: Instruct the patient and significant others about the procedure.
Maintain privacy and ensure uninterrupted time during the procedure.
Determine if irrigation will be continuous or intermittent.
Observe universal precautions.
Prepare sterile irrigation materials, maintaining aseptic technique per institutional protocol.
Position the patient comfortably, lying down in a semi-sitting position.
Cleanse the entry point or the Y-connector termination with alcohol before and after the procedure.
Instill irrigation fluids according to institutional protocol.
Keep the patient fully informed of each irrigation if repetition is needed.
Monitor the patient's comfort level and ease of irrigation with each instillation if repetition is needed.
Discontinue irrigations if the procedure becomes too difficult or if the catheter appears obstructed.
Notify the physician if the catheter is obstructed.
Monitor and maintain correct flow with continuous infusions.
Connect the catheter to a new drainage bag at the end of irrigation.
Record the amount of fluid used, fluid characteristics, amount returned, and patient responsiveness according to institutional protocol.
Ensure patient comfort at the end of the procedure.

definicao: Maintenance and promotion of oral hygiene and dental health for the patient at risk for developing oral or dental lesions.
atividades: Establish a routine of oral care.
Apply lubricant to moisturize lips and oral mucosa as needed.
Monitor the color, shine, and presence of debris on teeth.
Identify the risk of developing stomatitis secondary to medication therapy.
Encourage and assist the patient in rinsing the mouth.
Monitor therapeutic effects of topical anesthetics, protective oral pastes, and topical or systemic analgesics, as appropriate.
Instruct and assist the patient in performing oral hygiene after meals and whenever needed.
Monitor signs and symptoms of glossitis and stomatitis.
Consult with the physician or dentist about adjustment of braces/appliances and alternative oral care methods if they are causing oral mucous membrane irritation.
Consult with the physician if dry mouth, irritation, and discomfort persist.
Facilitate tooth brushing and flossing at regular intervals.
Recommend the use of a soft-bristled toothbrush.
Instruct the person to brush teeth, gums, and tongue.
Recommend a healthy diet and adequate water consumption.
Arrange dental check-ups as needed.
Assist with denture care as needed.
Encourage denture users to brush gums and tongue and rinse the oral cavity daily.
Discourage smoking and chewing tobacco.
Instruct the patient to chew sugarless gum to increase saliva and clean teeth.

definicao: Preserving vascular access sites (arteriovenous).
atividades: Monitor the catheter exit site for migration.
Monitor the access site for hyperemia, edema, warmth, drainage, bleeding, hematoma, and decreased sensation.
Apply sterile gauze, ointment, and dressing to the central venous catheter site at each treatment.
Monitor the AV fistula patency at frequent intervals (e.g., palpate for thrill and auscultate for bruit).
Heparinize newly inserted central venous dialysis catheters.

definicao: Minimizing the effect of disrupted family processes.
atividades: Determine typical family processes.
Determine interruptions in typical family processes.
Identify the effects of role changes on the family process.
Encourage continued contact with family members, as appropriate.
Maintain opportunities for flexible visitation to meet family and patient needs.
Discuss strategies to normalize family life with family members.
Assist family members in implementing normalization strategies for their situation.
Discuss existing social support mechanisms for the family.
Assist family members in using existing support mechanisms.
Minimize interruptions in the family routine by facilitating family routines and rituals, such as private meals together or family discussions for communication and decision-making.
Provide mechanisms for family members to remain in communication with other family members (e.g., phones, email and photo messages, audio and video).
Provide opportunities for ongoing parental care of hospitalized children.
Provide opportunities for adult family members to maintain current professional commitments, if possible, or to use the Family Leave Act.
Assist family members in facilitating the patient's visits home, when appropriate.
Identify home care needs and how they would be incorporated into the family lifestyle.
Schedule home care activities to minimize interruptions to the family routine.
Teach family time management/organizational skills when performing home care, as needed.

definicao: Use of marketing principles to influence health beliefs, attitudes, and behaviors for the benefit of a target population.
atividades: Maintain focus on the target audience throughout all activities.
Cultivate partnerships with the target audience and appropriate professionals.
Identify the overall goal to be achieved in collaboration with the target audience.
Identify formal, social, and governmental groups involved.
Conduct a needs assessment of the environment, identifying the wants of the target audience.
Conduct a group or organizational assessment.
Identify appropriate qualitative and quantitative research to provide information, support, and indicators of success.
Outline an action plan based on mutually agreed goals.
Cultivate the voluntary base of the planned social change.
Identify the product (behavior change), the price (consumer actions), and the place (how the product reaches the consumer).
Consider financial resources needed and available.
Identify specific activities for each goal, including naming appropriate participants.
Implement the plan in collaboration with the target population.
Control the plan by monitoring assigned tasks.
Provide appropriate reports periodically.
Evaluate the plan to achieve goals considering sustainability and accessibility.
Modify the plan as needed.

definicao: Stimulation of the skin and underlying tissues with varying degrees of manual pressure to decrease pain, produce relaxation, and/or improve circulation.
atividades: Screen for contraindications, such as decreased platelets, decreased skin integrity, deep vein thrombosis, areas with open wounds, redness or inflammation, tumors, and hypersensitivity to touch.
Assess the patient's willingness to have a massage.
Establish a time period for the massage to achieve the desired response.
Select the area(s) of the body to be massaged.
Wash hands with warm water.
Prepare a cozy, comfortable, private environment free from distractions.
Place the patient in a comfortable position that facilitates massage.
Use coverings to expose only the area to be massaged, as needed.
Cover unexposed areas with blankets, sheets, or towels, as needed.
Use lotion, oil, or dry powder to reduce friction (no lotion or oils on the head or scalp), assessing for any sensitivities or contraindications.
Warm the lotion or oil in the palms of the hands or place the container in warm water for a few minutes.
Massage using continuous, even strokes; kneading or vibration with palms, fingers, and thumbs.
Adapt massage area, technique, and pressure to the patient's perception of comfort and massage goal.
Massage the hands or feet if other areas are inconvenient or more comfortable for the patient.
Encourage the patient to take deep breaths and relax during the massage.
Encourage the patient to report any part of the massage that is

definicao: Facilitating constructive dialogue between opposing parties with the goal of resolving disputes in a mutually acceptable manner.
atividades: Provide a private, neutral environment for the conversation.
Allow parties to express their personal concerns.
Offer guidance throughout the process.
Maintain one's neutrality throughout the process.
Employ various communication techniques (e.g., active listening, questioning, paraphrasing, reflection).
Facilitate the definition of problems.
Assist parties in identifying possible solutions to the problems.
Facilitate the search for outcomes acceptable to both parties.
Support participants' efforts to promote resolution.
Monitor the flow of the mediation process.

definicao: Strengthening the individual's confidence in their own ability to perform a health behavior.
atividades: Explore the individual's perception of their ability to perform the desired behavior.
Explore the individual's perception of the benefits achieved by performing the desired behavior.
Identify the individual's perception of the risks involved in not performing the desired behavior.
Identify barriers to behavior change.
Provide information about the desired behavior.
Assist the individual in committing to an action plan to change behavior.
Reinforce confidence in adopting behavior changes and taking action.
Provide a supportive environment for learning the knowledge and skills necessary to perform the behavior.
Use culturally and age-appropriate teaching strategies (e.g., games, computer-assisted education, or conversation maps).
Simulate/demonstrate the desired behavior.
Use role-playing to practice the behavior.
Provide positive reinforcement and emotional support during the learning process and in implementing the behavior.
Provide opportunities for mastery experiences (e.g., successful implementation of the behavior).
Use persuasive positive affirmations about the individual's ability to adopt the behavior.
Encourage interaction with other individuals who are successfully changing their behavior (e.g., participation in a support group or educational group).
Prepare the individual for the physiological and emotional states that may be experienced during initial attempts to adopt a new behavior.

definicao: Assisting a patient in exploring and understanding their thoughts, feelings, motivations, and behaviors.
atividades: Encourage the patient to recognize and discuss thoughts and feelings.
Assist the patient in realizing that each person is unique.
Assist the patient in identifying values that contribute to self-concept.
Assist the patient in identifying usual feelings about themselves.
Share observations or thoughts about the patient's behavior or response.
Facilitate the patient's identification of usual response patterns to various situations.
Assist the patient in identifying life priorities.
Assist the patient in identifying the impact of illness on self-concept.
Verbalize the patient's denial of reality, as appropriate.
Confront the patient's ambivalent feelings (anger or depression).
Make observations about the patient's current emotional state.
Assist the patient in accepting dependence on others, as appropriate.
Assist the patient in changing their view of themselves as a victim by defining their own rights, as appropriate.
Assist the patient in becoming aware of negative self-affirmations.
Assist the patient in identifying feelings of guilt.
Assist the patient in identifying situations that precipitate anxiety.
Explore with the patient the need for control.
Assist the patient in identifying their own positive attributes.
Assist the patient/family in identifying reasons for improvement.
Assist the patient in identifying skills, learning styles.
Assist the patient in re-examining negative self-perceptions.
Assist the patient in identifying the source of motivation.
Assist the patient in identifying self-destructive behaviors.
Facilitate self-expression with a peer group.
Assist the patient in recognizing contradictory affirmations.

definicao: Improving cooperation between healthcare disciplines and professionals.
Use of strategies that increase the communication abilities of a person with diminished hearing.
atividades: Perform or arrange routine hearing assessments and screenings.
Monitor for cerumen buildup.
Instruct the patient not to use objects smaller than the tip of their finger (e.g., cotton swabs, bobby pins, toothpicks, and other pointed objects) to remove cerumen.
Remove excessive cerumen with the twisted end of a washcloth while pulling the ear downward.
Consider ear irrigation for removal of excessive cerumen if watchful waiting, manual removal, and ceruminolytic agents have not been effective.
Observe and document the patient's preferred communication method (e.g., verbal, written, lip reading, or sign language) in the care plan.
Get the patient's attention before speaking (e.g., obtain their attention through touch).
Avoid noisy environments when communicating.
Avoid communicating more than 60 to 90 cm from the patient.
Use gestures when needed.
Listen attentively, giving the patient adequate time to process communication and respond.
Avoid shouting at the patient.
Facilitate lip reading by looking directly at the patient in a well-lit location.
Ask the patient to suggest strategies for improving communication (e.g., speak towards the better ear and direct towards the well-lit area).
Face the patient directly, making eye contact, and avoid turning away mid-sentence.
Simplify language (i.e., do not use slang and use short, simple sentences), as appropriate.
Use a lower, deeper voice when speaking.
Avoid childish speech and exaggerated expressions.
Avoid smoking, eating or chewing gum, and covering your mouth when speaking.
Verify what was said or written using the patient's response before continuing.
Facilitate the use of hearing aids and assistive listening devices (e.g., telephone amplifier, wired devices, personal frequency modulation, and computers).
Remove and insert the hearing aid properly.
Remove the hearing aid battery when not in use for several days.
Clean the detachable ear mold using mild soap solution, removing moisture and residue with a soft cloth, and avoid isopropyl alcohol, solvents, and oil.
Clean the non-detachable ear mold with a damp cloth, removing moisture or residue with a soft cloth, and avoid isopropyl alcohol, solvents, and oil.
Check hearing aid batteries routinely, replacing when needed.
Consult manufacturer's instructions for proper use, care, and maintenance of hearing aids and assistive listening devices.
Instruct the patient, nursing staff, and family on the use, care, and maintenance of hearing aids and assistive listening devices.
Assist the patient or family in acquiring a hearing aid and assistive listening device.
Refer to the primary care provider or specialist for evaluation, treatment, and auditory rehabilitation.

definicao: Use of strategies that increase the communication abilities of a person with impaired speech.
atividades: Monitor speech rate, pressure, rhythm, amount, volume, and diction.
Monitor cognitive, anatomical, and physiological processes associated with speaking ability (e.g., memory, hearing, and language).
Instruct the patient or family on cognitive, anatomical, and physiological processes involved in speaking ability.
Monitor the patient for frustration, anger, depression, or other responses to impaired speaking ability.
Recognize emotional and physical behaviors as forms of communication.
Provide alternative methods to speech communication (e.g., tablet,

definicao: Use of strategies that increase the communication abilities of a person with diminished vision.
atividades: Perform or arrange routine vision assessments and screenings.
Monitor the functional implications of diminished vision (e.g., risk of injury, depression, anxiety, and ability to perform activities of daily living and valued activities).
Identify yourself when entering the patient's space.
Assist the patient in enhancing stimulation of other senses (e.g., aromas, taste, and texture of food).
Ensure the patient's glasses or contact lenses have an up-to-date prescription, are clean, and properly stored when not in use.
Provide adequate lighting in the environment.
Minimize glare (offer sunglasses or close curtains).
Provide literature with large print.
Describe the environment to the patient.
Keep the environment organized.
Avoid rearranging items in the patient's environment without notifying them.
Provide aids for activities of daily living (e.g., large-numbered clock and telephone).

Apply labels to distinguish frequently used items (place color-coded dots on appliance dials, mark medication bottles using contrasting colors or rubber bands, and mark similar-colored clothing with safety pins).
Use bright, contrasting colors in the environment.
Read correspondence, newspapers, and other pertinent information to the patient.
Identify items on the meal tray in a clockwise manner.
Fold currency in different ways to facilitate identification.
Provide magnification devices (e.g., portable magnifiers, stand magnifiers, and video magnifiers).
Provide visual substitutes (e.g., braille materials, audiobooks, talking clocks, and tactile markers).

Assist parents, family, educators, and caregivers involved with a visually impaired child in meeting information needs (e.g., how to teach the child, anticipatory guidance, and developmental considerations).
Instruct parents, family, educators, and caregivers to recognize and respond to non-traditional forms of expression (e.g., movements and facial expressions).
Assist parents, family, educators, and caregivers in developing reliable and functional communication systems (e.g., microswitches or voice output devices).
Assist the patient or family in identifying available resources for vision rehabilitation.
Provide referral for the patient needing surgical or other medical treatment.
Provide referral to support services (e.g., social, occupational, and psychological).

definicao: Improving the ability and willingness to receive information.
atividades: Provide a non-threatening environment.
Establish a relationship.
Establish credibility as a teacher, as appropriate.
Maximize the patient's hemodynamic status to facilitate brain oxygenation (e.g., positioning and medication adjustments), as appropriate.
Fulfill the patient's basic physiological needs (e.g., hunger, thirst, warmth, and oxygen).
Decrease the patient's fatigue level, as appropriate.
Manage the patient's pain, as appropriate.
Avoid the use of medications that may alter the patient's perception (e.g., narcotics and hypnotics), as appropriate.
Monitor the patient's level of orientation/confusion.
Increase the patient's orientation to reality, as appropriate.
Maximize sensory input through the use of glasses, hearing aids, and so forth, as appropriate.

definicao: Improving the patient's conscious and unconscious perceptions and attitudes about their body.
atividades: Determine the patient's expectations of body image based on developmental stage.
Use anticipatory guidance to prepare the patient for predictable body image changes.
Determine if perceived aversion to certain physical characteristics creates dysfunctional social paralysis in adolescents and high-risk groups.
Assist the patient in discussing changes caused by illness or surgery, as appropriate.
Assist the patient in determining the extent of actual body changes or level of functioning.
Determine if any recent physical changes have been incorporated into the patient's body image.
Assist the patient in separating physical appearance from feelings of personal worth, as appropriate.
Assist the patient in determining the influence of the peer group on their perception of current body image.
Assist the patient in discussing changes caused by puberty, as appropriate.
Assist the patient in discussing changes caused by a normal pregnancy, as appropriate.
Assist the patient in discussing changes caused by aging, as appropriate.
Instruct the patient on normal body changes associated with various stages of aging, as appropriate.
Assist the patient in discussing stressors affecting body image due to congenital condition, injury, illness, or surgery.
Identify the effects of culture, religion, race, gender, and age on the patient's body image.
Monitor the frequency of self-critical statements.
Monitor if the patient can look at the affected body part.
Monitor for statements identifying perceptions of body image regarding body shape and weight.
Use self-portrait drawings as an assessment mechanism for the child's perceptions of their own body.
Instruct children on the functions of various body parts, as appropriate.
Determine the patient's and family's perceptions of altered body image versus reality.
Identify coping strategies used by parents in response to changes in the child's appearance.
Determine how the child responds to parental reactions, as appropriate.
Teach parents about the importance of their responses to the child's body changes and future adjustment, as appropriate.
Assist parents in identifying feelings prior to intervention with the child, as appropriate.
Determine if a body image change has contributed to increased social isolation.
Assist the patient in identifying body parts about which they have positive perceptions.
Identify ways to reduce the effect of any disfigurement through clothing, wigs, or cosmetics, as appropriate.
Assist the patient in identifying actions that can improve appearance.
Assist the hospitalized patient in applying cosmetics before receiving visitors, as appropriate.
Facilitate contact with people with similar body image changes.
Identify support groups available to the patient.
Assist the patient at risk for anorexia or bulimia in developing more realistic expectations of body image.
Use self-disclosure exercises with groups of adolescents or other distressed people about normal physical attributes.

definicao: Facilitating a person's ability to interact with others.
atividades: Encourage maximum involvement in already established relationships.

definicao: Developing an individual's ability to independently and effectively cope with the demands and challenges of everyday life.
atividades: Establish a relationship using empathy, warmth, spontaneity, organization, patience, and persistence.
Determine the patient's, family's, group's, or community's life skills learning needs.
Assess the patient's educational level.
Determine the patient's level of knowledge of the life skill.
Assess the patient's current skill level and understanding of content.
Assess the patient's learning style.
Mutually agree on goals for the life skills program.
Determine the number of sessions and the duration of the program.
Enhance motivation by setting achievable incremental goals.
Assess the patient's cognitive, psychomotor, and affective skills and deficits.

Determine the patient's ability to learn specific information (e.g., consider the patient's developmental level, physiological status, orientation, pain, fatigue, unmet basic needs, emotional state, and adaptation to illness).

Determine the patient's motivation to learn specific information (e.g., consider the patient's health beliefs, past non-adherence, negative experiences with healthcare or learning, and conflicting goals).
Choose appropriate teaching methods and strategies.
Choose appropriate educational materials.
Adapt content to the patient's cognitive, psychomotor, and affective skills and deficits.
Break down more complex skills into component steps to allow for incremental progress.
Adjust instruction to facilitate learning as appropriate.
Provide an environment conducive to learning.
Use role-playing of appropriate behaviors with scenarios that simulate real-life interpersonal interactions.
Provide positive feedback contingent on improvements in the patient's learning skills.
Use assignments to practice and improve performance of new skills in real-life situations.
Instruct on appropriate strategies to improve communication skills, if needed.
Provide assertiveness training, if needed.
Use strategies to improve the patient's self-awareness.
Provide appropriate social skills training, if needed.
Assist the patient in solving problems constructively.
Instruct the patient on how to manage conflict, if needed.
Instruct the patient in setting priorities and decision-making.
Assist the patient in values clarification.
Provide instruction on time management, if needed.
Provide instruction on diet, nutrition, and food preparation, if needed.
Instruct the patient in the use of stress management techniques, as appropriate.
Instruct the patient on how to manage disease symptoms, if appropriate.
Instruct the patient in medication management, if appropriate.
