atividades: Determine if the pregnancy was the patient's choice.
Discuss with the patient and significant other (if involved) the options regarding the pregnancy, including terminating the pregnancy, keeping the baby, or placing the baby for adoption.
Discuss factors related to unplanned pregnancy (e.g., multiple partners, substance abuse, intimate partner violence, likelihood of sexually transmitted infection).
Assist the patient in identifying support systems.
Encourage the patient to involve the support system in the decision-making process.
Support the patient and significant other in their decision about the pregnancy.
Clarify any misinformation about contraceptive use.

Refer to community agencies with services that support the patient in the pregnancy decision, as well as other health concerns (e.g., sexually transmitted infections, substance abuse, partner violence).

definicao: Management, training, and support for patients and significant others in the evaluation and treatment of infertility.
atividades: Support the couple through the infertility history and evaluation, recognizing the stress experienced in obtaining detailed history and during the long evaluation and treatment process.
Explain the types of infertility (e.g., primary or secondary; male or female).
Explain the female reproductive cycle to the patient as needed.
Assist the patient in determining the ovulation period through basal body temperature, vaginal secretion changes, mid-luteal phase serum progesterone levels, and other physiological indicators.
Prepare the patient physically and psychologically for the gynecological examination.
Explain the purpose of the procedure and the sensations the patient may experience during the procedure.
Assist the partner in completing the semen analysis and clinical investigation.
Determine the couple's understanding of the test results and recommended therapy.
Determine the degree to which partners are able and willing to participate in the complex infertility treatment.
Assist with expressions of grief and disappointment and feelings of failure.
Encourage expressions of feelings about sexuality, self-image, and self-esteem.
Assist individuals in redefining concepts of success and failure as needed.
Refer to a support group for infertile couples, as appropriate.
Assist with problem-solving to help the couple evaluate alternatives to biological parenthood.
Determine the effect of infertility on the couple's relationship.

definicao: Deliberate positioning of the patient or a body part to promote physiological and/or psychological well-being.
atividades: Use an appropriate therapeutic mattress/bed.
Provide a firm mattress.
Explain to the patient that they will be moved, as appropriate.
Encourage the patient to participate in position changes, as appropriate.
Monitor oxygenation status before and after position changes.
Pre-medicate the patient before turning, as appropriate.
Place the patient in the prescribed therapeutic position.
Incorporate the preferred sleeping position into the care plan, if not contraindicated.
Position in proper body alignment.
Immobilize or support the affected body part as appropriate.
Elevate the affected body part as appropriate.
Position to relieve dyspnea (e.g., semi-Fowler's position), as appropriate.
Provide support for edematous areas (e.g., pillow under arms and scrotal support), as appropriate.
Position to facilitate ventilation/perfusion, as appropriate.
Encourage active and passive range-of-motion exercises, as appropriate.

definicao: Placement of a patient in an appropriately selected wheelchair to enhance comfort, promote skin integrity, and promote independence.
atividades: Select an appropriate wheelchair for the patient (e.g., standard adult, semi-reclining, fully reclining, for amputees, extra-large, narrow).
Select a wheelchair with a seat at an appropriate height from the floor for the patient who can move and support their feet on the floor.
Select an appropriate cushion for the patient's needs.
Use proper body mechanics during patient positioning.
Check the patient's position in the wheelchair while sitting on the cushion and wearing appropriate footwear.
Position the pelvis in the midline and as far back on the seat as possible.
Check the leveling and alignment of the iliac crests.
Ensure there is at least a 5 to 7 cm space between the patient and the sides of the chair.
Ensure the wheelchair allows at least a 5 to 7 cm gap between the posterior knee and the seat.
Check that the footrests are at least 5 cm off the floor.
Maintain the hip angle at 100 degrees, knees at 105 degrees, and ankles at 90 degrees, with heels supported on the footrest.
Measure the distance from the padding under the elbow, add 2.5 cm, and adjust armrests to this height.
Adjust the backrest to provide necessary support, generally 10 to 15 degrees from vertical alignment.
Tilt the seat 10 degrees posteriorly.
Position the legs so they are at 20 degrees from the vertical position.
Monitor for the patient's inability to maintain correct posture in the wheelchair.
Monitor the effects of prolonged sitting (e.g., pressure injuries, skin breakdown, bruising, contractures, discomfort, incontinence, social isolation, falls).
Provide modifications or accessories for the wheelchair to correct patient problems or muscle weakness.
Provide padding and other accessories (e.g., molded cushions, padded leg and arm supports, padded trays) for patients with special needs.
Facilitate frequent small body position changes.
Determine the appropriate length of time for the patient to remain in the wheelchair based on their health status.
Instruct the patient on how to transfer from bed to wheelchair, as appropriate.
Provide a trapeze or slide board to assist with transfer, as appropriate.
Instruct the patient on how to operate a wheelchair, as appropriate.
Instruct the patient on exercises to increase upper body strength, as appropriate.

definicao: Placement of the patient or body part to promote surgical exposure while reducing or eliminating the risk of discomfort and complications.
atividades: Determine the length and type of procedure, anesthetic requirements, patient age, body weight, and medication regimen used.

Note nutritional status, presence of chronic disease, comorbidities, pre-existing pressure injuries, and risk conditions (e.g., obesity, diabetes, anemia, advanced age, bone demineralization conditions, pediatric patients).

Identify high-risk surgical situations (procedure duration over 2 hours, morbidly obese patients, vascular surgeries, sustained excessive pressure on certain body areas, cooled environment with exposure of large body surfaces).
Determine the range of motion, joint stability, or presence of prostheses or implants.
Check peripheral circulation and neurological conditions.
Check skin integrity.
Document specific risk factors that may predispose the patient to positioning-related injuries.
Check the operating table before patient transfer to ensure it is functioning, locked, and properly prepared with correct padded attachments.
Ensure all necessary equipment is clean and in good

definicao: Achieving optimal and appropriate body alignment for the patient experiencing or at risk for spinal injury or vertebral irritability.
atividades: Immobilize or support the affected body part as appropriate.
Place in the prescribed therapeutic position.
Avoid applying pressure on the affected body part.
Support the affected body part.
Provide neck support.
Use proper body mechanics during patient positioning.
Provide a firm mattress.
Place the patient on an air mattress if possible.
Leave an adapted alarm system near the patient (e.g., easy activation, voice control, straw activation, chin movement activation), depending on the level of motor function.
Maintain proper body alignment.
Position with head and neck in alignment.
Use heel immobilizers to maintain ankles in a neutral position.
Avoid positioning the patient on the bone graft donor site.
Position the head of the bed as low as possible (according to pulmonary function) to increase body surface area and decrease pressure on bony prominences.
Turn using the logrolling technique every 2 hours or more frequently as indicated.
Stabilize the spine during position changes, keeping the spine in anatomical position (e.g., without rotation).
Monitor brain tissue oxygen levels and intracranial pressure in critically ill patients during position changes, as appropriate.
Apply a cervical orthosis.
Instruct on cervical orthosis care when needed.
Monitor the patient's self-care ability while wearing a cervical orthosis or immobilizers.
Apply and maintain an immobilizer.
Monitor skin integrity under the immobilizer or orthosis.

definicao: Protection of a localized area with limited perfusion.
atividades: Perform a comprehensive assessment of peripheral circulation (i.e., check peripheral pulses, edema, capillary refill, color, extremity temperature, and ankle-brachial index, if indicated).
Pay attention to at-risk patients (e.g., diabetics, smokers, elderly, hypertensives, and those with elevated cholesterol levels) for comprehensive peripheral assessments and risk factor modification.
Do not place IV access or draw blood in the affected extremity.
Avoid taking blood pressure in the affected extremity.
Avoid applying pressure or tourniquet to the affected extremity.
Maintain adequate hydration to prevent increased blood viscosity.
Avoid injuring the affected area.
Prevent wound infection.
Instruct the patient to test bath water before entering to avoid burning the skin.
Instruct the patient on foot and nail care.
Instruct the patient and family on protection against injury to the affected area.
Encourage smoking cessation and regular physical exercise in patients with claudication.
Encourage walking to the point of claudication and slightly beyond each time to help develop collateral circulation in the lower extremities.
Instruct the patient and family on medication therapies for blood pressure control, anticoagulation, and cholesterol reduction.
Instruct the patient on avoiding beta-blockers for blood pressure control, as they cause peripheral vasoconstriction and worsen claudication.
Instruct the patient on dietary measures to improve circulation (e.g., low-saturated-fat diet and consumption of omega-3 fish oils).
Instruct diabetic patients on the need for adequate blood glucose control.
Instruct the patient on proper skin care (e.g., moisturizing dry leg skin, immediate attention to potential wounds and injuries).
Provide the patient and family with information on smoking cessation, if applicable.
Monitor the extremities for heat, redness, pain, or edema on the skin.
Instruct the patient on signs and symptoms indicating the need for emergency care (e.g., pain that does not subside with rest, wound complications, loss of sensation).
Encourage patient participation in vascular rehabilitation programs.

definicao: Minimization of the potential for iatrogenic injury to the patient related to a surgical procedure.
atividades: Check the monitor ground wire.
Prepare artificial oxygenation and mechanical ventilation equipment and materials (e.g., laryngoscopes, tubes, suction devices, masks, Magill forceps, stylets, stethoscope).
Confirm correct functioning of the equipment.
Monitor specific accessories for the required surgical position (e.g., supports, holders).
Check suction for adequate pressure and complete assembly of masks/gas cylinders, tubes, and catheters.
Remove all equipment in unsafe condition.
Confirm consent for surgery and other treatments, as appropriate.
Participate with other team members in preoperative instruction per institutional policy.
Receive the patient, establishing a trusting relationship and offering counseling.
Confirm with the patient or other relevant persons the procedure and surgical site.
Confirm that the patient identification bracelet and blood type bracelet are correct.
Ask the patient or another relevant person for their name and date of birth.
Participate in the preoperative time-out to verify the correct patient, procedure, and site per institutional policy.
Ensure documentation and communication of any allergies.
Assist in transferring the patient to the operating table with monitoring devices.
Preserve patient privacy by avoiding unnecessary exposure or chilling.

definicao: Prevention or minimization of risk factors in a patient at risk for aspiration.
atividades: Monitor level of consciousness, cough reflex, gag reflex, and swallowing ability.
Investigate dysphagia, as appropriate.
Maintain an airway.
Minimize the use of narcotics and sedatives.
Minimize the use of medications known to delay gastric emptying, as appropriate.
Monitor pulmonary status.
Monitor bowel care needs.
Position the head of the bed at 30 degrees or more (nasogastric tube feeding) to 90 degrees or as high as possible.
Maintain the head of the bed elevated for 30 to 45 minutes after feeding.
Keep the tracheal cuff inflated, as appropriate.
Keep suction equipment available.
Supervise or assist during feeding as needed.
Provide small portions of food.
Verify nasogastric or gastrostomy tube placement before initiating the diet.
Check gastric residual before initiating the diet.
Avoid initiating the diet if gastric residual is high (i.e., greater than 250 mL for nasogastric tube or greater than 100 mL for gastrostomy).
Use an infusion pump for enteral feeding rather than gravity or bolus infusion, if appropriate.
Use prokinetic agents as appropriate.
Avoid liquids or use thickening agents.
Offer foods or liquids that can form a bolus before swallowing.
Cut food into small pieces.
Request medications in elixir form.
Cut or crush tablets before administration.
Inspect the oral cavity for residual food or medication.
Provide oral care.
Suggest consultation with a speech-language pathologist, as appropriate.
Suggest a barium swallow or videofluoroscopy, as appropriate.

definicao: Prevention or minimization of potential injury presented by a patient with a confirmed seizure disorder.
atividades: Provide a low bed, as appropriate.
Accompany the patient during activities outside the unit, as appropriate.
Monitor the medication regimen.
Monitor adherence to anticonvulsant medications.
Have the patient or significant other maintain a record of medications taken and occurrence of seizure activity.
Instruct the patient not to drive.
Instruct the patient regarding medications and side effects.
Instruct the family or significant other regarding seizure first aid.
Monitor anticonvulsant drug levels, as appropriate.
Instruct the patient to carry a medication alert card.
Remove potentially dangerous objects from the environment.
Keep suction at the bedside.
Keep a bag-valve-mask at the bedside.
Keep oral or nasopharyngeal airway equipment at the bedside.

definicao: Reduction of the risk of embolism in a patient with thrombi or at risk for thrombus formation.
atividades: Obtain a detailed patient health history to determine the patient's risk level (e.g., recent surgery, bone fractures, ongoing cancer treatment, pregnancy, postpartum, immobility, paralysis, edematous extremities, chronic obstructive pulmonary disease [COPD], stroke, central venous access device, history of deep vein thrombosis [DVT] or peripheral embolism [PE], or obesity place the patient at high risk).
Implement institutional protocol for at-risk patients.
Carefully assess any reports of: recent-onset wheezing, hemoptysis, or inspiratory pain; chest, shoulder, back, or pleuritic pain; dyspnea, tachypnea, tachycardia, or syncope.
Assess for the presence of Virchow's triad: venous stasis, hypercoagulability, and trauma resulting in intimal injury.
Perform a complete pulmonary assessment.
Perform a complete assessment of peripheral circulation (i.e., check peripheral pulses, edema, capillary refill, color, presence of pain in the affected extremity, and extremity temperature).
Initiate the appropriate thromboprophylaxis regimen in at-risk patients immediately according to institutional policy and protocol.
Administer low-dose prophylactic anticoagulant and/or antiplatelet (e.g., heparin, clopidogrel, warfarin, acetylsalicylic acid, dipyridamole, dextran) according to institutional policy and protocol.
Elevate any limb suspected of being affected 20 degrees or more above heart level to increase venous return.
Apply graduated elastic compression stockings or sleeves (GECS) to reduce the risk of DVT or DVT recurrence according to institutional policy and protocol.

Maintain graduated elastic compression stockings or sleeves to prevent the development of post-thrombotic syndrome (PTS), which is precipitated by long-standing clots in the affected extremity and unsatisfactory venous flow.
Apply an intermittent pneumatic compression device according to institutional policy and protocol.
Remove graduated elastic compression stockings or sleeves and the intermittent pneumatic compression device for 15 to 20 minutes every 8 hours according to institutional policy and protocol.
Avoid antecubital intravenous access and instruct radiology and laboratory staff to limit access to antecubital veins for tests, if possible.
Administer promethazine intravenously in 25 to 50 mL of normal saline at a slow infusion rate and avoid diluting in less than 10 mL of normal saline.
Assist the patient with passive or active range-of-motion exercises, as appropriate.
Encourage the patient to flex and extend the foot and legs at least 10 times every hour.
Change the patient's position every 2 hours, encourage early mobilization or ambulation as tolerated.
Prevent damage to the vascular lumen by avoiding local pressure, trauma, infection, or sepsis.
Avoid massaging or compressing the muscles of the affected extremity.
Instruct the patient not to cross their legs and avoid sitting for long periods with legs dangling.
Instruct the patient to avoid activities that result in Valsalva maneuver (e.g., straining during defecation).
Administer medications that prevent Valsalva maneuver episodes (e.g., stool softeners, antiemetics), as appropriate.

Instruct the patient and/or family on appropriate precautions (e.g., walking; drinking plenty of fluids; avoiding alcohol; avoiding long periods of immobility, especially with legs dangling, such as during air travel or long car trips).
Instruct the patient and/or family on all low-dose prophylactic anticoagulants and/or new antiplatelet agents.

Instruct the patient to report excessive bleeding (e.g., unusual nosebleeds, hematemesis, blood in urine, gum bleeding, unexpected vaginal bleeding, heavy menstrual bleeding; bright red or dark blood in stools), unusual bruising, unusual pain or edema, blue or purple discoloration of toes, toe pain, sores or white spots in the mouth or throat).
Instruct the patient to use a medical alert bracelet.

Instruct the patient to maintain a consistent diet (e.g., consume a constant amount of dark green leafy vegetables rich in vitamin K that may interfere with anticoagulants, so that the medication dose must be adjusted to dietary intake).
Instruct the patient to take the anticoagulant at the same time each day and not to double the dose the next day if a dose is missed.

Instruct the patient to check with the healthcare provider: before taking any medication or herbal preparation, including over-the-counter products; before switching medication brands; and before discontinuing a medication.
Instruct the patient and family about graduated elastic compression stockings.
Encourage smoking cessation.

definicao: Minimizing the risk of a patient leaving the treatment environment without authorization when leaving poses a threat to their safety or that of others.
atividades: Monitor the patient's mental status (e.g., dementia, delirium, developmental disabilities, altered mental status due to brain injury or disease, psychosis, depression).

Monitor the patient for indicators of potential elopement (e.g., verbal cues, patient wandering near exits, multiple layers of clothing, packed bags, disorientation, separation anxiety, homesickness, suicidal ideation).
Clarify the patient's legal status (e.g., minor or adult and voluntary or court-ordered treatment).
Communicate the risk to other professionals.
Familiarize the patient with the environment and routine to decrease anxiety.
Limit the patient to a physically safe environment (e.g., locked doors or alarms on exits, locked windows), as appropriate.

definicao: Reduction of internal and external stimuli or stressors to minimize the risk of rebleeding before surgery or endovascular procedure to secure a ruptured aneurysm.
atividades: Place the patient in a private room.
Prescribe bed rest with bedside commode use, as appropriate.
Keep the room dark.
Reduce stimuli in the patient's environment.
Restrict television, radio, and other stimuli.
Monitor reaction to visitors.
Limit visits if indicated.
Provide information to the patient and family about the need for environmental changes and visitor limitations.
Provide sedation, if needed.
Administer pain medications as needed.
Monitor neurological status.
Notify the physician of neurological deterioration.
Monitor pulse and blood pressure.
Maintain hemodynamic parameters within prescribed limits.
Monitor intracranial pressure (ICP) and cerebral perfusion pressure (CPP) if indicated.
Monitor cerebrospinal fluid output and characteristics if indicated.
Administer stool softeners.
Avoid rectal stimulation.
Instruct the patient not to strain or perform Valsalva maneuver.
Implement seizure precautions.
Administer anticonvulsants as appropriate.

definicao: Prevention or reduction of hypermetabolic response to pharmacologic agents used during surgery.
atividades: Ask the patient about personal or family history of malignant hyperthermia, unexpected deaths from anesthetic, muscle disorder, or unexplained postoperative fever.
Refer the patient with a family history of malignant hyperthermia for further testing to determine risk (e.g., muscle contracture test, molecular genetic test).
Notify the surgical team of the patient's history or risk status.
Keep emergency equipment for malignant hyperthermia per protocol.
Review malignant hyperthermia emergency care with the team per protocol.
Monitor vital signs, including core body temperature.

Provide an anesthesia machine free of precipitating anesthetic agents for patients at risk for malignant hyperthermia or discontinue use of the anesthesia machine for the patient experiencing malignant hyperthermia.
Place a cooling water mattress under the patient at risk for malignant hyperthermia at the start of the procedure.
Use non-triggering anesthetic agents for patients at risk or experiencing malignant hyperthermia (e.g., opioids, benzodiazepines, local anesthetics, nitrous oxide, and barbiturates).

Avoid or discontinue the use of triggering agents (e.g., succinylcholine used alone or in conjunction with volatile inhalational agents such as halothane, enflurane, isoflurane, sevoflurane, or desflurane).

Monitor for signs of malignant hyperthermia (e.g., hypercapnia, hyperthermia, tachycardia, tachypnea, metabolic acidosis, arrhythmias, cyanosis, mottled skin, muscle rigidity, profuse diaphoresis, and unstable blood pressure).
Discontinue the procedure if possible.
Provide emergency treatment supplies.
Obtain blood and urine samples.

Monitor for changes in laboratory values (e.g., increased end-tidal carbon dioxide with decreased oxygen saturation, increased serum calcium, increased potassium, unexplained metabolic acidosis, hematuria, and myoglobinuria).
Monitor electrocardiogram results.
Intubate or assist with intubation if endotracheal tube is not yet

definicao: Prevention of fire-causing behaviors.
atividades: Search for incendiary material with the patient (e.g., matches, lighters) on admission and each time the patient returns to the institution (e.g., from a leave or recreational activity).
Routinely search the patient's environment for removal of flammable materials.
Establish appropriate behavioral expectations and consequences considering the patient's cognitive functioning level and self-control ability.
Communicate rules, behavioral expectations, and consequences to the patient.
Communicate the risk to other caregivers.
Conduct continuous surveillance in an environment free of flammable materials.
Provide close supervision if the patient is allowed to smoke.
Obtain a verbal agreement from the patient to avoid engaging in fire-causing activities.
Encourage expression of feelings appropriately.
Assist the patient with impulse control training as appropriate.
Increase surveillance and safety (e.g., area restriction or seclusion) if the risk of fire-setting behavior increases.

definicao: Reduction of stimuli that may induce bleeding or hemorrhage in at-risk patients.
atividades: Review the patient's history for specific risk factors (e.g., surgery, trauma, ulcers, hemophilia, impaired coagulation function, inhibition of coagulation by medication regimen).

Closely monitor the patient for signs and symptoms of internal and external bleeding (e.g., distension or edema of the affected body part, change in type or amount of drainage from a surgical drain, blood-soaked dressings, accumulation of blood under the patient).
Monitor hemoglobin and hematocrit levels before and after blood loss, as indicated.
Monitor signs and symptoms of persistent bleeding (e.g., hypotension, weak rapid pulse, cold clammy skin, rapid breathing, restlessness, decreased urine output).
Maintain careful intake and output record.
Maintain IV access as appropriate.
Monitor coagulation tests, including prothrombin time, partial thromboplastin time, fibrinogen, fibrin degradation/split products, and platelet count, as appropriate.
Monitor orthostatic vital signs.
Maintain bed rest during active bleeding.
Administer blood products (e.g., platelets and fresh frozen plasma), as appropriate.
Protect the patient from trauma that could cause bleeding.
Avoid injections (IM or SC), as appropriate.
Avoid administering medications that further compromise coagulation times (e.g., clopidogrel, heparin, warfarin, or non-steroidal anti-inflammatory drugs [NSAIDs] like acetylsalicylic acid).

Instruct the patient to avoid medications that further compromise coagulation times (e.g., clopidogrel, heparin, warfarin, or non-steroidal anti-inflammatory drugs [NSAIDs] like acetylsalicylic acid).
Instruct the ambulating patient to wear footwear.
Use a soft toothbrush or sponge for oral care.
Use an electric razor instead of blades for shaving.
Instruct the patient to avoid invasive procedures; if necessary, closely monitor for bleeding.
Coordinate scheduling of invasive procedures with platelet or fresh frozen plasma transfusions, if appropriate.
Avoid inserting objects into a bleeding orifice.
Avoid taking rectal temperatures.
Instruct the patient to avoid heavy lifting.
Administer medications (e.g., antacids), as appropriate.
Instruct the patient to increase consumption of vitamin K-rich foods (e.g., dark green leafy vegetables like spinach and kale, cauliflower, broccoli, and soy), as appropriate.
Use a therapeutic mattress to minimize skin trauma.
Instruct the patient to avoid constipation (e.g., encourage

definicao: Reducing the risk of systemic reaction to latex.
atividades: Ask the patient or other appropriate person about history of neural tube defect (e.g., spina bifida) or congenital urologic condition (e.g., bladder exstrophy).
Ask the patient or appropriate person about history of systemic reaction to natural rubber latex (e.g., facial or scleral edema, tearing, urticaria, rhinitis, and wheezing).
Ask the patient or other appropriate person about allergies to foods such as bananas, kiwi, avocado, mango, and chestnuts.
Refer the patient to an allergist for allergy testing, as appropriate.
Record allergy or risk in the patient's chart.
Place an allergy bracelet on the patient.
Place a sign indicating latex precautions.
Survey the environment and remove latex products.
Monitor the latex-free environment.
Monitor the patient for signs and symptoms of systemic reaction.
Report information to the physician, pharmacist, and other healthcare professionals as indicated.
Administer medications as appropriate.
Instruct the patient and family about risk factors for developing latex allergy.
Instruct the patient and family about signs and symptoms of a reaction.
Instruct the patient and family about latex content in household products and substitution with non-latex products, as appropriate.
Instruct the patient to wear a medical alert tag.
Instruct the patient and family about emergency treatment (e.g., epinephrine), as appropriate.
Instruct visitors about the latex-free environment (e.g., latex balloons).

definicao: Limiting the risk of laser-related injury to the patient and others.
atividades: Ensure all personnel working in a laser environment have knowledge of established laser safety, including review of safety information provided by the laser manufacturer.
Ensure all personnel operating laser equipment have received adequate training.

Determine the nominal hazard zone (e.g., the space in which the level of direct, reflected, or scattered radiation used during normal laser operation exceeds the applicable maximum permissible exposure).
Place clearly marked laser signs at all entrances to laser treatment areas when lasers are in use.
Ensure all doors and windows in the nominal hazard zone remain closed.
Ensure windows, including glass doors, are covered with a barrier that blocks transmission of the laser beam type being used, as appropriate.
Implement procedures to prevent accidental activation or misdirection of laser beams.
Restrict access to laser keys to authorized personnel.
Place lasers in standby mode when not in active use.
Place the laser foot pedal in a convenient position for the operator, with the activation mechanism identified.
Remove other pedals from the area.
Ensure the laser user is the only one to activate the equipment with the pedal.
Ensure the laser assistant has no intervening responsibilities that would require abandoning the laser without supervision during active use.
Provide easy access to the emergency shut-off switch to disable the laser in case of component failure or undesirable event.
Minimize reflective surfaces during laser surgery.
Provide appropriate lasers, fibers, filters, lenses, and fixations as appropriate.
Ensure people in the nominal hazard zone wear eye protection or filters of specific wavelength and optical density for the laser in use.
Ensure the correct laser wavelength and optical density eye protection are available at the entrance of a room where a laser is in use.
Ensure the patient's eyes and eyelids are protected from the laser beam.
Verify that instruments and supplies are laser-safe.
Verify that ointments and solutions are non-flammable.
Set up and connect the plume evacuator as appropriate.
Remove surgical smoke through the use of a smoke evacuation system in open and minimally invasive procedures to prevent occupational exposure to laser-generated air contaminants.
Consider used smoke evacuator filters, tubing, and wands potentially infectious and handle using standard precautions; dispose of as biohazardous waste.
Ensure personal respiratory protection use (e.g., N95 filtering facepiece respirator, high-filtration surgical mask) during procedures that generate surgical smoke.
Check supplies and equipment for fire extinguishing.
Ensure personnel are prepared to extinguish fires using the appropriate extinguisher by laser type, based on manufacturer suggestions.
Set up the laser per protocol.
Inspect electrical cords.
Check for laser fiber faults.
Activate the entry area control system as appropriate.
Test the laser firing.
Instruct the patient on the importance of not moving during laser use, when appropriate.
Immobilize the patient's body part as appropriate.

definicao: Assistance and support in learning experiences for a student.
atividades: Introduce students to team members and patients.
Describe the clinical focus of the unit and institution.
Communicate unit and institution goals.
Orient students to the unit and institution.
Demonstrate an accepting attitude towards the student assigned to the unit and institution.
Recognize the importance of their behavior as a role model.
Discuss the objectives of the experience, as appropriate.
Encourage open communication between staff and students.
Make recommendations for patient assignments or potential learning experiences available to students, considering course objectives and student skill level, as appropriate.
Ensure student acceptance as caregivers by patients.
Determine the student's level of knowledge and skill before delegating a task.
Provide as many opportunities as possible to practice complex skills (e.g., IV cannulation, catheter insertion, leading team meetings, staff presentations).
Assist students in using policy and procedure manuals.
Ensure students know and understand fire, disaster, and emergency procedure policies.
Assist students in locating necessary supplies.
Provide information and standards for universal precautions.
Discuss the care plan for assigned patients as appropriate.
Guide students in applying the nursing process.
Include students in care planning discussions as appropriate.
Facilitate student communication with physicians and how to report on patients.
Discuss any problems involving students with the clinical instructor as appropriate.
Offer observational experiences for activities beyond the student's skill level.
Provide feedback to the clinical instructor on student performance as appropriate.
Establish and use electronic technologies (e.g., email, text messaging, social media) to stay in contact with faculty members and students, as appropriate.
Provide constructive feedback to the student when appropriate.
Assist the student with new procedures as appropriate.
Discuss nursing practice issues with students based on specific patient situations, as appropriate.
Sign off on progress notes with students as appropriate.
Involve students in research activities as appropriate.
Support leadership experiences for students as appropriate.
Role model the development of collaborative relationships with other healthcare providers.
Inform the clinical instructor of any changes in institutional policy as appropriate.
Orient to the computerized system as appropriate.
Encourage the student to work the same days and hours as the preceptor.
Discourage the use of multiple preceptors per student.

definicao: Assistance and support to a new or transferred employee through planned orientation to a specific clinical area.
atividades: Introduce the new employee to other team members.
Describe the clinical focus of the unit/institution.
Communicate the goals of the unit/institution.
Demonstrate a welcoming attitude towards the individual hired by the unit/institution.
Discuss the objectives of the orientation period.
Provide a checklist for orientations as appropriate.
Shape the orientation to the new employee's needs.
Discuss the organization of the unit and/or other types of healthcare professionals and their specific responsibilities.
Review the skills required for the position.
Review fire and disaster plans as appropriate.
Review code blue procedures as appropriate.

definicao: Providing care to a patient immediately before surgery and determining whether required procedures and tests are documented in the clinical record.
atividades: Identify the patient's level of anxiety/fear about the surgical procedure.
Reinforce preoperative teaching information.
Explain procedures in a way the patient understands.
Complete the perioperative checklist.
Ensure the patient has been fasting.
Ensure the complete history and physical examination are recorded in the record.
Verify that the surgical consent form has been properly signed.
Ensure the surgeon has marked the surgical site with a permanent marker, as indicated.
Involve the patient in surgical site marking, as indicated.
Verify that required laboratory and diagnostic test results are in the chart.
Confirm the availability of blood transfusions, as appropriate.
Confirm ECG completion, as appropriate.
List allergies on the front of the chart.
Report concerns (e.g., abnormal laboratory or diagnostic test results, problems related to the patient's understanding of the planned procedure) to the surgeon.
Report considerations for special care, such as blindness, hearing loss, or disability, to the surgical team, as appropriate.
Determine if the patient's wishes about healthcare are known (e.g., advanced directives, organ donor cards).
Confirm the patient identification bracelet, allergy bracelet, and blood type bracelet are legible and placed correctly.
Remove jewelry and/or place tape over rings, as appropriate.
Remove nail polish, makeup, and hairpins, as appropriate.
Remove dentures, glasses, contact lenses, or other prostheses, as appropriate.
Ensure money or other valuables are secured in a safe place, as appropriate.
Administer bowel preparation medications, as appropriate.
Explain preoperative medications to be used, as appropriate.
Administer and document preoperative medications, as appropriate.
Initiate IV therapy per orders.
Send necessary medications or equipment with the patient to the operating room, as appropriate.
Insert NG tube or Foley catheter, as appropriate.
Explain tubes and equipment associated with preparatory activities.
Perform shave/hair removal, bath, enema, and/or douche, as appropriate.
Apply anti-embolism stockings, as appropriate.
Apply sequential compression device sleeves, as appropriate.
Instruct the patient to void immediately before preoperative medication administration, as appropriate.
Verify the patient is wearing appropriate attire based on institutional policy.
Support the patient with a high level of anxiety/fear.
Assist the patient onto the transport stretcher, as appropriate.
Provide time for family members to talk with the patient before transport.
Encourage parents to accompany children to the operating room, as appropriate.
Provide information to family members about waiting areas and visiting hours for surgical patients.
Support family members as appropriate.
Prepare the room for the patient's postoperative return.

definicao: Preparation for an effective response to bioterrorist events or disasters.
atividades: Identify potential types of chemical agents with potential for terrorism (e.g., agents affecting the system

definicao: Preparation for an effective response to a large-scale disaster.
atividades: Identify potential types of disasters for the area (e.g., weather-related, industrial, environmental).
Work with other agencies in disaster planning (e.g., Police, Fire Department, Red Cross, Salvation Army, ambulance services, social service agencies).
Develop plans for specific types of disasters (e.g., mass casualty incident, bomb, tornado, hurricane, flood, chemical spill), as appropriate.
Identify all available medical and social institutional resources in the community to respond to a disaster.
Develop a disaster notification network to alert staff.
Develop triage procedures.
Establish predetermined roles during a disaster.
Identify a meeting place to help disaster victims.
Identify alternative meeting places for healthcare professionals.
Know where disaster equipment and supplies are stored.
Perform periodic checks of equipment.
Check and replenish stock routinely.
Train healthcare professionals on disaster plan(s) routinely.
Encourage community preparedness for disaster situations.
Instruct community members on safety, self-help, and first aid measures.

Encourage community members to have a personal preparedness plan (e.g., emergency phone numbers, battery-operated radio, working flashlight, first aid kit, medical information, doctor information, people to notify in case of emergency).
Assist in preparing shelters and emergency care posts.
Conduct disaster drills annually or as appropriate.
Evaluate the performance of the disaster team after a disaster or simulated disaster drill.
Identify a mechanism for debriefing for healthcare professionals after a disaster.
Sensitize healthcare professionals to potential psychological effects (e.g., depression, sadness, fear, anger, phobias, guilt, irritability, anxiety) of a disaster.
Identify resources for post-disaster referral (e.g., rehabilitation, convalescence, counseling).
Identify post-disaster needs (e.g., ongoing disaster-related healthcare needs, epidemiological data collection, assessment of disaster cause, arrangements for prevention of recurrence).
Update disaster plans as needed.

definicao: Providing information and support to facilitate birth and enhance the ability of individuals to develop and perform the parenting role.
atividades: Teach the mother and partner(s) about the physiology of labor and delivery.

Explore the plan for labor and delivery (e.g., birth environment, who will attend the mother, who will be present, what technology will be used, who will cut the cord, feeding preferences, and discharge plan).
Instruct the mother and partner(s) on signs of labor.
Inform the mother of when to go to the hospital in preparation for delivery.
Discuss pain control options with the mother.
Instruct the mother on measures to take if the desire is to avoid episiotomy, such as perineal massage, Kegel exercises, optimal nutrition, and immediate treatment of vaginitis.
Instruct the mother on delivery options if complications arise.
Explain routine monitoring that may occur during labor and delivery.
Teach the mother and partner(s) about breathing and relaxation techniques to be used during labor and delivery.
Teach the partner(s) comfort measures for the mother during labor (e.g., back massage, back pressure, and positioning).
Prepare the partner(s) to assist the mother during labor.

definicao: Prescribing medications for a health problem.
atividades: Assess signs and symptoms of the current health problem.
Determine past health history and medication use.
Identify known allergies.
Determine the patient's/family's ability to administer medication.
Identify medications that are indicated for current problems.
Prescribe medications according to prescriptive authority and/or protocol.
Write the prescription using the medication name and including dose and administration instructions.
Write out problematic abbreviations that are easily misinterpreted (e.g., micrograms, milligrams, units).
Ensure decimal points used in doses are clearly seen using leading zeros (e.g., 0.2 versus 2).
Avoid using trailing zeros (e.g., 2 versus 2.0).
Use electronic prescribing methods if available.
Use standardized abbreviations, acronyms, and symbols.
Ensure all medication orders are written accurately, completely, and with the necessary specificity for the intended use.
Follow recommendations for initial medication doses (e.g., milligrams per kilogram of body weight, body surface area, or lowest effective dose).
Consult with the physician or pharmacist as appropriate.
Consult, in the US, the Physician's Desk Reference, and other references, if needed.
Consult pharmaceutical company representatives as appropriate.
Teach the patient and/or family the method of medication administration, as appropriate.
Teach the patient and/or family members the expected action and side effects of the medication.
Provide alternatives for administration time and modality of self-administered medications to minimize lifestyle effects.
Instruct the patient and family on how to fill the prescription, if needed.
Instruct the patient/family on when to seek additional help.
Monitor therapeutic and adverse effects of the medication, as appropriate.

Maintain knowledge of medications used in practice, including indications for use, precautions, adverse effects, toxic effects, and dosage information, as required by prescriptive authority standards and regulations.

definicao: Ordering a diagnostic test to identify or monitor a health problem.
atividades: Assess the signs and symptoms of the current health problem.
Consider the status of existing chronic health conditions.
Review past medical history, medications, allergies, and previous diagnostic tests related to the current condition.
Assess the diagnostic utility of the test to answer the specific clinical question (e.g., understand the sensitivity and specificity of a diagnostic test for the presenting condition).
Consult evidence-based practice guidelines, specialists, and other healthcare professionals as appropriate.
Inform the patient or family of the reasons for the proposed test, including how the information should benefit medical decision-making or ongoing monitoring.
Allow the patient and family to discuss and question the test.
Provide alternatives for the diagnostic test as appropriate.
Consider the availability and cost of diagnostic tests and include the patient and family in the discussion.
Instruct the patient and family on what to expect from the diagnostic test.
Have a system to ensure that test results are received on the expected date.
Identify a method to ensure accurate communication of the date, time, and location of the test to the patient or caregiver.
Instruct the patient and family on the date, time, and location and how to receive test results.
Employ a system that allows for timely return of diagnostic test results and ensures that lost or delayed results will be recorded and delivered.
Monitor for adverse effects of the diagnostic test.

Stay current on diagnostic tests used in practice (e.g., sensitivity or specificity, indications, alternatives, practice standards, evidence-based practice, adverse effects, monitoring, and regulations and legal aspects).

definicao: Prescribing non-pharmacological treatment for a health problem.
atividades: Determine the signs and symptoms of the current health problem.
Review past medical history, medications, allergies, and diagnostic tests already performed for the current condition.
Review past and current therapeutic treatments attempted for the health problem, including reasons for treatment discontinuation.
Document the effects of other treatments on the health problem.
Identify non-pharmacological treatments (e.g., exercise, diet, physiotherapy, occupational therapy, heat and cold treatments) that are indicated for current health problems.
Consult accepted evidence-based practice guidelines, specialists, and other healthcare professionals as appropriate.
Consider the availability and cost of the recommended treatment and include the patient and family in the discussion.
Instruct the patient and family members about the reasons for the proposed treatment, expected outcomes, and duration of treatment.
Allow the patient and family to question and discuss the diagnosis and treatment and offer alternatives.
Refer to the appropriate healthcare professional.
Monitor for adverse effects of the treatment.
Ensure follow-up to assess response to treatment.
Stay current on treatments commonly used in practice, including reason, alternatives, standards of care, evidence-based practice, adverse effects, monitoring, and local regulations or legal policies.

definicao: Keeping company, both physically and psychologically, during periods of need.
atividades: Demonstrate an attitude of acceptance.
Verbally communicate empathy or understanding of the patient's experiences.
Be sensitive to the patient's traditions and beliefs.
Establish a trusting and positive relationship.
Listen to the patient's concerns.
Use silence as appropriate.
Touch the patient to express concern as appropriate.
Be physically available as a helping link.
Remain physically present without expecting interaction responses.
Provide privacy for the patient and family as needed.
Offer to stay with the patient during initial interactions with others on the unit.
Help the patient understand that you are available, but do not reinforce dependent behaviors.
Stay with the patient to promote safety and reduce fear.
Reinforce and assist parents in their support role with their children.
Stay with the patient and provide reassurance of confidence and safety during periods of anxiety.
Offer to contact other people who can provide support (e.g., priest/rabbi), as appropriate.

definicao: Providing information, counseling, and treatment that favor reproductive health and the ability to conceive.
atividades: Discuss factors related to infertility (e.g., maternal age over 35, sexually transmitted infections, chemotherapy, and radiation).
Encourage conception before age 35, as appropriate.
Instruct the patient on how to prevent sexually transmitted infections.
Inform the patient about signs and symptoms of sexually transmitted infections and the importance of early and aggressive treatment.
Perform a gynecological examination, as appropriate.
Obtain cervical cultures, as appropriate.
Prescribe treatment as indicated for sexually transmitted infection or vaginal infection.
Counsel the patient to seek evaluation and treatment for sexually transmitted infection if the partner exhibits symptoms even if the patient has no symptoms.
Counsel the patient on the need for treatment of the partner's sexually transmitted infection if the culture is positive.
Report positive cultures for sexually transmitted infection as required by law.
Discuss the effects of different contraceptive methods on future fertility.
Counsel the patient on contraceptive use.
Counsel the patient to avoid using intrauterine contraceptive devices.
Inform patients about occupational and environmental risks to fertility (e.g., radiation, chemicals, stress, infections, other environmental factors, and shift work).
Inform the patient about more conservative options that are likely to preserve fertility when gynecological or abdominal surgery is indicated.
Refer the patient for a thorough physical examination to investigate health problems affecting fertility (e.g., amenorrhea, diabetes, endometriosis, and thyroid disease).
Encourage early and aggressive treatment of endometriosis.
Review lifestyle habits that may alter fertility (e.g., smoking, substance abuse, alcohol consumption, nutrition, exercise, and sexual behavior).
Refer to a wellness or lifestyle modification program as appropriate.
Inform patients about the effects of alcohol, tobacco, other substances, and other factors on sperm production and male sexual function.
Refer the patient with a history indicative of a possible fertility disorder for early diagnosis and treatment.

definicao: Instituting special precautions in a patient at risk for injury from a fall.
atividades: Identify the patient's cognitive or physical deficits that may increase the potential for falls in a specific environment.
Identify behaviors and factors affecting fall risk.
Review fall history with the patient and family.
Identify environmental characteristics that may increase the potential for falls (e.g., slippery floors and open stairs).
Monitor gait, balance, and level of fatigue with ambulation.
Ask the patient to be aware of balance perception, as appropriate.
Share observations about gait and movement with the patient.
Suggest gait changes to the patient.
Train the patient to adapt to suggested gait modifications.
Assist the unsteady individual with ambulation.
Provide assistive devices (e.g., cane and walker) for stable gait.
Encourage the patient to use a cane or walker as appropriate.
Instruct the patient on the use of a cane or walker as appropriate.
Keep assistive devices in good working condition.
Lock wheelchair, bed, or stretcher wheels during patient transfer.
Place items within the patient's reach.
Instruct the patient to ask for help with movement as appropriate.
Teach the patient how to fall to minimize injury.
Post reminders to remind the patient to ask for help getting out of bed, as appropriate.
Monitor transfer ability from bed to chair and vice versa.
Use appropriate techniques to transfer the patient from and to the wheelchair, bed, toilet.
Provide an elevated toilet seat for easy transfer.
Provide chairs of appropriate height with backs and arms for easy transfer.
Provide a mattress with firm edges to facilitate transfer.
Use side rails of appropriate length and height to prevent falls from bed, as needed.
Place the mechanical bed in the lowest position.
Provide a sleeping surface close to the floor as needed.
Provide a bean bag chair to limit mobility, as appropriate.
Place a foam support on the chair seat to prevent the patient from getting up, as appropriate.
Use a partially filled water mattress in bed to limit mobility, as appropriate.
Provide the dependent patient with a means to call for help (e.g., call bell or light) when the caregiver is not present.
Respond to the call immediately.
Assist with hygiene at frequent, scheduled intervals.
Use a bed alarm to alert the caregiver that the individual is getting out of bed, as appropriate.
Mark doorways and edges of steps as needed.
Remove furniture at floor level (e.g., footrests and tables) that pose a tripping hazard.
Avoid clutter on the floor.
Provide adequate lighting to increase visibility.
Provide a bedside lamp.
Provide visible handrails and grab bars.
Place gates at open entrances leading to stairs.
Provide non-slip and non-skid flooring.
Provide non-slip flooring in the bathtub or shower.
Provide wide, non-slip, sturdy steps to facilitate access.
Provide easily accessible storage areas.
Provide heavy furniture that does not move if used for support.
Orient the patient to the 'physical layout' of the room.
Avoid unnecessary rearrangement of the physical environment.
Ensure the patient wears properly sized, securely fastened footwear with non-skid soles.
Instruct the patient to wear prescribed glasses, as appropriate, when out of bed.
Instruct family members about risk factors contributing to falls and how to decrease such risks.
Suggest home adaptations to increase safety.
Instruct the family on the importance of handrails on stairs, in the bathroom, and in hallways.
Assist the family in identifying and modifying home hazards.
Suggest safe footwear.
Instruct the patient to avoid ice and other slippery outdoor surfaces.
Develop ways for the patient to safely participate in leisure activities.
Institute a physical exercise program that includes walking.
Post signs to alert staff that the patient is at high risk for falls.
Collaborate with other healthcare team members to minimize side effects of medications that contribute to falls (e.g., orthostatic hypotension and unsteady gait).
Provide close supervision and/or restraint device (e.g., infant seat with safety belt) when placing infants/young children on elevated surfaces (e.g., table, high chair).
Remove objects that provide the child with support to climb to elevated surfaces.
Keep crib side rails raised when the caregiver is not present, as appropriate.
Provide a bubble top cover for cribs of patients from

definicao: Prevention and early detection of dry eye in an at-risk individual.
atividades: Monitor signs and symptoms of dry eye (e.g., hyperemia, burning, itching, drainage, eye pain, difficulty opening eyes when walking and moving eyelids, blurred vision).

Identify personal characteristics (e.g., age, gender, hormones, autoimmune diseases, chemical burns) and environmental factors (e.g., dry air, air conditioning, sunlight) that may increase the potential for dry eye.
Monitor the blink reflex.
Identify eyelid position.
Monitor tear volume using tear test strips.
Investigate corneal epithelial damage using a standard test.
Monitor the patient's ability to insert, remove, and clean contact lenses, as appropriate.
Instruct the patient to avoid prolonged reading and computer use.
Ensure the endotracheal fixation is not too tight in patients ventilated via an endotracheal tube.
Monitor ventilator mode and pressure in patients on mechanical ventilation.

Identify the frequency and type of care according to eyelid position in lagophthalmos (e.g., in cases of comatose patients, under deep sedation and neuromuscular blockade, facial paralysis, Bell's palsy, thyrotoxic exophthalmos, paralytic ectropion).
Perform eye care at least twice daily, as appropriate.
Apply lubricants (e.g., drops, ointments) to assist with tear production, as appropriate.
Cover eyes with effective devices (e.g., cover and polyethylene, polyacrylamide gel, hypoallergenic tape), as appropriate.
Ensure eyelids are closed.
Prepare the patient for tarsorrhaphy to protect the cornea.
Inspect the ocular surface and cornea for effects of care and prophylactic treatment.
Report abnormal signs and symptoms to the physician.

definicao: Prevention of a self-imposed, controlling religious lifestyle.
atividades: Identify individuals at risk for excessive dependence on religions, religious leaders, and/or religious practices.
Examine religious practices in terms of balanced relationships and beliefs.
Explore and encourage behaviors that contribute to faith growth and development.
Explore with individuals the elements of religious fanaticism and freedom for religious formation.
Explain how people who feel ashamed are vulnerable to adherence to religious fanaticism.
Use gratitude and forgiveness as ways of defending against fanaticism formation processes.
Offer to pray for healthy and positive relationships with oneself, God/Higher Power, and others, as appropriate.
Instruct individuals on the process of faith development.
Instruct individuals on the dangers of using religion to control other people.
Promote the formation of self-help groups or support groups to explore religious balance.
Identify and share resources for groups and professional counseling services within the community.

definicao: Decreasing the risk of sports-related injury in young athletes.
atividades: Encourage general physical fitness as a prerequisite to sports participation.
Encourage modification of game rules according to participant age and ability.
Inform parents about the differences between recreational sports and organized competitive sports.
Assist the athlete in finding a sport that fits well with interests and abilities, and that promotes the development of lifelong conditioning behaviors.
Assist parents and athletes in setting realistic goals for participation.
Provide resources for parents, athletes, and coaches regarding the psychosocial aspects of sports involvement.
Encourage appropriate matching of competitors by age, weight, and physical maturity stage.
Monitor adherence to recommended training guidelines and correct biomechanics.
Monitor compliance with safety regulations.
Monitor the playing field for conditions for safe play.
Monitor the adequacy of use and safety conditions of equipment.
Encourage appropriate supervision for training, recreation, and competitive events.
Monitor sports physical examinations to ensure they are completed before participation.
Encourage the adoption of warm-up and cool-down activities for injury prevention.
Use certified athletic trainers for competitive sports in the first and last year of high school.
Ensure medical coverage at sports competition events as appropriate.
Develop an emergency plan for serious injury cases.
Coordinate pre-season seminars for athletes, families, and coaches to increase awareness of injury prevention.

definicao: Prevention of pressure injury for an individual at high risk for developing them.
atividades: Use a recognized risk assessment tool to monitor the individual's risk factors (e.g., Braden Scale).
Use methods of measuring skin temperature to determine the risk of pressure injury according to institutional protocol.
Encourage the individual not to smoke and to avoid alcohol use.
Document any previous incidence of pressure injury formation.
Document weight and weight changes.
Document skin status on admission and daily.
Intensively monitor reddened areas.
Remove excessive skin moisture resulting from perspiration, wound drainage, and fecal and urinary incontinence.
Apply protective barriers, such as creams or moisture-absorbing materials, to remove excess moisture, as appropriate.
Modify positioning every 1 to 2 hours.
Carefully change the patient's position (avoid shearing forces) to prevent injury to fragile skin.
Place the repositioning schedule at the bedside, as appropriate.
Inspect the skin over bony prominences and other pressure points at least once daily during repositioning.
Avoid massaging over bony prominences.
Position with pillows to lift pressure points from the bed.
Keep linens clean, dry, and wrinkle-free.
Make the bed with toe pleats.
Use specialized beds and mattresses, as appropriate.
Use equipment in the bed that protects the patient.
Avoid 'doughnut' type equipment on the sacral area.
Moisturize dry, intact skin.
Avoid hot water and use mild soap during baths.
Monitor for sources of pressure and friction.
Apply heel and elbow protectors, as appropriate.
Facilitate small, frequent position changes.
Provide a trapeze to assist with frequent position changes.
Monitor the individual's mobility and activity.
Ensure adequate dietary intake, especially of protein, B and C vitamins, iron, and calories, using supplements as appropriate.
Assist the individual in maintaining a healthy weight.
Instruct the family or caregiver on signs of skin injury, as appropriate.

definicao: Detection and treatment of a patient at risk for impending shock.
atividades: Monitor for early compensatory responses to shock (e.g., normal blood pressure, narrowed pulse pressure, mild orthostatic hypotension [15 to 25 mmHg], mild capillary refill delay, pale/cold or flushed skin, mild tachypnea, nausea and vomiting, increased thirst, or weakness).
Monitor early signs of systemic inflammatory response syndrome (e.g., elevated temperature, tachycardia, tachypnea, hypocarbia, leukocytosis or leukopenia).

Monitor early signs of allergic reactions (e.g., rhinitis, sneezing, stridor, dyspnea, pruritus, urticaria and papules, cutaneous angioedema, gastrointestinal upset, abdominal pain, diarrhea, anxiety, and agitation).

Monitor early signs of cardiac compromise (e.g., declining cardiac output and urine output, rising systemic vascular resistance and pulmonary capillary pressure, pulmonary crackles, S3 and S4 heart sounds, and tachycardia).
Monitor possible sources of fluid loss (e.g., chest tube, wound, and nasogastric drainage; diarrhea; vomiting; increasing abdominal and limb circumference, hematemesis, or melena).
Monitor circulatory status (e.g., blood pressure, skin color, skin temperature, heart sounds, heart rate and rhythm, presence and quality of peripheral pulses, and capillary refill).
Monitor signs of inadequate tissue oxygenation (e.g., apprehension, increased anxiety, mental status changes, agitation, oliguria, and cold, mottled periphery).

intervencao: Monitor Daily Weight, Intake, and Output
Monitor Laboratory Values, Especially Hemoglobin and Hematocrit Levels, Coagulation Profile, Arterial Blood Gases, Lactate Levels, Electrolyte Levels, Cultures, and Biochemical Profile
Monitor Invasive Hemodynamic Parameters (e.g., Central Venous Pressure, Mean Arterial Pressure, and Central/Mixed Venous Oxygen Saturation), as Appropriate
Monitor Sublingual CO2 or Gastric Tonometry, as Appropriate
Observe for Bruising, Petechiae, and Mucous Membrane Condition
Observe Color, Amount, and Frequency of Stools, Vomitus, and Nasogastric Drainage
Test Urine for Blood and Protein, as Appropriate
Monitor Signs/Symptoms of Ascites and Abdominal or Back Pain
Position the Patient Supine with Legs Elevated (Volume, Vasogenic) or Supine with Head and Shoulders Elevated (Cardiogenic), as Appropriate
Institute and Maintain Airway Clearance, as Appropriate
Administer IV and/or Oral Fluids, as Appropriate
Insert and Maintain Large-Bore IV Access, as Appropriate
Administer IV Fluid Challenge with Concomitant Monitoring of Hemodynamic Pressures and Urine Output, as Appropriate
Administer Antiarrhythmics, Diuretics, and/or Vasopressors, as Appropriate
Administer Packed Red Blood Cells, Fresh Frozen Plasma, and/or Platelets, as Appropriate
Initiate Early Administration of Antimicrobial Agents and Monitor Closely for Effectiveness, as Appropriate
Administer Oxygen and/or Mechanical Ventilation, as Appropriate
Administer Anti-inflammatory Agents and/or Bronchodilators, as Appropriate
Monitor Blood Glucose and Administer Insulin Therapy, as Appropriate
Administer Epinephrine IV, Intraosseous, or Endotracheal, as Appropriate
Teach the Patient to Avoid Known Allergens and Show How to Use an Anaphylaxis Kit, as Appropriate
Perform Skin Testing to Determine Causal Agents of Anaphylaxis and/or Allergic Reactions, as Appropriate
Alert Patients at Risk for Severe Allergic Reactions to the Need for Desensitization Therapy
Alert At-Risk Patients to Wear or Carry Medical Alert Information
Instruct the Patient and/or Family about Shock Precipitating Factors
Instruct the Patient and Family about Signs/Symptoms of Impending Shock
Instruct the Patient and Family about Steps to Follow upon Onset of Shock Symptoms

definicao: Reducing the risk of self-inflicted harm with the intent to end one's life.
atividades: Determine the presence and degree of suicide risk.
Determine if the patient has means to carry out suicide plans.
Consider hospitalization of the patient with severe suicidal behavior risk.

Treat and manage psychiatric illnesses or symptoms that may place the patient at risk for suicide (e.g., mood disorder, hallucinations, delusional ideas, panic, substance abuse, grief, personality disorder, organic impairment, crisis).
Administer medications to reduce anxiety, agitation, or psychosis and stabilize mood, as appropriate.
Advocate for issues related to quality of life and pain control.
Check the patient's mouth after medication administration to ensure they did not hide it for later overdose attempt.
Provide small quantities of prescribed medications that may be lethal to at-risk individuals, thus reducing the opportunity for suicide, as appropriate.
Monitor for side effects and desired outcomes of medications.
Involve the patient in planning their own treatment, as appropriate.
Instruct the patient on coping strategies (e.g., assertiveness training, impulse control, and progressive muscle relaxation), as appropriate.
Establish a contract (verbal or written) with the patient of 'no self-harm' for a specific period, renewing it at specific intervals, as appropriate.
Implement necessary actions to reduce immediate individual distress when negotiating a no-self-harm or safety contract.
Identify immediate safety needs when negotiating the no-self-harm or safety contract.
Assist the individual in discussing their feelings about the contract.
Observe the individual for signs of incongruence indicating a lack of commitment to fulfilling the contract.

Act to prevent the individual from harming themselves or taking their own life when the contract is against self-harm or for safety (e.g., increased observation, removal of objects that could be used for self-harm).
Interact with the patient at regular intervals to convey caring and openness and to create an opportunity to talk about feelings.
Use a direct, non-judgmental approach when discussing suicide.
Encourage the patient to seek caregivers to talk to if an urgent need to harm themselves arises.
Avoid repeated discussions about past suicide history, keeping discussions focused on the present and future.
Discuss plans to deal with suicidal thoughts in the future (e.g., precipitating factors, who to contact, where to seek help, ways to alleviate feelings of self-harm).
Assist the patient in identifying a network of support people and resources (e.g., religious, family, caregivers).
Initiate suicide precautions (e.g., observe and monitor the patient continuously, provide a protective environment) in a patient at severe risk for suicide.
Place the patient in the least restrictive environment that allows the necessary level of observation.
Continue regular assessment of suicide risk (at least

definicao: Prevention of a lifestyle involving alcohol or other substance use.
atividades: Assist the individual in tolerating higher levels of stress, as appropriate.
Prepare the individual for difficult or painful events.
Reduce environmental stress that causes irritation or frustration.
Reduce social isolation, as appropriate.
Support measures regulating the sale and distribution of alcoholic beverages to minors.
Lobby for increased legal drinking age.
Recommend responsible changes in the school curriculum on alcohol and other substances for elementary school grades.
Conduct school programs on how to avoid substances and alcoholic beverages as recreational activities.
Encourage responsible decision-making about lifestyle options.
Recommend media campaigns on substance use-related issues in the community.
Instruct parents on the importance of example in substance use.
Instruct parents and teachers on how to identify signs and symptoms of addiction.
Assist the individual in identifying tension-reduction substitution strategies.
Offer support or organize community groups to reduce alcohol-related injuries, such as SADD (Students Against Destructive Decisions) and MADD (Mothers Against Drunk Driving).
Survey middle and high school students on alcohol and other substance use and alcohol-related behaviors.
Instruct parents to support school policies that prohibit alcohol and other substance use in extracurricular activities.
Assist in organizing activities for adolescents after events such as graduation and other school events.
Facilitate coordination of efforts among various community groups concerned with substance use.
Encourage parents to participate in their children's activities from preschool to adolescence.

definicao: Providing immediate care for minor burns, wounds, poisoning, bites, and stings.
atividades: Instruct others to call for help, if needed.
Employ precautionary measures to reduce the risk of infection while providing care.
Monitor vital signs, as appropriate.
Record the characteristics of the wound or burn, including drainage, color, size, and odor.
Institute appropriate care for the wound or burn.
Institute measures (e.g., pressure, pressure dressing, positioning) to reduce or minimize bleeding.
Use rest, ice, compression, and elevation (RICE) for bone, joint, and muscle injuries in the extremities.
Apply a splint to the affected extremity.
Clean skin exposed to poison ivy, oak, or sumac (i.e., use water and soap or copious amounts of rubbing alcohol) and nettles (i.e., use water and soap).
Wash tissue exposed to chemicals (except lye or

definicao: Enhancing the individual's belief in their own ability to initiate and sustain actions.
atividades: Assist the patient and family in identifying areas of hope in life.
Inform the patient if the current situation is a temporary state.
Demonstrate hope by acknowledging the patient's intrinsic worth and viewing the patient's illness as only one facet of the individual.
Expand the patient's repertoire of coping mechanisms.
Teach reality recognition through studying the situation and making contingency plans.
Assist the patient in creating and reviewing goals related to the object of hope.
Help the patient expand spiritually.
Avoid masking the truth.
Facilitate the patient's incorporation of personal loss of body image.
Facilitate patient/family in reliving and savoring past achievements and experiences.
Emphasize maintaining relationships, such as mentioning the names of loved ones to the unresponsive patient.
Employ a life review and/or reminiscence guide as appropriate.
Actively involve the patient in their own care.
Develop a care plan involving degrees of achievable goals, moving from simple to more complex objectives.
Encourage therapeutic relationships with significant others.
Teach the family about the positive aspects of hope (e.g., developing meaningful discussions that reflect love and the patient's need).
Provide the patient/family with the opportunity to be involved with support groups.
Create an environment that facilitates the patient's religious practice, as appropriate.

definicao: Promotion of family cohesion and unity.
atividades: Be a listener for family members.
Establish trusting relationships with family members.
Determine the family's understanding of the circumstances.
Determine the family's feelings about their situation.
Assist the family in resolving unrealistic feelings of guilt or responsibility, as indicated.
Determine typical family relationships for each family.
Monitor the family's current relationships.
Identify the family's typical coping mechanisms.
Identify conflicting priorities among family members.
Assist the family in conflict resolution.
Counsel family members on effective coping skills for their own use.
Respect the individual privacy of family members.
Provide privacy for the family.
Tell family members that it is safe and acceptable to use typical expressions of affection in the hospital environment.
Facilitate a cohesive environment among family members.
Provide family members with information about the patient's condition regularly, according to patient preference.
Collaborate with the family in problem-solving and decision-making.
Encourage the family to maintain positive relationships.
Facilitate open communication among family members.
Allow patient care by family members as appropriate.
Facilitate family visits.
Refer the family to support groups with other families dealing with similar problems.
Refer for family therapy as indicated.

definicao: Facilitating the maturation of individuals or families who are adding a baby to the family unit.
atividades: Establish a trusting relationship with the parents.
Listen to family concerns, feelings, and questions.
Respect and support the family's cultural value system.
Identify family interaction patterns.
Assist the family in identifying strengths and weaknesses.
Identify the family's normal coping mechanisms.
Assist the family in developing adaptive coping mechanisms to deal with the transition to parenthood.
Monitor parental adaptation to the parenting role.
Prepare parents for the role changes involved in becoming parents.
Instruct parents on the potential for role conflict and role overload.
Promote self-efficacy in performing the parenting role.
Prepare parents for parenting role responsibilities.

definicao: Facilitating the use of posture and movement in daily activities to prevent fatigue, strain, or musculoskeletal injury.
atividades: Determine the patient's commitment to learning and adopting correct posture.
Collaborate with the physical therapist in developing a body mechanics promotion plan, as indicated.
Determine the patient's understanding of body mechanics and exercises (e.g., demonstration of correct techniques when performing activities/exercises).
Instruct the patient on the structure and function of the spine and ideal posture for moving and using the body.
Instruct the patient on the need for correct posture to prevent fatigue, strain, or injury.
Instruct the patient on how to use posture and body mechanics to prevent injury when performing any physical activity.
Determine the patient's perception of their own musculoskeletal changes and the potential effects of posture and muscle tissue.
Instruct on the use of a firm mattress/chair or pillow, if appropriate.
Instruct to avoid sleeping in the prone position.
Assist in demonstrating proper sleeping positions.
Assist in avoiding sitting in the same position for prolonged periods.
Demonstrate how to shift weight from one foot to the other when standing.
Instruct the patient to move their feet first and then their body when turning to walk from a standing position.
Use body mechanics principles along with safe patient handling and assistive devices for movement.
Assist the patient/family in identifying appropriate posture exercises.
Assist the patient in selecting warm-up activities before starting non-routine exercise or tasks.
Assist the patient in performing flexion exercises to facilitate back mobility as indicated.
Instruct the patient/family on frequency and number of repetitions of each exercise.
Monitor the patient's improvement in posture/body mechanics.
Provide information on possible positional causes of muscle or joint pain.

definicao: Assisting parents and other family members of children with chronic illnesses or disabilities in providing normal life experiences for their children and families.
atividades: Assist the family in accepting the child's illness.
Promote the child's development as part of the family system without letting them become the central focus of the family.
Assist the family in seeing the child first as a child, rather than as a chronically ill or disabled individual.
Offer opportunities for the child to have normal childhood experiences.
Encourage interaction with healthy peers.
Do not emphasize the peculiarity of the child's condition.
Encourage parents to make the child look as normal as possible.
Assist the family in avoiding potentially embarrassing situations with the child.
Encourage the family to modify preconceived developmental expectations, focusing more on unexpected smaller achievements.
Assist the family in making changes to the home environment that decrease reminders of the child's special needs.
Determine the accessibility of an activity and the child's ability to participate in that activity.
Identify necessary adaptations to accommodate the child's limitations for participation in normal activities.

definicao: Providing information, support, and coordination of comprehensive services for high-risk families.
atividades: Identify and enroll high-risk families in follow-up programs.
Encourage mothers to receive early and regular prenatal care.
Visit mothers in the hospital before discharge to begin establishing a trusting relationship and schedule follow-up visits.
Make home visits according to the level of risk.
Assist parents in having realistic and developmentally appropriate expectations of the child's level and ability.
Assist parents in coping with the transition and expectations of parenthood.
Recommend male visitors to work with fathers, as appropriate.
Provide necessary anticipatory guidance at different developmental levels.
Provide handouts, books, and other materials to develop parenting skills.
Discuss strategies for adopting age-appropriate behaviors for the child.
Assist parents in identifying the newborn's temperament.
Teach parents to respond to behavioral cues exhibited by their infant.
Model and encourage parent-child interaction.
Refer parents to support groups as appropriate.
Assist parents in developing, maintaining, and using social support systems.
Listen to parents' problems and concerns non-judgmentally.
Provide positive and structured feedback on successes in parenting skills development to enhance self-esteem.
Assist parents in developing social skills.
Teach and model problem-solving skills.
Enhance problem-solving skills through examples, practice, and reinforcement.
Provide toys through toy lending centers.
Monitor the child's health status, well-child visits, and immunization status.
Monitor the parents' health status and health maintenance activities.
Provide transportation for child assessment visits or other services as needed.
Refer to community resources as appropriate.
Coordinate community agencies to work with the family.
Provide referrals for vocational training or employment agencies as needed.
