Inform parents of locations where they can receive family planning guidance.
Monitor consistent and correct use of contraceptives as appropriate.
Assist in finding childcare as needed.
Refer to respite care clinics as appropriate.
Refer to domestic violence prevention centers as needed.
Refer to substance abuse treatment as needed.
Collect and record data for follow-up and evaluation programs as indicated.

definicao: Promoting adequate perfusion and limiting complications for a patient with or at risk for inadequate cerebral perfusion.
atividades: Consult the physician to determine hemodynamic parameters.
Administer and titrate vasoactive agents as prescribed to maintain hemodynamic parameters.
Induce hypertension with volume expanders or inotropes or vasoconstrictors as prescribed to maintain hemodynamic parameters and maintain or optimize cerebral perfusion pressure (CPP).
Monitor the patient's prothrombin time (PT) and partial thromboplastin time (PTT) to maintain them one to two times normal, as appropriate.
Monitor side effects of anticoagulant therapy (e.g., test blood in stools and nasogastric tube drainage).
Administer rheologic agents (e.g., low-dose mannitol or low-molecular-weight dextran) as prescribed.
Obtain a blood sample to monitor hematocrit, electrolytes, and glucose levels.
Maintain hematocrit level for hemodilution therapy.

definicao: Assisting individuals, families, and communities in developing, using, and strengthening protective factors to be used in coping with environmental and social stressors.
atividades: Facilitate family cohesion.
Encourage family support.
Encourage the development and adherence to family routines and traditions (e.g., births, holidays).
Assist youth in seeing their families as a resource for advice and support.
Facilitate family communication.
Encourage the family to eat meals together regularly.
Connect youth with interested adults in the community.
Provide the family/community with models for conventional behavior.
Motivate youth to strive for academic achievements and goals.
Encourage family involvement with the child's schoolwork and activities.
Assist the family in providing an atmosphere conducive to learning.
Encourage the family/community to value achievements.
Encourage the family/community to value health.
Encourage positive health-seeking behaviors.
Assist parents in determining age-appropriate expectations for their children.
Encourage the family to establish rules and consequences for children's and youth's behaviors.
Assist parents in establishing norms for parental monitoring of children's friends and activities.
Assist youth in developing assertiveness skills.
Assist youth in developing decision-making skills.
Assist youth in developing friendship-making skills.
Encourage attendance of families and youth at religious services and/or activities.
Encourage youth involvement in school and/or volunteer club activities.
Create opportunities for youth involvement in community volunteer activities.
Assist youth in developing social and global awareness.
Promote quality, caring schools in the community.
Arrange for schools/gyms and libraries to remain open after business hours for activities.
Inform and involve the community in youth programs.
Facilitate the development and use of neighborhood resources.
Assist youth/families/communities in developing optimism for the future.

definicao: Promoting oral hygiene and dental care for a patient with normal oral and dental health.
atividades: Monitor the condition of the patient's mouth (e.g., lips, tongue, mucous membranes, teeth, gums, dental appliances, and their fit).
Provide oral health assessment and risk analysis.
Determine the patient's usual dental hygiene routine, identifying areas to be addressed if needed.

Instruct the patient or family on the frequency and quality of proper oral health care (e.g., flossing, brushing, and rinsing; adequate nutrition; use of fluoridated water, supplement, or other preventive product; and other considerations based on developmental level and self-care ability).
Assist the patient with brushing (teeth, gums, and tongue), rinsing, and flossing as needed.
Assist the patient using dentures with oral care (i.e., removal, cleaning, and reinsertion of dentures; brushing gums, remaining teeth, and tongue; and massaging gums with a toothbrush or fingers).

Provide oral care for the unconscious patient using appropriate precautions (i.e., turn the patient's head to the side or place on the side lying down when possible, insert a padded bite block or tongue depressor; avoid

definicao: Assisting individuals, families, and communities in increasing awareness of measures to reduce unintentional injury in motorized and non-motorized vehicles.
atividades: Determine current awareness of vehicle safety, as appropriate.
Identify the safety needs of the target audience.
Identify individuals and groups at high risk for vehicle injury.
Identify safety risks in the environment.
Eliminate safety risks in the environment when possible.
Provide information about the risks associated with the use of motorized and non-motorized vehicles, as indicated.
Instruct high-risk populations about vehicle-related risks and harms (e.g., drinking alcohol, risky behaviors, non-adherence to rules and laws).
Collaborate with community agencies in educational efforts to promote vehicle safety (e.g., schools, police, local health department, child safety coalitions).
Provide literature on the importance and methods of increasing vehicle safety.
Instruct on the rules of highway use for motorized and non-motorized vehicles.
Instruct on the importance of proper and regular use of protective equipment to decrease the risk of injury (e.g., child seats, seat belts, helmets).
Emphasize the importance of always using seat belts.
Encourage drivers not to start the vehicle before all passengers are seated and using protective equipment.
Encourage adults to role model seat belt use and safe driving practices.
Provide information on proper adjustment so that seat belts are used safely and comfortably.
Monitor the use of approved child safety seats and seat belts.
Instruct on the proper installation of child safety seats.
Instruct parents to place children in child safety seats and children under 13 years of age in the back seat of the vehicle.
Encourage parents to use child safety seats during travel (e.g., air, train, bus).
Demonstrate strategies parents can use to keep children occupied while restrained with seat belts or child safety seats.
Praise children and families for proper and regular use of vehicle safety practices.
Make child safety seats available to all families through community service agencies.
Inform parents of the importance of choosing an appropriate bicycle for the child and adjusting it appropriately as the child grows.
Encourage the use of accessories to increase vehicle safety (e.g., mirrors, horns, reflective materials, lights).
Highlight the importance of always wearing helmets and bright reflective clothing when using bicycles, motorcycles, and other motorized vehicles (e.g., off-road vehicles, snow vehicles).
Emphasize the importance of wearing protective footwear and clothing when using motorized and non-motorized vehicles.
Monitor community injury rates to determine future educational needs.
Support legislative initiatives that promote and enforce vehicle safety.

definicao: Facilitating the development of a lasting affectionate relationship between parents and the infant.
atividades: Discuss with the patient(s) cultural expressions of bonding before and after birth.
Discuss the patient(s)'s reaction to the pregnancy.
Assess the patient(s)'s image of the unborn child.
Discuss the patient(s)'s experience of hearing fetal heart sounds.
Discuss the patient(s)'s experience of seeing the baby via ultrasound.
Discuss the patient(s)'s experience of fetal movements.
Encourage the patient(s) to attend prenatal classes.
Instruct the patient's partner(s) on ways to participate during labor and delivery.
Place the newborn in skin-to-skin contact with the mother/father immediately after birth.
Provide the opportunity for parents to see, hold, and examine the newborn shortly after birth (i.e., delay unnecessary procedures and provide privacy).
Facilitate eye contact between the mother and newborn immediately

definicao: Facilitating family members' involvement in the patient's emotional and physical care.
atividades: Establish a personal relationship with the patient and family members involved in care.
Identify family members' abilities to be involved in patient care.
Create a culture of flexibility for the family.
Determine the physical, emotional, and educational resources of the primary caregiver.
Identify the patient's self-care deficits.
Identify family members' preferences for involvement with the patient.
Identify family members' expectations of the patient.
Anticipate and identify family needs.
Encourage family members and the patient to help develop a care plan, including expected outcomes and implementation of the care plan.
Encourage family members and the patient to be assertive in interactions with healthcare professionals.
Monitor family structure and roles.
Monitor family member involvement in patient care.
Encourage care by family members during hospitalization or care in a long-term care setting.
Provide crucial information to family members about the patient's status according to patient preference.
Facilitate family members' understanding of the medical aspects of the patient's condition.
Provide necessary support for the family to make informed decisions.
Identify family members' perception of the situation, precipitating events, the patient's feelings, and behaviors.
Identify other situational stressors for family members.
Identify physical symptoms in family members related to stress (e.g., easy crying, nausea, vomiting, distraction).
Determine the patient's level of dependence on family members according to age or illness.
Encourage focus on any positive aspects of the patient's situation.
Identify and respect coping mechanisms used by family members.
Identify with family members the patient's coping difficulties.
Identify with family members the patient's strengths and abilities within the family.
Inform family members of factors that may improve the patient's condition.
Encourage family members to maintain or continue family relationships as appropriate.

definicao: Facilitating regular physical activity to maintain or advance to a higher level of physical fitness.
atividades: Assess the individual's health beliefs about exercise.
Explore previous experiences with exercise.
Determine the patient's motivation to start/continue the exercise program.
Explore barriers to exercise.
Encourage verbalization of feelings about exercise or the need to exercise.
Encourage the patient to start or continue exercise.
Assist in identifying a positive role model to maintain the exercise program.
Assist the patient in developing an appropriate exercise program to meet needs.
Assist the patient in setting short- and long-term goals for the exercise program.
Assist the patient in scheduling regular times for the exercise program in their weekly routine.
Perform exercise activities with the patient as appropriate.
Include family/caregivers in planning and maintaining the exercise program.
Inform the patient about health benefits and physiological effects of exercise.
Instruct the patient on the appropriate type of exercise for their health level, in collaboration with the physician and/or exercise physiologist.
Instruct the patient on the desired frequency, duration, and intensity for the exercise program.
Monitor patient adherence to the exercise/activity program.
Assist the patient in preparing and maintaining a progress chart/record to motivate adherence to the exercise program.
Instruct the patient on conditions that warrant suspension or alteration of the exercise program.
Instruct the patient on appropriate warm-up and cool-down exercises.
Instruct the patient on techniques to avoid injury when exercising.
Instruct the patient on appropriate breathing techniques to maximize oxygen uptake during physical exercise.
Provide a reinforcement schedule to improve patient motivation (e.g., increased endurance estimate; weekly weighing).
Monitor the patient's response to the exercise program.
Provide positive feedback for the patient's efforts.

definicao: Facilitating systematic slow muscle stretching and holding to induce relaxation, prepare muscles/joints for more vigorous exercise, or increase or maintain body flexibility.
atividades: Obtain medical clearance to institute a stretching exercise plan, as needed.
Assist the patient in exploring their beliefs, motivation, and level of neuromusculoskeletal fitness.
Assist in developing realistic short- and long-term goals based on current physical capacity level and lifestyle.
Provide information on age-related changes in neuromusculoskeletal structure and the effects of disuse.
Provide information on sequence options, specific stretching activities, location, and time.
Assist in developing an exercise schedule compatible with age, physical conditions, goals, motivation, and lifestyle.

Assist in developing an exercise plan that incorporates an ordered sequence of stretching movements, increments in the duration of the movement maintenance phase, and increments in the number of repetitions for each slow maintenance stretching movement, consistent with the level of musculoskeletal capacity and presence of disease.
Instruct to start the exercise routine with muscle groups or joints that are less stiff or painful and gradually move to more restricted muscle groups or joints.
Stretch major muscle groups, including hip extensors, knee extensors, ankle plantar flexors, biceps, triceps, shoulders, back extensors, and abdominal muscles.
Instruct to extend the muscle or joint slowly to the point of full stretch (or reasonable discomfort) and hold for the specified time, slowly releasing the stretched muscles.
Instruct to avoid rapid, forced, or bouncing movement to prevent hyperstimulation of the myotatic reflex or excessive muscle pain.

Instruct ways to monitor their own adherence and progress to the schedule toward goals (e.g., increments in joint range of motion; awareness of muscle tension release; increased duration of the 'maintenance' phase and number of repetitions without pain and fatigue; increases in tolerance to vigorous exercise).
Provide illustrated and written take-home instructions for each movement component.
Follow up with return demonstrations of the exercises as needed.
Monitor adherence to technique and schedule at specified follow-up time and location.
Monitor exercise tolerance (e.g., presence of symptoms such as shortness of breath, rapid pulse, pallor, dizziness, pain, or joint or muscle edema) during exercise.
Reassess the exercise plan if symptoms of poor exercise tolerance persist after exercise interruption.
Collaborate with family members in planning, teaching, and monitoring an exercise plan.

definicao: Prevention and early detection of infection in an at-risk patient.
atividades: Monitor systemic and localized signs and symptoms of infection.
Monitor vulnerability to infection.
Review international travel history.
Monitor total granulocyte, white blood cell counts, and differential results.
Follow neutropenic precautions, as appropriate.
Limit the number of visitors, as appropriate.
Avoid close contact between pets and immunocompromised hosts.
Screen all visitors for communicable disease.
Maintain asepsis for the at-risk patient.
Maintain isolation techniques, as appropriate.
Provide appropriate skin care in edematous areas.
Inspect skin and mucous membranes for redness, excessive warmth, or drainage.
Inspect the condition of any surgical incision and wound.
Obtain cultures as needed.
Promote sufficient nutritional intake.
Encourage fluid intake, as appropriate.
Encourage rest.
Monitor change in energy level or malaise.
Encourage increased mobility and exercise, as appropriate.
Encourage deep breathing and coughing, as appropriate.
Administer an immunizing agent, as appropriate.
Instruct the patient to take antibiotics as prescribed.
Maintain rational antibiotic use.
Do not attempt antibiotic treatment for viral infections.
Teach the patient and family about the differences between viral and bacterial infections.
Teach the patient and family about signs and symptoms of infection and when to notify healthcare institutions.
Teach the patient and family members how to avoid infections.
Eliminate fresh fruits, vegetables, and pepper from the diet of patients with

definicao: Prevention and detection of disease and injury in populations at risk from environmental hazards.
atividades: Assess the environment for actual and potential risks.
Analyze the level of risk associated with the environment (e.g., lifestyle habits, work, environment, water, housing, food, waste, radiation, and violence).
Instruct at-risk populations about environmental hazards.
Monitor the incidence of disease and injury related to environmental hazards.
Maintain knowledge associated with specific environmental standards (e.g., Environmental Protection Agency [EPA] and Occupational Safety and Health Administration [OSHA] regulations).
Notify authorized agencies to protect the environment of known hazards.
Collaborate with other regulatory agencies to improve environmental safety.
Advocate for safer environmental designs, protection systems, and use of protective devices.
Support programs for disclosure of environmental risks.
Screen at-risk populations for evidence of exposure to environmental hazards.
Participate in data collection related to the incidence and prevalence of exposure to environmental hazards.

definicao: Protection of the rights of a patient, especially a minor, incapacitated, or incompetent patient, unable to make decisions.
atividades: Provide the patient with the 'Patient's Bill of Rights.'
Provide an environment for private conversations between patient, family, and healthcare professionals.
Protect patient privacy during hygiene activities, elimination, and bathing.
Determine if the patient's wishes about healthcare are known in the form of advance directives (living will, healthcare proxy).
Honor the patient's right to receive appropriate treatment for pain in acute, chronic, and terminal conditions.
Determine who is legally responsible for giving consent for treatment or research.
Work with the physician and hospital administration to honor patient and family wishes.
Avoid forcing any type of treatment.
Note religious preference.
Know the legal status of living wills.
Honor the patient's expressed wishes in a living will or healthcare proxy, as appropriate.
Honor written 'Do Not Resuscitate' orders.
Assist a dying person in resolving unfinished business.
Record in the medical record any observed fact related to the patient's mental competence to make a will.
Intervene in situations involving unsafe or inadequate care.
Be aware of mandatory reporting in the patient's record as determined by legislation.
Limit access to the patient's record to immediate healthcare professionals.
Maintain confidentiality of patient health information.

definicao: Obtaining a blood sample from a non-cannulated artery to assess oxygen and carbon dioxide levels and acid-base balance.
atividades: Maintain universal precautions.
Palpate the brachial or radial artery for a pulse.
Perform Allen's test before puncturing the radial artery.
Clean the area with an appropriate solution.
Aspirate a small amount of heparin into a syringe to coat the syringe barrel and needle lumen.
Remove all air bubbles from the syringe.
Stabilize the artery by tensing the skin.
Insert the needle directly over the pulse at a 45 to 60-degree angle.
Obtain a 3 to 5 mL blood sample.
Remove the needle when the sample is obtained.
Apply pressure for 5 to 15 minutes.
Place the cap on the syringe and place on ice immediately.
Label the sample according to institutional protocol.
Arrange transport of the sample to the laboratory.
Apply a pressure dressing over the site, as appropriate.
Record temperature, oxygen percentage, delivery method, puncture site, and circulatory assessment after puncture.
Interpret results and adjust treatment as appropriate.

definicao: Withdrawal of a venous blood sample from a non-cannulated vein.
Collection of blood and blood products from donors.
atividades: Maintain standard precautions.
Follow institutional protocol for donor screening and acceptance (e.g., substance abuse, HIV status).
Obtain donor demographic information.
Obtain written consent from the donor authorizing the collection and use of blood.
Ensure the donor has eaten 4 to 6 hours before donating blood.
Determine hemoglobin and hematocrit levels.
Check weight and vital signs before donation.
Ensure availability of emergency equipment.
Ensure the skin at the venipuncture site is free of lesions.
Maintain strict aseptic technique.
Set up the equipment.
Place the donor in a semi-sitting position during the donation process.
Cleanse the skin with iodine preparation before venipuncture according to institutional protocol.
Perform venipuncture.
Connect the blood collection tubing to the container.
Ensure blood collected in bags is mixed with anticoagulant.
Instruct the individual to elevate the arm and apply firm pressure for 2 to 3 minutes after the blood or blood product donation process is complete.
Place a pressure dressing over the venipuncture site as appropriate.
Instruct the individual to remain supine for an additional 1 to 2 minutes if they experience fainting or weakness.
Encourage the donor to remain seated for 10 to 15 minutes after donation.
Instruct the individual to eat and drink fluids shortly after donation.
Label and store blood according to institutional protocol.
Stay with the donor during and immediately after blood collection.
Instruct the individual to leave the pressure dressing on for several hours after donation.
Instruct the individual to avoid heavy meals for several hours after donation.
Instruct the individual not to smoke for 1 hour and avoid alcoholic beverages for 3 hours after donation.
Instruct the individual to increase fluid intake for 2 days after donation.
Instruct the individual to eat adequately for 2 weeks after donation.

intervencao: Vessel Puncture: Catheterized Vessel Blood Sample
definicao: Aspiration of a blood sample through a long-term vascular catheter for laboratory tests.
atividades: Set up equipment, wash hands, and put on gloves.
Stop any intravenous infusion that may contaminate the blood sample.
Remove the cap from the access connection, clean the connection with alcohol, and allow to dry.
Follow the manufacturer's instructions for obtaining a sample from a long-term catheter.
Apply the central tourniquet to the peripheral intravenous site only if necessary.
Connect the needleless adapter and vacutainer, or syringe, to the vascular access connection, open the line to the patient by adjusting the stopcock or opening clamps.
Gently aspirate blood into the appropriate tube or syringe for the specimen; discard the first amount determined by the catheter.

definicao: Insertion of a cannulated needle into a peripheral vein for the purpose of administering fluids, blood, or medications.
atividades: Verify the prescription for IV therapy.
Instruct the patient about the procedure.
Maintain aseptic technique.
Identify if the patient is allergic to medications, iodine, or tape.
Identify if the patient has a clotting problem or is using medications that may affect coagulation.
Provide emotional support, as appropriate.
Place the patient supine.
Ask parents to hold and calm the child as appropriate.
Ensure patient comfort in positioning.
Ask the patient to remain still during venipuncture.
Remove all clothing from the target extremity.
Select an appropriate vein for venipuncture, considering patient preference, past IV experience, and non-dominant hand.

Consider assessment factors when examining veins for catheter insertion (e.g., patient age, catheter purpose, catheter gauge, catheter material, proximity of catheter to joints, extremity condition, patient condition, professional skill).

Initiate IV access in the opposite arm for patients with arteriovenous fistulas or shunts or conditions that contraindicate cannulation (e.g., lymphedema, mastectomy, lymph node dissection, radiation therapy).
Choose an appropriate type of needle based on the purpose and expected duration of use.
Choose an 18-gauge needle if possible for blood administration in adults.
Apply warm compresses if needed to increase blood flow and facilitate vein visualization (e.g., dry warm towels).
Apply topical analgesia when indicated according to institutional protocol.
Apply sufficient pressure with the tourniquet to impede venous circulation but not arterial flow.
Follow time recommendations for topical analgesia effectiveness (e.g., some topical agents require 2 hours to take effect).
Apply a tourniquet 8 to 10 cm above the puncture site as appropriate.
Instruct the patient to keep the extremity lower than the heart to allow maximum blood flow to the selected site.
Massage the patient's arm from the proximal to distal extremity as appropriate.
Tap gently over the puncture area after applying the tourniquet when appropriate.
Ask the patient to open and close their hand repeatedly as needed.
Clean the area with an appropriate solution based on institutional protocol.
Administer 1 or 2% lidocaine at the insertion site according to institutional protocol.
Insert the needle according to manufacturer's instructions, using only needles with needlestick prevention features.
Determine correct placement by observing for blood in the chamber or tubing.
Remove the tourniquet as soon as possible.
Secure firmly in place.
Connect a needle or IV tubing or flush and connect to a normal saline line as appropriate and according to institutional protocol.
Apply a small transparent dressing over the IV insertion site.
Mark the IV dressing site with date, gauge, and initials according to institutional protocol.
Apply an arm board, taking care not to compromise circulation, when appropriate.
Maintain universal precautions.

definicao: Solo confinement in a fully protected environment under close nursing surveillance for safety or behavioral management purposes.
atividades: Obtain a physician's order when required by institutional policy to use a physical restraint intervention.
Designate a nursing team member to communicate with the patient and direct other teams.
Identify for the patient and significant others the behaviors that required intervention.
Explain to the patient and significant others the procedure, purpose, and period of intervention in understandable and non-punitive terms.
Explain to the patient and significant others the behaviors necessary to discontinue the intervention.
Establish with the patient (when able) a commitment to maintain behavior control.
Instruct on self-control methods as appropriate.
Assist with wearing safe clothing, as well as removal of jewelry and glasses.
Remove all items from the seclusion area that the patient could use to harm themselves or others.
Assist with nutrition, elimination, hydration, and personal hygiene needs.
Provide food and fluids in unbreakable containers.
Provide appropriate levels of supervision and surveillance to monitor the patient and enable therapeutic actions as needed.
Inform the patient of video surveillance as appropriate.
Explain the reasons for video monitoring.
Carefully consider who will be in charge of watching or monitoring the video to observe changes in the patient's condition.
Reassure the patient about safety in the seclusion area during monitoring.
Distinguish between direct visual inspection and checks performed by video monitoring, and document accordingly.
Reaffirm your presence to the patient periodically.
Administer medications if needed for anxiety or agitation.
Provide psychological comfort to the patient as needed.
Monitor the seclusion area for temperature, cleanliness, and safety.
Decrease sensory stimuli around the seclusion area.
Arrange for routine cleaning of the seclusion area.
Assess, at regular intervals, the patient's need for continued restraint intervention.
Involve the patient, when appropriate, in decision-making regarding more or less restrictive interventions.
Determine the patient's need for continued seclusion.

Record the justification for the restrictive intervention, the patient's response to the intervention, the patient's physical condition, nursing care provided throughout the intervention, and the justification for discontinuing the intervention.
Debrief with the patient and staff about the end of the restrictive intervention, the circumstances leading to its use, and any patient concerns about the intervention itself.
Institute the next level of restrictive intervention (e.g., physical restraint or area restriction) as needed.

definicao: Comparison of the patient's home medications with admission, transfer, and/or discharge orders to ensure accuracy and patient safety.
atividades: Use a standardized tool to obtain all medication information, including prescription medications, over-the-counter medications, and dietary and herbal supplements.
Obtain a complete medication history by examining medication bottles or lists, checking with the patient and family, and/or communicating with physicians and pharmacy as needed.
Document medication name, dosage, frequency, and route on a medication list.
Determine when medications were last taken.
Compare the medication list with indications and health history to ensure the list is accurate and complete.
Reconcile medications at all transition points, including admission, transfer, and discharge.
Reconcile medications with changes in the patient's condition or with medication changes.
Communicate discrepancies to the professionals who ordered them.

definicao: Reduction of apprehension, fear, foreboding, or discomfort related to an unidentified source of anticipated danger.
atividades: Use a calm, reassuring approach.
Clearly establish expectations for the patient's behavior.
Explain all procedures, including sensations likely to be experienced during the procedure.
Seek to understand the patient's perspective on the stressful situation.
Provide factual information regarding diagnosis, treatment, and prognosis.
Stay with the patient to promote safety and decrease fear.
Encourage the family to stay with the patient as appropriate.
Provide objects that symbolize safety.
Massage back/neck as appropriate.
Encourage non-competitive activities as appropriate.
Keep treatment equipment out of sight.
Listen attentively.
Reinforce behavior as appropriate.
Create an atmosphere to facilitate trust.
Encourage verbalization of feelings, perceptions, and fears.
Identify changes in anxiety level.
Provide diversion activities aimed at tension reduction.
Assist the patient in identifying situations that precipitate anxiety.
Control stimuli to patient needs as appropriate.
Support the use of appropriate defense mechanisms.
Assist the patient in articulating a realistic description of an impending event.
Determine the patient's decision-making ability.
Instruct the patient on the use of relaxation techniques.
Administer medications to reduce anxiety as appropriate.
Assess verbal and non-verbal signs of anxiety.

definicao: Prevention of flatus formation and facilitation of excess gas elimination.
atividades: Teach the patient how flatus is produced and methods of relief.
Teach the patient that passing gas 13 to 21 times per day is normal.

Instruct the patient to avoid situations that cause excessive air swallowing (e.g., chewing gum, drinking carbonated beverages, eating quickly, using straws, chewing with mouth open, talking with mouth full, loose dentures).
Instruct the patient to avoid gas-producing foods (e.g., beans, cabbage, Brussels sprouts, radishes, onions, cauliflower, cucumbers, beer, prunes, pears, apples, whole wheat products).
Discuss dairy product use.
Recommend discontinuation of artificial sweeteners found in some sugar-free items, gum, and candies.
Recommend elimination of fried and fatty foods from the diet.
Discuss temporarily cutting high-fiber foods and slowly adding them back to the diet while monitoring gas increase.
Recommend taking fiber supplements with a glass of water and drinking plenty of fluids throughout the day.
Monitor for gastric fullness, abdominal distension, cramps, and excessive gas elimination by mouth or anus.
Monitor bowel sounds.
Recommend that the patient keep a diary of foods and liquids consumed and the times of day gas is passed.
Provide appropriate exercise (e.g., ambulation).
Insert a lubricated rectal catheter into the rectum; secure with tape; insert the distal end of the catheter into a receptacle as appropriate.
Administer a laxative, suppository, or enema as appropriate.
Monitor side effects of medication administration.
Limit oral intake if the lower gastrointestinal system is inactive.
Position the patient in the left lateral decubitus position with knees flexed as appropriate.
Provide antiflatulence medication (e.g., alpha-galactosidase, simethicone, lactase) as appropriate.
Discuss the use of probiotic foods (e.g., soy sauce, yogurt, pickled vegetables) and probiotics (e.g., PreScript-Assist®, VSL#3®) to decrease abnormal intestinal gas.
Notify the physician if gas is accompanied by prolonged abdominal pain, blood in stool, weight loss, chest pain, or persistent nausea or vomiting.

definicao: Assisting the individual in preparing for and coping with a move from one environment to another.
atividades: Explore whether the individual has moved in the past.
Include the individual in the moving plans as appropriate.
Explore what is most important in the individual's life (e.g., family, friends, personal belongings).
Encourage the individual and family to discuss concerns about the move.
Explore with the individual previous strategies for coping with other problems.

definicao: Limitation of blood volume loss during a bleeding episode.
atividades: Identify the cause of bleeding.
Closely monitor the patient for bleeding.
Apply direct pressure or a pressure dressing if appropriate.
Apply an ice pack to the affected area as appropriate.
Monitor the amount and nature of blood loss.
Monitor the size and character of the hematoma if present.
Observe hemoglobin/hematocrit levels before and after blood loss.
Monitor blood pressure trends and hemodynamic parameters if available (e.g., central venous pressure and pulmonary capillary pressure).
Monitor fluid status, including intake and output.
Monitor coagulation tests, including prothrombin time (PT), partial thromboplastin time (PTT), fibrinogen, fibrin degradation/split products, and platelet count, as appropriate.
Monitor determinants of tissue oxygen delivery (e.g., PaO2, SaO2, and hemoglobin levels and cardiac output), if available.
Monitor neurological function.
Inspect for bleeding from mucous membranes, bruising after minor trauma, oozing from puncture sites, and presence of petechiae.
Monitor signs and symptoms of persistent bleeding (i.e., check all secretions for frank or occult blood).
Provide blood products for transfusion if needed.
Maintain patent IV access.
Administer blood products (e.g., platelets and fresh frozen plasma), as appropriate.
Test for blood in all excretions and observe for blood in vomitus, sputum, stools, urine, nasogastric drainage, and wound drainage, as appropriate.
Take appropriate precautions when handling blood products or components or blood-tinged secretions.
Assess the patient's psychological response to hemorrhage and perception of events.
Instruct the patient and/or family on signs of bleeding and appropriate actions (e.g., notify the nurse) if bleeding occurs.
Instruct the patient on activity limitations.
Instruct the patient and family on the severity of blood loss and appropriate actions being implemented.

definicao: Limitation of blood loss from a wound resulting from trauma, incisions, or passage of a tube or catheter.
atividades: Apply manual pressure over the bleeding or potential bleeding area.
Apply an ice pack to the affected area.
Apply a pressure dressing to the bleeding site.
Use a mechanical device (e.g., C-clamp) to apply pressure for longer periods as appropriate.
Replace or reinforce the pressure dressing as appropriate.
Monitor vital signs as appropriate.
Accurately monitor intake and output.
Elevate the bleeding extremity.
Maintain continuous bladder irrigation if appropriate.
Monitor the size and character of the hematoma if present.
Monitor pulses distal to the bleeding site.
Instruct the patient to apply pressure to the site when sneezing, coughing, and so on.
Instruct the patient on activity restrictions if appropriate.
Instruct the patient and/or family on signs of bleeding and appropriate actions (e.g., notify the nurse) if bleeding recurs.

definicao: Limitation of the amount of blood loss from the upper and lower gastrointestinal tract and related complications.
atividades: Assess the patient's psychological response to hemorrhage and perception of events.
Maintain a clear airway if needed.
Monitor determinants of tissue oxygen delivery (e.g., PaO2, SaO2, and hemoglobin levels and cardiac output), if available.
Monitor signs and symptoms of persistent bleeding (e.g., check all secretions for frank or occult blood).
Monitor fluid status, including intake and output, as appropriate.
Administer IV fluids as appropriate.
Monitor for hypovolemic shock (e.g., decreased blood pressure; rapid, thready pulse; increased respiratory rate; diaphoresis; restlessness; cold, clammy skin).

definicao: Limitation of the amount of blood loss from the nasal cavity.
atividades: Apply manual pressure above the nasal bridge.
Identify the cause of bleeding.
Monitor the amount and nature of blood loss.
Monitor the amount of bleeding into the oropharynx.
Apply an ice pack to the affected area.
Pack the nasal cavity if appropriate.
Administer blood products or components (e.g., platelets and fresh frozen plasma), as appropriate.
Observe hemoglobin/hematocrit levels before and after blood loss as indicated.
Promote stress reduction.
Provide pain relief/comfort measures.
Maintain a clear airway.
Assist the patient with oral care as appropriate.
Administer humidified oxygen as appropriate.
Monitor vital signs as appropriate.
Place the patient in semi-Fowler's position as appropriate.
Instruct the patient on activity restrictions as appropriate.
Instruct the patient to avoid traumatizing the nostrils (e.g., avoid picking, blowing, or touching the nose).
Instruct the patient and/or family on signs of bleeding and appropriate actions (e.g., notify the nurse) if bleeding recurs.

definicao: Limitation of the amount of blood loss from the uterus after delivery.
atividades: Review obstetric history and labor records for risk factors for postpartum hemorrhage (e.g., history of postpartum hemorrhage, excessive uterine distension, rapid labor, prolonged third stage, uterine infection, lacerations, retained placenta, delivery, induction, anesthesia use, pre-eclampsia, prolonged second stage, assisted delivery, cesarean section, or precipitous delivery).
Perform uterine fundal massage to ensure a firm consistency.
Increase the frequency of fundal massage until the uterus becomes firm.
Observe the episiotomy for blood loss.
Apply ice to the perineum.
Inspect the placenta for integrity and possible missing fragments.
Assess for bladder distension.
Encourage voiding or catheterize a distended bladder.
Observe lochia characteristics (color, clots, and volume).
Quantify blood loss.
Notify the primary care provider of excessive blood loss.
Request assistance from other nurses in emergency procedures and to take over newborn care.
Elevate the legs.
Initiate IV infusion with appropriate needles for blood transfusion.
Initiate a second IV access as appropriate.
Administer IV or IM oxytocics per protocol or medical order.
Monitor maternal vital signs every 15 minutes or more frequently as appropriate.
Cover the patient with warmed blankets.
Monitor the patient's color, level of consciousness, and pain.
Initiate oxygen therapy at 6 to 8 L per face mask.
Insert a Foley catheter with urine collection bag to monitor urine output as appropriate.
Request emergency laboratory tests or blood units.
Administer blood products or components as appropriate.
Identify the mother's beliefs about blood transfusions.
Assist the primary care provider with uterine tamponade, hematoma evacuation, or laceration suturing as appropriate.
Keep the patient and family informed of the clinical condition and

definicao: Limitation of the amount of blood loss from the gravid uterus during the third trimester of pregnancy.
atividades: Obtain a history of vaginal blood loss from the patient (e.g., onset, amount, frequency of pad changes, blood color, presence and location of pain, and presence of clots).

Review risk factors for late pregnancy bleeding (e.g., placental abruption, smoking, cocaine use, hypertension, diabetes, multiparity, previous cesarean section, previous and current placenta previa, infertility treatment, multiple gestations, short interpregnancy interval, age over 35).
Obtain an accurate estimate of fetal age from the reported last menstrual period, previous ultrasound dating reports, or obstetric history, if available.
Examine the perineum, clothing, linens, or pads for the amount and character of bleeding.
Weigh blood-soaked materials and clots to quantify blood loss.
Continuously monitor the character and amount of bleeding.
Monitor maternal vital signs frequently.
Initiate continuous electronic fetal monitoring.
Palpate the uterus for uterine contractions or increased uterine tone.
Monitor the electronic fetal tracing for evidence of uteroplacental insufficiency (e.g., late decelerations, decreased long-term variability, and absence of accelerations).
Monitor for membrane rupture.
Report the patient's status and any changes in the amount and frequency of bleeding as appropriate.
Initiate fetal resuscitation when there are abnormal (non-reassuring) signs of uteroplacental insufficiency.
Defer digital cervical examination until placental location has been verified by ultrasound.
Assist with fetal surveillance tests.
Perform or assist with speculum examination to visualize blood loss and cervical status.
Initiate IV access for fluid replacement.
Administer oxygen as prescribed.
Collect blood for diagnostic tests (e.g., complete blood count, coagulation tests, Rh, type and crossmatch, Kleihauer-Betke test) as prescribed.
Monitor intake and output.
Elevate the lower extremities to increase perfusion to vital organs and the fetus.
Administer blood products or components as appropriate.
Initiate safety measures (e.g., strict bed rest and lateral position).
Instruct the patient to report increases in vaginal bleeding (e.g., sudden gushes, clots, and trickling) during hospitalization.
Offer empathy, understanding, and emotional support.
Provide information about procedures, diagnostic tests, and treatments.
Instruct the woman to differentiate between old and new bleeding.
Instruct the woman to monitor fetal movement to assess fetal well-being.

Instruct the patient on lifestyle changes to reduce the chance of further bleeding (e.g., smoking cessation assistance, sexual abstinence, bed rest, constipation control, nutrition management, and coping improvement), as appropriate.
Provide discharge planning, including referral to home care nursing.
Schedule follow-up with antepartum fetal surveillance.
Discuss reasons for returning to the hospital.
Discuss the use of the emergency medical system for transport as appropriate.

definicao: Challenging the patient to alter distorted thought patterns and perceive themselves and the world more realistically.
atividades: Assist the patient in accepting that self-statements are mediators of emotional arousal.
Assist the patient in understanding that the inability to achieve desired behaviors often results from irrational self-statements.
Assist the patient in modifying irrational self-statements to make them rational.
Highlight dysfunctional thinking styles (e.g., polarized thinking, overgeneralization, magnification, personalization).
Assist the patient in labeling the painful emotion (e.g., anger, anxiety, hopelessness) they are feeling.
Assist the patient in identifying perceived stressors (e.g., situations, events, interactions with others) that contributed to their stress.
Assist the patient in identifying their own dysfunctional interpretations of perceived stressors.
Assist the patient in recognizing the irrationality of certain beliefs compared with reality.
Assist the patient in replacing dysfunctional interpretations of stressful situations, events, and interactions with reality-based interpretations.
Make statements/ask questions that challenge the patient's perception/behavior.
Make a statement that describes an alternative way of viewing the situation.
Assist the patient in identifying the belief system that affects health status.
Make use of the patient's habitual belief system to perceive the situation differently.

definicao: Promoting writing as a means of providing opportunities for reflection on and analysis of past events, experiences, thoughts, and feelings.
atividades: Discuss experiences with interventions and receptivity similar to the intervention.
Establish goals and objectives.
Explain various approaches to journaling and decide on a journaling technique (e.g., free flow, topics, or intensive journaling).
Determine a timeframe for completing the task.
Encourage writing without interruption at least three times per week for 20 minutes.
Ensure the environment is ideal for completing the task (e.g., patient is in a comfortable position, room is well-lit, patient wears glasses).
Minimize emotional, visual, auditory, olfactory, and visceral distractions.
Maintain privacy and ensure confidentiality.
Allow the person to select the media and method (e.g., pen, pencil, marker, journal, computer, recorder, etc.).
Gather all necessary supplies.
Instruct the person to date the journal entries for future reference and reflection.
Encourage writing in the order things occur without restriction on any topic.
Encourage description and narration of events in the form of stories, images, associated thoughts, and feelings.
Describe experiences considering the five senses as appropriate.
Promote deeper expression of thoughts and feelings.
Teach not to pay attention to punctuation, spelling, sentence structure, and/or grammar.
Determine the ability to continue the intervention independently in the future.
Review the journal at defined intervals.
Monitor the achievement of established goals.

definicao: Achieving or maintaining body temperature within a normal range.
atividades: Monitor temperature at least every 2 hours as appropriate.
Monitor the newborn's temperature until stable.
Institute a continuous core temperature monitoring device as appropriate.
Monitor blood pressure, pulse, and respirations as appropriate.
Monitor skin color and temperature.
Monitor and report signs and symptoms of hypothermia and hyperthermia.
Promote adequate nutrient and fluid intake.
Swaddle the infant immediately after birth to prevent heat loss.
Wrap a low birth weight infant in plastic (e.g., polyethylene, polyurethane) immediately after birth while still covered with amniotic fluid, as appropriate and per institutional protocol.
Place socks and a cap to prevent heat loss in the newborn.
Place the newborn in an incubator or under radiant heat if needed.
Maintain humidity in the incubator at 50% or more to reduce evaporative heat loss.
Pre-warm items (e.g., blankets, stuffed animals) placed next to the infant in the incubator.
Instruct the patient on how to prevent heat exhaustion and heat stroke.
Discuss the importance of thermoregulation and possible negative effects of excessive shivering, as appropriate.
Instruct the patient, particularly elderly patients, on actions to avoid hypothermia due to cold exposure.
Inform the patient about signs of heat exhaustion and appropriate emergency treatment, as appropriate.
Inform about signs of hypothermia and appropriate emergency treatment, as appropriate.
Use a heating mattress, warm blankets, and a warm environment to raise body temperature, as appropriate.
Use a cooling mattress, water-circulating blankets, tepid baths, ice or ice pack application, and intravascular cooling catheter to lower body temperature, as appropriate.
Adjust room temperature to patient needs.
Administer appropriate medication to prevent or control shivering.
Administer antipyretic medication as appropriate.

Maintain normothermia in recently deceased patients who will be organ donors by increasing ambient air temperature, using infrared lights, warm air, or a warm water blanket, or instilling IV fluids as appropriate.

definicao: Achieving or maintaining desired body temperature during the surgical event.
atividades: Identify and discuss the type of anesthesia planned for the patient with the surgical team.

Identify the patient's risk factors for body temperature abnormalities (e.g., general or major regional anesthesia, age, major trauma, burn patients, low body weight, personal or family risk for malignant hyperthermia).
Pre-warm the patient with an active warming device (e.g., forced air) for at least 15 minutes before anesthesia induction, as appropriate.
Transport the patient using the warming device (e.g., heated incubator), as appropriate.
Install and regulate an active warming device (e.g., forced air).
Adjust ambient temperature to minimize the risk of hypothermia (i.e., in addition to air warming, when large surface areas are exposed, maintain ambient temperature above 23°C).
Minimize patient exposure during surgical preparation and the procedure when possible.

definicao: Optimization of heart rate, preload, afterload, and contractility.
atividades: Perform a comprehensive assessment of hemodynamic status (e.g., check blood pressure, heart rate, pulses, jugular venous pressure, central venous pressure, right and left atrial and ventricular pressures, and pulmonary artery pressure), as appropriate.
Use multiple parameters to determine the patient's clinical status (e.g., proportional pulse pressure is considered the definitive parameter).
Monitor and document proportional pulse pressure (e.g., systolic blood pressure minus diastolic pressure divided by systolic blood pressure, resulting in a ratio or percentage).
Provide frequent physical examination in at-risk populations (e.g., patients with heart failure).
Alleviate patient anxiety by providing accurate information and correcting any misconceptions.
Instruct the patient and family about hemodynamic monitoring (e.g., medications, therapies, purposes of equipment).
Explain care goals and how progress will be measured.

Recognize the presence of early warning signs and symptoms of hemodynamic compromise (e.g., dyspnea, decreased ability to exercise, orthopnea, profound fatigue, dizziness, edema, palpitations, paroxysmal nocturnal dyspnea, sudden weight gain).
Determine volume status (i.e., Is the patient hypervolemic, hypovolemic, or at a balanced volume level?).

Monitor signs and symptoms of volume status problems (e.g., neck vein distension, elevated right internal jugular venous pressure, positive abdominal jugular reflex, edema, ascites, crackles, dyspnea, orthopnea, paroxysmal nocturnal dyspnea).
Determine perfusion status (i.e., Is the patient cold, warm, or heated?).

Monitor signs and symptoms of perfusion status problems (e.g., symptomatic hypotension; cold extremities, including arms and legs; clouded mental status or constant drowsiness; elevated serum creatinine and BUN levels; hyponatremia; narrow pulse pressure; and proportional pulse pressure of 25% or less).
Auscultate lung sounds for crackles or other adventitious sounds.
Recognize that pulmonary adventitious sounds are not the only indicators of hemodynamic problems.
Auscultate heart sounds.
Monitor and document blood pressure, heart rate, rhythm, and pulses.
Monitor pacemaker function if appropriate.
Monitor systemic and pulmonary vascular resistances as appropriate.
Monitor cardiac output and cardiac index and left ventricular stroke work index as appropriate.
Administer positive inotropic and contractility medications.
Administer antiarrhythmic medications as appropriate.
Monitor medication effects.
Monitor peripheral pulses, capillary refill, and extremity temperature and color.
Elevate the head of the bed as appropriate.
Elevate the foot of the bed as appropriate.
Monitor peripheral edema; jugular vein distension, S3 and S4 heart sounds; dyspnea; weight gain; and organ distension, especially in the lungs and liver.
Monitor pulmonary capillary, pulmonary artery occlusion pressure, central venous pressure, and right atrial pressure as appropriate.
Monitor electrolyte levels.
Maintain fluid balance by administering IV fluids or diuretics as appropriate.
Administer vasodilator and vasoconstrictor medications as appropriate.
Monitor intake and output, urine output, and patient weight as appropriate.
Evaluate the effects of hydration.
Insert a urinary catheter if appropriate.
Minimize environmental stressors.
Collaborate with the physician as indicated.

definicao: Use of a specific sequence of hand positions and symbols to channel universal life force to recharge, realign, and rebalance the human energy field.
Written and verbal reports of any event in the patient care process that is inconsistent with desired patient outcomes or routine operations of the healthcare unit.
atividades: Identify events (e.g., patient falls, blood transfusion reactions, and equipment malfunction) that compromise patient safety and thus require reporting, as defined by institutional policy.
Notify the physician to evaluate the patient as appropriate.
Notify nursing supervision as appropriate.
Document in the patient's record that the physician was notified.
Complete incident report form(s), including factual information, patient hospital number, medical diagnosis, and admission date.
Document factual information about the event in the patient's record as appropriate.
Document nursing assessment and intervention after the event.
Identify and report medical device failures that lead to patient injury as appropriate.
Maintain confidentiality of the incident report according to institutional policy.
Initiate the Medical Device Reporting System for deaths or serious injuries resulting from a medical device.
Discuss the event with the involved team to determine what corrective action, if any, is necessary.
Provide feedback to the entire team on lessons learned and organizational changes resulting from an incident report as appropriate.
Promote a culture that supports the idea that voluntary reporting of events is a form of passive surveillance for near misses and unsafe conditions.
Combine event reports with active surveillance methods (e.g., direct observation, chart audits) to identify and prioritize threats to patient safety.
Understand that a higher workload may be related to a higher number of patient safety incidents.

definicao: Facilitating the tensing and releasing of successive muscle groups while relieving the resulting differences in sensation.
atividades: Explain the purpose and process of the technique to the patient.
Instruct the patient to wear comfortable, non-restrictive clothing.
Screen for orthopedic injuries to the neck or back where hyperextension of the upper spine could increase discomfort and cause complications.

Screen for increased intracranial pressure, capillary fragility, bleeding tendencies, severe acute cardiac difficulties with hypertension, or other conditions where tensed muscles could produce greater physiological injury and modify the technique as appropriate.
Seek a calm, comfortable environment.
Dim lighting.
Take precautions against interruptions.
Ask the patient to loosen tight clothing.
Instruct the patient to sit in a reclining chair or lie on a comfortable surface.
Instruct the patient to assume a passive attitude, focusing on achieving relaxation in specific body muscles and avoiding focusing on other thoughts.
Instruct the patient to take deep breaths recruiting abdominal muscles, hold for a few seconds, and then exhale slowly.
Ask the patient to repeat deep breathing several times, asking them to imagine tension being released from the body with each exhalation.
Ask the patient to systematically tense, for 5 to 10 seconds, each of the 8 to 16 major muscle groups progressively from head to toe.
Instruct the patient to focus on sensations in the muscles when tensing.
Instruct the patient to focus on sensations in the muscles when relaxing.
Ask the patient to initiate a deep breath to tense.

definicao: Rapid administration of prescribed intravenous fluids.
atividades: Obtain and maintain large-bore IV access.

Collaborate with physicians to ensure administration of crystalloids (e.g., normal saline and lactated Ringer's) and colloids (hydroxyethyl starch with sodium chloride, 5% plasma protein fraction), as appropriate.
Administer IV fluids as prescribed.
Obtain blood samples for crossmatching as appropriate.
Administer blood products and components as prescribed.
Monitor hemodynamic response.
Monitor oxygenation status.
Monitor for fluid overload.
Monitor output of various body fluids (e.g., urine, nasogastric drainage, and chest tube drainage).
Monitor BUN, creatinine, total protein, and albumin levels.
Monitor for pulmonary edema and third spacing.

definicao: Performance of emergency measures to sustain life.
atividades: Assess for patient unresponsiveness to determine appropriate action.
Call for help if there is no breathing or breathing is normal but no response.
Trigger a code according to institutional standards.
Obtain the automated external defibrillator (AED).
Apply the AED and implement specified actions.
Ensure rapid defibrillation if appropriate.
Perform cardiopulmonary resuscitation (CPR) focusing on chest compressions in adults and compressions with breaths for children, as appropriate.
Initiate 30 chest compressions at specific rate and depth, allow full chest recoil between compressions, minimize interruptions in compressions, and avoid excessive ventilation.
Ensure the patient's airway is clear.
Perform two rescue breaths after the initial 30 chest compressions.
Minimize the interval between interruption of chest compressions and a shock if indicated.
Adapt rescue actions to the most likely cause of the arrest (e.g., cardiac or respiratory arrest).
Monitor the quality of CPR performed.
Monitor the patient's response to resuscitation efforts.
Use head tilt or jaw thrust to maintain a clear airway.
Clear oral, nasal, and tracheal secretions when possible without interfering with chest compressions, as appropriate.
Administer manual ventilation when possible and without interrupting chest compressions, as appropriate.
Request medical assistance as appropriate.
Connect the person to an electrocardiographic (ECG) monitor if needed after defibrillation.
Establish IV access and administer IV fluids as indicated.
Verify electronic equipment is functioning properly.
Provide backup equipment.
Provide appropriate medications.
Use a cardiac or apnea monitor.
Obtain an ECG.
Interpret the ECG and apply cardioversion or defibrillation as needed.
Assess changes in chest pain.
Assist with endotracheal tube (ET) insertion as indicated.
Assess breath sounds after intubation for proper ET placement.
Assist during X-ray examination after intubation.
Ensure organized post-cardiac arrest care (e.g., safe transport to an appropriate care unit).
Offer opportunities for family members present during resuscitation when in the patient's best interest.

Provide support to family members present during resuscitation (e.g., ensure a safe environment, explain and comment on the procedure, allow appropriate communication with the patient, continuously assess needs, give opportunities to reflect on resuscitation efforts after the event).
Offer opportunities for team members to engage in tasks.
Document the sequence of events.

definicao: Performance of emergency measures to support the newborn's adaptation to extrauterine life.
atividades: Prepare resuscitation equipment before birth.
Test resuscitation equipment, suction, and oxygen flow to ensure proper functioning.
Place the newborn under a radiant warmer.
Insert a laryngoscope to visualize the trachea for suctioning meconium-stained fluid, as appropriate.
Intubate with an endotracheal tube to remove meconium from the lower airways, as appropriate.
Suction again until return is free of meconium.
Use mechanical suction to remove meconium from the lower airways.
Dry with a pre-warmed blanket to remove amniotic fluid, reduce heat loss, and stimulate the newborn.
Position the neonate supine with the neck slightly extended to open the airway.
Open the airway by slightly extending the neck, placing in a 'sniffing' position.
Suction secretions from the nose and mouth with a bulb syringe.
Provide tactile stimulation by rubbing the soles of the feet or the back of the newborn.
Monitor respirations.
Monitor heart rate.
Monitor oxygen saturation by placing an oximeter on the right wrist.
Initiate positive pressure ventilation if there is apnea, gasping, or heart rate below 100 bpm.
Adjust the oxygen blender to 21% with 5 to 8 L to fill the bag and titrate based on oxygen saturations as needed.
Adjust equipment to fill properly.
Obtain a mask seal covering the chin, mouth, and nose.
Ventilate at a rate of 40 to 60 breaths per minute, using 20 to 40 cm of water for initial breaths and 15 to 20 cm of water for subsequent pressures.
Auscultate to ensure adequate ventilation.
Check heart rate after 15 to 30 seconds of ventilation.
Perform chest compressions for heart rate below 60 bpm or if above 80 bpm without increase.
Compress the sternum 1 to 2 cm using a 3:1 ratio to perform 90 compressions and 30 breaths per minute.
Check heart rate after 30 seconds of compressions.
Continue compressions until heart rate is greater than or equal to 60 bpm.
Continue ventilations until adequate spontaneous breaths begin and the child's color becomes pink.
Insert the ET tube if there is prolonged ventilation or inadequate response to mask ventilation.
Auscultate bilateral breath sounds to confirm ET tube position.
Observe chest rise without gastric distension to ensure correct positioning.
Secure the ventilation tube to the face with tape.
Insert an orogastric tube if ventilation is administered for more than 2 minutes.
Prepare medications as needed (e.g., narcotic antagonists, epinephrine, volume expanders, and sodium bicarbonate).
Administer medications as ordered.
Document time, sequences, and neonatal responses to all resuscitation steps.
Provide explanations to parents as appropriate.
Assist with neonatal transfer or transport as appropriate.

definicao: Promoting healing for a patient with oral mucosal injury or dental injury.
atividades: Monitor the condition of the patient's mouth (e.g., lips, tongue, mucous membranes, teeth, gums, and dental appliances and their fit), including the character of abnormalities (e.g., size, color, and location of internal or external lesions or inflammation and other signs of infection).
Monitor changes in taste, swallowing, voice quality, and comfort.
Obtain authorization from a healthcare professional to perform oral hygiene if applicable.
Determine the frequency needed for oral care, encouraging the patient or family to adhere to a schedule or assist with oral care as needed.
Instruct the patient to use a soft-bristled toothbrush or disposable oral sponge.
Instruct the patient on the use and appropriate type of dental floss (i.e., avoid use when there is a risk of bleeding; use waxed floss to avoid tissue trauma).
Administer mouthwash to the patient (e.g., anesthetic, effervescent, normal saline, coating, antifungal, or antibacterial action).
Administer medication (e.g., analgesics, anesthetics, antimicrobials, and anti-inflammatories) if needed.
Remove dentures, encouraging the patient to use them only for meals.
Apply lubricant to moisturize lips and oral mucosa if needed.
Discourage smoking and chewing tobacco.
Discourage alcohol consumption.

Instruct the patient or family on the frequency and quality of proper oral health care (e.g., flossing, brushing, and rinsing; adequate nutrition; use of fluoridated water, supplement, or other preventive product; and other considerations based on the patient's developmental level and self-care ability).
Instruct the patient to avoid oral hygiene products containing glycerin, alcohol, or other dehydrating agents.
Instruct the patient to keep toothbrushes and other equipment clean.

Discuss the importance of adequate nutritional consumption (i.e., explain that malnutrition is caused by lack of folic acid, zinc, iron, and B-complex vitamins; encourage consumption of protein and vitamin C-containing foods).
Discourage intake of spicy, salty, acidic, dry, rough, or hard foods.
Instruct the patient to avoid foods that cause allergic reactions (e.g., coffee, cheese, nuts, citrus fruits, gluten, and potatoes), if applicable.
Encourage the patient to increase water intake.
Instruct the patient to avoid hot foods and liquids to prevent burns and worsening irritation.
Instruct the patient on signs and symptoms of stomatitis, including when to report to the healthcare provider.
Provide referral.

definicao: Use of the least restrictive limitation to the patient's mobility to a specific area for safety or behavior control purposes.
atividades: Establish that the least restrictive measure be initiated (if a lower level was used, prove that it was actually ineffective before advancing to the next level of restraint).

Obtain an order from an independent licensed practitioner (ILP) as required for selection of measures based on institutional, state, federal, and regulatory agency policy (e.g., Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services, The Joint Commission).
Identify for the patient and significant others the behaviors that required intervention.
Explain the procedure, purpose, and period of the intervention to the patient and significant others in understandable and non-punitive terms.
Identify to the patient and significant others the appropriate behaviors necessary for termination of the intervention. Repeat as needed.
Restrict to the appropriate designated area.
Control human and environmental sensory stimuli (e.g., visiting sessions, visuals, sounds, lighting, temperature, etc.) in the designated area as needed.
Use protective devices and measures (e.g., motion detectors, alarms, fences, gates, restraints, lockable chairs, locked doors, gloves).
Provide an appropriate level of supervision/surveillance to monitor the patient and allow therapeutic actions as needed.
Administer prescribed 'as needed' medications (e.g., anxiolytics, antipsychotics, sedatives) as appropriate.
Monitor the patient's reaction to the procedure.
Provide for the patient's physical needs and safety (e.g., cardiovascular, respiratory, neurological, elimination, nutrition, and skin integrity) as appropriate.
Provide psychological comfort and safety to the patient.
Offer structured activities within the designated area as appropriate.
Provide immediate feedback on inappropriate behavior that the patient can control and that contributes to the need for continued restrictive measure.
Provide verbal reminders to remain in the designated area as needed.
Assist the patient in modifying inappropriate behaviors when possible.
Provide positive reinforcement for appropriate behavior.
Monitor the need for changes (e.g., lower/higher level measure, continue, discontinue) to the restrictive measure at regular intervals.
Involve the patient in deciding to change a restrictive measure (e.g., lower/higher level measure, continue, discontinue) when appropriate.
Promote a debriefing session (e.g., addressing behaviors leading to measures, and the patient's concerns about the intervention) with the patient and staff after the intervention ends.
Document (e.g., justification for the restrictive measure, condition

definicao: Planning and evaluation of patient care with healthcare professionals from other disciplines.
atividades: Designate a leader to plan and schedule regular and necessary patient care meetings involving the healthcare professionals central to care.
Arrange for healthcare team members to meet in person or via videoconference.

Facilitate communication and collaboration among multidisciplinary team members to ensure effective and focused discussions that enable team members to problem-solve and prepare to meet patient needs efficiently.
Summarize patient health status data pertinent to care planning for patients.
Identify current nursing diagnoses.
Establish mutually agreed-upon goals.
Involve the patient's family members in care planning when appropriate.
Describe nursing interventions being implemented.
Use appropriate clinical protocols and evidence-based practice guidelines in treatment planning and care options.
Clarify responsibilities related to implementation of the patient's care plan.
Seek input from all multidisciplinary team members involved in planning the meeting.
Describe patient and family responses to nursing interventions.
Seek information on the effectiveness of nursing interventions.
Provide data to facilitate evaluation of the patient's care plan.
Discuss progress toward desired patient outcomes.
Recommend changes to the treatment plan if needed.
Review the patient's critical pathways or care plan when needed.
Analyze discharge plans.
Discuss referrals when appropriate.
Document treatment plans in interdisciplinary progress notes.

definicao: Systematic evaluation of peer performance compared with professional practice standards.
atividades: Develop and use policies to guide the function of the peer review committee and review processes as needed.
Participate in establishing protocols and standardizing professional practice.
Participate in committee meetings as appropriate.
Coordinate evaluation processes as needed.
Observe peers during performance of service as needed for evaluation.
Focus evaluation on patient quality, safety, and satisfaction outcomes.
Consider the nurse's developmental stage (novice to expert) when conducting the evaluation.
Identify performance requiring peer support.
Provide information in areas of strengths and development needs if indicated.
Review selected peer credentials as needed.
Recommend promotion or clinical advancement as appropriate.
Provide supervision and guidance as appropriate.
Offer opportunities for feedback.
Develop shared responsibility for change or progress as needed.
Coordinate appropriate continuing education and training as needed.
Participate in complaint procedures as needed.

definicao: Providing opportunities for sucking for the infant.
atividades: Select a soft pacifier or pacifier substitute that meets standards to prevent airway obstruction.
Use a pacifier that has been cleaned or sterilized daily, used for only one patient, and has not been in contact with contaminated areas.
Place the largest pacifier the infant can tolerate on top of their tongue.
Position the infant to allow the tongue to fall to the floor of the mouth.
Position the index finger and thumb under the infant's jaw to support the sucking reflex if needed.
Move the infant's tongue rhythmically with the pacifier to encourage sucking if needed.
Gently rub the infant's face to stimulate the sucking reflex.
Offer the pacifier to encourage sucking during tube feeding and for 5 minutes after tube feeding.
Offer the pacifier to encourage sucking at least every 4 hours for infants receiving long-term hyperalimentation.

definicao: Continuous and purposeful acquisition, interpretation, and synthesis of patient data for decision-making.
atividades: Determine the patient's health risks as appropriate.
Obtain information about normal behavior and routines.
Ask the patient about their perception of their health condition.
Choose appropriate patient indices for continuous monitoring based on patient condition.

Determine areas that meet patients needing the rapid response team (e.g., changes in vital signs, low or high heart rate, low or high blood pressure, difficulty breathing, low oxygen saturation even with increased oxygen delivery, change in level of consciousness, repeated or prolonged seizures, chest pain, acute changes in mental status, or when the nurse or patient 'simply feels that something is wrong').
Activate the rapid response team when indicated per institutional protocol.
Ask the patient about recent signs, symptoms, or problems.
Establish the frequency of data collection and interpretation as indicated by the patient's status.

Monitor unstable patients or stable patients with chronic illness (e.g., patients requiring frequent neurological assessments, patients with cardiac arrhythmias, patients receiving continuous intravenous infusions of medications such as nitroglycerin or insulin).
Facilitate acquisition of diagnostic tests as appropriate.
Interpret diagnostic test results as appropriate.
Retrieve and interpret laboratory data.
Contact the physician as appropriate.
Explain diagnostic test results to the patient and family.
Involve the patient and family in monitoring activities as appropriate.
Monitor the patient's ability to perform self-care activities.
Monitor neurological status.
Monitor behavior patterns.
Monitor cognitive ability.
Monitor emotional status.
Monitor vital signs as appropriate.
Collaborate with the physician to institute invasive hemodynamic monitoring as appropriate.
Collaborate with the physician to institute intracranial pressure (ICP) monitoring as appropriate.
Monitor comfort level and act appropriately.
Monitor coping strategies used by the patient and family.
Monitor changes in sleep pattern.
Monitor oxygenation and initiate measures to promote adequate oxygenation of vital organs.
Initiate routine skin surveillance in high-risk patients.
Monitor signs and symptoms of fluid and electrolyte imbalance.
Monitor tissue perfusion as appropriate.
Monitor infection as appropriate.
Monitor nutritional status as appropriate.
Monitor gastrointestinal function as appropriate.
Monitor elimination patterns as appropriate.
Monitor bleeding tendencies in high-risk patients.
Observe the type and amount of drainage from drains and orifices and notify the physician of significant changes.
Troubleshoot equipment and systems to improve reliable patient data acquisition.
Compare current status with previous status to detect improvement and deterioration in the patient's condition.
Initiate and/or change treatment to maintain patient parameters within established limits set by the physician using established protocols.
Facilitate acquisition of interdisciplinary services (e.g., religious or audiology services) as appropriate.
Obtain medical consultation when patient data indicate the need for modification of medical therapy.
Institute appropriate treatment using validated protocols.
Prioritize actions based on patient condition.
Analyze medical orders with the patient's condition to ensure patient safety.
Obtain consultation with the appropriate healthcare professional to initiate new treatment or modify existing treatments.

Provide an environment conducive to desired patient outcomes (e.g., match nurse competence with patient care needs; provide the required patient:nurse ratio; provide adequate support staff; ensure continuity of care).

definicao: Purposeful and continuous acquisition, interpretation, and synthesis of data for community decision-making.
atividades: Identify the purpose, procedures, and reporting mechanisms for required and voluntary health data reporting systems.
Collect data related to health events, such as diseases and injuries to be reported.
Establish the frequency of data collection and analysis.
Report data using standard reporting mechanisms.
Collaborate with other agencies in data collection, analysis, and reporting.

definicao: Purposeful and continuous acquisition of patient data via electronic modalities (telephone, videoconference, email) from distant locations, plus interpretation and synthesis of these data for clinical decision-making with individuals or populations.
atividades: Determine if you are interacting with the patient or with another person, and that you have the patient's permission to interact with them.
Identify yourself by name, credentials, institution; let the caller know if the call is being recorded (e.g., for quality monitoring).
Inform the patient about the interaction process and obtain consent.
Determine the patient's health risk(s) as appropriate.
Obtain information about the patient's usual behaviors and routines.
Establish the frequency of data collection and interpretation as indicated by the patient's condition.
Monitor data entry for validity and reliability.
Interpret results of diagnostic indicators, such as vital signs, glucose data, ECGs.
Collaborate/consult medical resources as needed.
Explain test results and interventions to the patient and family.
Monitor comfort level and act appropriately.
Monitor coping strategies and actions used by the patient and family.
Initiate skin surveillance in high-risk patients when high-resolution video monitoring is in use.
Monitor potential problems based on current condition (e.g., infection, fluid and electrolyte balance, tissue perfusion, nutrition, and elimination).
Troubleshoot all equipment and system problems to improve reliable patient data acquisition.
Coordinate placement/replacement/adjustment of equipment/supplies.
Compare current status with previous status to detect improvement and/or deterioration in the patient's condition.
Initiate and/or modify medical treatment to maintain patient parameters within established limits set by the physician using established guidelines.
Facilitate acquisition of interdisciplinary services (e.g., religious services) as appropriate.
Prioritize actions based on patient condition.
Analyze medical orders with the patient's condition to ensure safety.
Obtain consultation with the appropriate healthcare professional to initiate new treatment or modify existing treatments.
Advocate for patient well-being as needed.
Maintain confidentiality, bearing in mind the specific threats to confidentiality implicit in the electronic modality in use.
Determine the need and establish time frames for additional intermittent access.
Document assessments, advice, guidance, or other information given to the patient according to specific guidelines.
Determine how the patient or family can be accessed for future surveillance as appropriate.
Document permission to return the call and identify person(s) who can receive the call.
Identify or aggregate data with implications for programs or populations.

definicao: Purposeful and continuous acquisition, interpretation, and synthesis of maternal-fetal data for treatment, observation, or admission.
atividades: Review obstetric history when available.
Determine maternal-fetal risk through patient interview.
Establish gestational age by reviewing history or calculating the estimated date of delivery (EDD) from the last menstrual cycle.
