Monitor maternal vital signs.
Monitor the woman's and support person's behavior.
Implement electronic fetal monitoring as appropriate.
Inquire about the presence and quality of fetal movements.
Monitor for signs of preterm labor (e.g., less than 4 contractions per hour, backache, cramping, vaginal discharge, and pelvic pressure in the period of 20 to 37 weeks gestation), as appropriate.

Monitor for appearance of signs of pregnancy-induced hypertension (e.g., hypertension, headache, blurred vision, nausea, vomiting, visual changes, hyperreflexia, edema, and proteinuria), as appropriate.
Monitor elimination patterns as appropriate.
Monitor for appearance of signs of urinary tract infection as appropriate.
Facilitate acquisition of diagnostic tests as appropriate.
Interpret diagnostic test results as appropriate.
Retrieve and interpret laboratory data; contact the physician as appropriate.
Explain diagnostic test results to the patient and family.
Initiate interventions for IV therapy, volume replacement, and medication administration as ordered.
Promote comfort levels and take necessary actions.
Monitor nutritional status as appropriate.
Monitor changes in sleep patterns as appropriate.
Obtain history of sexually transmitted infections and frequency of intercourse as appropriate.
Monitor uterine activity (e.g., frequency, duration, and intensity of contractions).
Perform Leopold's maneuvers to determine fetal position.

definicao: Collection and analysis of patient data to maintain skin and mucous membrane integrity.
atividades: Inspect skin and mucous membranes for redness, excessive warmth, edema, or drainage.
Observe extremities for color, warmth, swelling, pulses, texture, edema, and ulcerations.
Inspect the condition of the surgical incision as appropriate.
Use an assessment tool to identify patients at risk for skin breakdown (e.g., Braden Scale).
Monitor skin color and temperature.
Monitor skin and mucous membranes for discolored areas, bruising, and breakdown.
Monitor skin for rashes and abrasions.
Monitor for excessive dryness and moisture.
Monitor for sources of pressure and friction.
Monitor for infection, especially in edematous areas.
Inspect clothing for compression.
Record observed changes in skin or mucous membranes.
Institute measures to prevent further deterioration (e.g., overlay mattresses, repositioning schedule).
Instruct family/caregivers on signs of skin breakdown as appropriate.

definicao: Facilitating the delivery of highly qualified patient care by others.
atividades: Create a work environment that validates the importance of each employee to the organization.
Recognize the employee's area of specialty.
Ensure team members provide care within their area of practice expertise.
Ensure team members have current licensure as appropriate.
Select an appropriate management style for the work situation and employee characteristics.
Encourage open communication.
Identify opportunities for participation in decision-making.
Provide a job description to all new employees.
Provide clear expectations for job performance.
Share evaluation methods used with the employee.
Promote teamwork and a sense of purpose in the work group.
Set goals for the team when appropriate.
Consider employee growth in task assignments.
Share information about the organization and future plans.
Listen to employee concerns and suggestions.
Provide feedback on job performance at regular intervals.
Provide training and encouragement.
Reinforce good performance.
Facilitate opportunities for the employee to be among the 'winners.'
Offer recognition for behavior that supports the organization's goals.
Maintain an attitude of trust in others.
Seek recommendations from employees when appropriate.
Use informal networks to achieve goals.
Offer challenges and opportunities for employee growth.
Monitor the quality of job performance.
Monitor the quality of relationships between employees and other healthcare professionals.
Incorporate knowledge of generational differences when assigning and evaluating work.
Document employee strengths and weaknesses.
Seek information about employee concerns with patient care and the work environment.
Seek feedback from patients regarding care provided.
Encourage staff to resolve their own problems.
Initiate disciplinary action when appropriate following policies and procedures.
Counsel the employee on how to improve performance.
Establish deadlines for necessary behavior changes.
Provide re-education for performance improvement when needed.
Complete evaluation forms at appropriate time intervals.
Discuss evaluation results privately.

definicao: Facilitating the cessation of milk production, minimizing painful engorgement.
atividades: Discuss pumping options (e.g., with a hand pump, manually, and electric pumping).
Instruct the patient to express enough milk with a hand pump, manually, or electric pumping to reduce breast pressure, but not enough to empty the breast.
Assist the patient in obtaining a good quality pump for use.

definicao: Control of uterine contractions before 37 weeks gestation to prevent preterm birth.
atividades: Review history for risk factors commonly related to preterm labor (e.g., personal or family history of preterm labor or miscarriage; multifetal gestation; uterine cervical abnormalities; early cervical change; uterine irritability; infection, including periodontal disease; short interpregnancy interval; technology-induced contraception; history of first and/or second trimester vaginal bleeding; positive fetal fibronectin; extremes of pre-pregnancy body weight).
Determine fetal age based on last menstrual period, early ultrasound, uterine height measurements, date of quickening, and date of audible fetal heart tones.
Interview about the onset and duration of preterm labor symptoms.
Ask about activities prior to the onset of preterm labor.
Determine the membrane pattern.
Obtain urine and cervical cultures.
Document uterine activity using palpation as well as electronic fetal monitoring.
Obtain initial maternal weight.
Position the mother laterally to optimize placental perfusion.
Initiate oral or intravenous hydration.
Observe contraindications to tocolytic use (e.g., chorioamnionitis, pre-eclampsia, hemorrhage, fetal death, or severe intrauterine growth restriction).
Initiate subcutaneous or IV tocolytic medication per medical order or protocol if hydration does not reduce uterine activity.
Monitor maternal vital signs, fetal heart rate, and uterine activity every 15 minutes during the initiation of IV tocolysis.
Monitor side effects of tocolytic therapy, including loss of deep tendon reflexes and respiratory depression if magnesium sulfate is administered.
Educate the patient and family about normal side effects of tocolytic therapy (e.g., tremors, headaches, palpitations, anxiety, nausea, vomiting, flushing, heat).
Provide interventions to reduce discomfort of normal side effects (e.g., relaxation therapy, anxiety reduction, and touch therapy).
Educate the patient and family about abnormal effects of tocolytic therapy to report to the physician (e.g., chest pain, shortness of breath, tachycardia, or recurrent contractions).
Obtain an initial ECG as appropriate.
Monitor intake and output.
Auscultate lungs.
Administer corticosteroid therapy if indicated and prescribed to accelerate fetal lung maturity.
Determine the patient's and family's knowledge about fetal development and preterm birth, as well as motivation to prolong the pregnancy.
Involve the patient and family in planning home care.
Initiate teaching for home care, including medication regimen, activity restrictions, diet and hydration, sexual abstinence, and ways to avoid constipation.
Teach contraction palpation techniques.
Provide written educational material to the family.
Provide referrals to help the family care for the child, home maintenance, and leisure activities as appropriate.
Discuss signs of recurrent preterm labor and reinforce the need to seek healthcare immediately if symptoms return and continue for 1 hour.
Provide written discharge instructions, including explicit indications for seeking healthcare.

definicao: Approximation of wound edges using sterile suture material and a needle.
atividades: Identify patient allergies to anesthetics, suture materials, sterile adhesive strips, tape, and iodophor or other topical solutions.
Identify keloid formation history when appropriate.
Refer deep, facial, joint, or potentially infected wounds to a physician.
Immobilize frightened children or confused adults as appropriate.
Shave hair around the wound using the appropriate technique.
Cleanse the surrounding skin with soap and water or another mild antiseptic solution.
Use sterile technique during the suturing procedure.
Administer topical or injectable anesthetic to the area when appropriate.
Allow sufficient time for the anesthetic to take effect.
Select a suture needle of appropriate gauge and suture material.
Determine the most appropriate suturing method (continuous or interrupted) for the wound.
Position the needle to enter and exit perpendicular to the skin surface.
Pull the needle through the skin, following the line or curve of the needle itself.
Pull the suture firmly without bunching the skin.
Secure the suture line with square knots.
Apply sterile adhesive strips to improve wound closure (e.g., place perpendicular to the incision line; place

definicao: Reducing anxiety in patients with acute distress.
atividades: Maintain calm and deliberate attitudes.
Maintain eye contact with the patient.
Reduce or eliminate stimuli that generate fear or anxiety.
Stay with the patient.
Reassure the patient about their safety.
Identify significant others whose presence may help the patient.
Hold and comfort an infant or child.
Rock the infant as appropriate.
Speak softly or sing to the infant or child.
Offer a pacifier to the infant as appropriate.
Instruct the patient on techniques to use to calm an infant (e.g., talk to them, place a hand on their abdomen, hold their arms, hold and rock).
Reserve time and space to be alone as appropriate.
Sit and talk with the patient.
Facilitate the patient's expression of anger constructively.
Massage the forehead as appropriate.
Offer warm liquids or milk.
Massage the back as appropriate.
Offer a warm immersion bath or shower.
Instruct the patient on methods to decrease anxiety (e.g., slow breathing techniques, distraction, visualization, meditation, progressive muscle relaxation, calm music), as appropriate.
Provide anxiolytic medications as needed.

definicao: Intentional use of animals to provide affection, attention, diversion, and relaxation.
atividades: Determine the patient's acceptance of animals as therapeutic agents.
Check for animal allergies.
Teach the patient/family the purpose and rationale for having animals in a care setting.
Ensure compliance with standards for selection, training, and hygiene of animals in the therapeutic program.
Ensure compliance with standards for maintaining animal health in the therapeutic program.
Comply with health inspection rules regarding animals in an institution.
Develop/maintain a protocol that establishes appropriate responses in cases of accidents or injuries resulting from animal contact.
Provide therapeutic animals for the patient, such as dogs, cats, horses, snakes, turtles, rodents, guinea pigs, and birds.
Avoid visits of animals to unpredictable or violent patients.
Closely monitor animal visits to patients with special conditions (e.g., open wounds, delicate skin, multiple IV access, or other equipment).
Facilitate the patient holding and petting the therapy animals.
Encourage repeated petting of the therapy animal.
Facilitate the patient observing the therapy animals.
Encourage the patient to express emotions about the animals.
Arrange exercises for the patient as appropriate.
Encourage the patient to play with the therapy animals.
Encourage the patient to feed/groom the animals.
Have the patient and others who have come into contact with an animal wash their hands.
Provide opportunity to remember and share past experiences with pets/other animals.

definicao: Use of specific activity or exercise protocols to improve or restore controlled body movements.
atividades: Determine the patient's readiness to engage in the activity or exercise protocol.

definicao: Promotion and assistance in ambulation to maintain or restore autonomous and voluntary functions during treatment and recovery from illness or injury.
atividades: Dress the patient in loose-fitting clothing.
Assist the patient in wearing footwear that facilitates ambulation and prevents injury.
Provide a low bed as appropriate.
Place the bed positioning control within easy reach.
Encourage sitting on the side of the bed with legs dangling or in a chair as tolerated.
Assist the patient in sitting on the side of the bed to facilitate postural adjustments.
Consult the physical therapist on the ambulation plan as needed.
Instruct on the availability of assistive devices if appropriate.
Instruct the patient on how to position during the transfer process.
Use a transfer belt to assist with transfer and ambulation as needed.
Assist the patient with transfer as needed.
Provide cue cards at the bedside to facilitate learning about transfers.
Apply/provide an assistive device (e.g., cane, walker, or wheelchair) for ambulation if the patient cannot maintain balance.
Assist the patient with initial ambulation and as needed.
Instruct the patient/caregiver on safe transfer and ambulation techniques.
Monitor the patient during the use of crutches or other gait assistive devices.
Assist the patient in getting up and ambulating for a specified distance and with the appropriate number of healthcare staff.
Assist the patient in establishing realistic distance increments for ambulation.
Encourage independent ambulation within safe limits.
Encourage the patient to get up spontaneously if appropriate.

definicao: Use of specific activities, postures, and movements to maintain, improve, or restore balance.
Use of active and passive body movement to maintain or restore joint flexibility.
atividades: Determine movement limitations and the effect on joint function.
Collaborate with the physical therapist in developing and implementing an exercise program.
Determine the patient's motivation level to maintain or restore joint movement.
Explain to the patient/family the purpose and plan of exercises for the joint.
Monitor the location and nature of discomfort or pain during movement/activity.
Initiate pain control measures before starting joint exercise.
Dress the patient in loose-fitting clothing.
Protect the patient from trauma during exercise.
Assist the patient in positioning optimally for passive/active joint movement.
Encourage active range-of-motion exercises according to a scheduled regular program.
Perform passive (PROM) or assisted (AROM) range-of-motion exercises as indicated.
Instruct the patient/family on how to systematically perform passive, assisted, or active range-of-motion exercises.
Provide written discharge instructions for exercise.
Assist the patient in developing a schedule for active range-of-motion exercises.
Encourage the patient to visualize body movement before starting it.
Assist with regular rhythmic joint movement within the limits of pain, endurance, and joint mobility.
Encourage the patient to sit on the side of the bed with legs dangling or in a chair as tolerated.
Encourage ambulation if appropriate.
Determine progress toward goal achievement.
Provide positive reinforcement for performing joint exercises.

definicao: Application of medicinal leeches to help drain engorged venous blood from replanted or transplanted tissue.
atividades: Use leeches only if the patient has intact arterial blood supply to prevent infection by endosymbiotic bacteria present in the leech gut.
Instruct the patient that the leech's salivary glands secrete a local anesthetic that helps mask the sensation of the bite.
Instruct the patient that local anesthetic will not be needed in the replanted tissue as the nerves have been recently reanastomosed.
Instruct the patient that leeches secrete hirudin, an anticoagulant; so that up to 50 mL of blood may ooze from the site for 24 to 48 hours after removal.
Reassure the patient that leech therapy is accepted as a medical treatment.
Use universal precautions.
Use one leech per patient to prevent infection transfer from another patient.
Clean the graft (skin flap) or finger with sterile water and dry with a sterile cloth.
Instruct the patient not to touch the leech or remove it manually once applied.
Surround the site with towels and/or gauze to prevent the leech from

definicao: Facilitating swallowing and preventing complications of impaired swallowing.
atividades: Collaborate with other care team members (i.e., occupational therapist, speech-language pathologist, and dietitian) to continue the patient's rehabilitation plan.
Determine the patient's ability to focus attention on learning/executing feeding and swallowing tasks.
Remove environmental distractions before starting swallowing work with the patient.
Provide privacy to the patient as desired or indicated.
Position yourself so the patient can see you and hear what you say.
Explain the reasons for the swallowing regimen to the patient/family.
Collaborate with the speech-language pathologist in instructing the patient's family about the swallowing exercise regimen.
Provide/use assistive devices as appropriate.
Avoid using straws for drinking.
Assist the patient in sitting upright (as close to 90 degrees as possible) for feeding/exercising.
Assist the patient in positioning the head flexed forward in preparation for swallowing ('chin tuck').
Assist the patient in remaining seated for 30 minutes after finishing the meal.
Instruct the patient to open and close their mouth in preparation for food manipulation.
Instruct the patient not to talk during meals as appropriate.
Instruct the patient to say 'ahs' in staccato to promote soft palate elevation as appropriate.
Provide a lollipop for the patient to suck to increase tongue strength as appropriate.
Assist the hemiplegic patient in sitting with the affected arm straight on the table.
Assist the patient in placing food at the back of the mouth and on the unaffected side.
Monitor signs and symptoms of aspiration.
Monitor the patient's tongue movements during feeding.
Monitor lip closure while the patient eats, drinks, and swallows.
Monitor for signs of fatigue during eating, drinking, and swallowing.
Provide a rest period before eating/exercising to avoid excessive fatigue.
Check the mouth for food pocketing in the cheek after eating.
Instruct the patient to reach food particles on the lips or chin using their tongue.
Assist the patient in removing food particles from lips and chin if they cannot extend their tongue.
Instruct family/caregiver on the patient's position, feeding, and monitoring.
Instruct the patient/caregiver on nutritional needs and dietary modifications in collaboration with a dietitian.
Instruct the patient/caregiver on emergency measures for choking.
Instruct the patient/caregiver on ways to check for food pocketing in the cheeks after meals.
Provide written instructions as appropriate.
Provide scheduled practice sessions for family/caregiver as needed.
Provide/monitor food/liquid consistency based on swallowing study findings.
Consult a speech-language pathologist and/or physician about gradually increasing the consistency of food provided to the patient.
Assist in maintaining adequate fluid and calorie intake.
Monitor body weight.
Monitor body hydration (e.g., intake, output, skin turgor, mucous membranes).
Provide oral care as needed.

definicao: Administration and monitoring of dialysis solution into and out of the peritoneal cavity.
atividades: Explain the selected peritoneal dialysis procedure and its purpose.
Warm the dialysis fluid before infusion.
Assess catheter patency, noting difficulty in inflow/outflow.
Maintain a record of inflow/outflow volumes and individual/cumulative fluid balance.
Ask the patient to empty the bladder before peritoneal catheter insertion.
Avoid excessive mechanical stress on dialysis catheters.

definicao: Application of psychotherapeutic techniques to a group, including the use of member interactions.
atividades: Determine the purpose of the group (e.g., reality testing maintenance, communication facilitation, interpersonal skills examination, and support) and the nature of the group process.
Form a group of optimal size: 5 to 12 members.

Create specific rules and guidelines for the group (e.g., confidentiality, respectful communication and behavior, attendance, member socialization outside the group, participation) to ensure all members comply.
Conduct an individual orientation session for each new group member before the first group session.
Provide a manual including group rules and guidelines for all members.
Choose group members who are willing to participate actively and take responsibility for their own problems.
Determine if the motivation level is high enough to benefit from group therapy.
Use a co-leader as appropriate.
Address the issue of mandatory attendance.
Address the issue of compulsory presence.
Address the issue of whether new users can join at any time.
Establish a time and place for the group meeting.
Conduct sessions of 1 to 2 hours as appropriate.
Start and end on time and expect participants to remain until the end.
Arrange chairs in a close circle.
Move the group to the working phase as quickly as possible.
Assist the group in forming therapeutic norms.
Assist the group in overcoming resistance to change.
Give the group a sense of direction that allows them to identify and resolve each stage of development.
Use the 'process illumination' technique to encourage exploration of the meaning of the message.
Encourage self-disclosure and discussion of the past only as it relates to group function and goals.
Use the 'here-and-now activation' technique to shift focus from general to personal, from abstract to specific.
Encourage members to share.
Encourage members to share what they have in common with others.
Encourage members to share their anger, sadness, humor, distrust, and other feelings with each other.
Assist members in exploring and accepting any anger felt towards the group leader and others.
Confront behaviors that threaten group cohesion (e.g., lateness, absences, disruption of extra-group socialization, and scapegoating).
Provide social reinforcement (e.g., verbal and non-verbal) for desirable behavior/responses.
Provide structured group exercises as appropriate to promote group function and insight.
Use role-playing and problem-solving as appropriate.
Assist members in providing feedback to each other so they develop insights into their own behavior.
Incorporate leaderless sessions when appropriate.
Incorporate leaderless sessions when appropriate for group goals and function.
End the session with a summary of the process.
Meet individually with the member wishing to terminate therapy early to assess the reason.
Assist the member in leaving the group if appropriate.
Assist the group in reviewing the past history and relationship of a member with the group when someone leaves.
Recruit new members as appropriate to maintain group integrity.

definicao: Use of recollections of past events, feelings, and thoughts to facilitate pleasure, quality of life, or adaptation to current circumstances.
atividades: Choose a comfortable environment.
Provide an adequate amount of time.
Identify, with the patient, a theme for each session (e.g., professional life).
Choose an appropriately small group of participants for the

definicao: Use of encouragement and promotion of relaxation techniques to reduce undesirable signs and symptoms such as pain, muscle tension, or anxiety.
atividades: Describe the reason for relaxation and the benefits, limits, and types of relaxation available (e.g., music, meditation, rhythmic breathing, jaw relaxation, and progressive muscle relaxation).
Assess for decreased energy level, inability to concentrate, or other competing symptoms that may interfere with cognitive ability to focus on the relaxation technique.
Determine if any past relaxation intervention was helpful.
Consider the individual's desire to participate, ability to participate, preferences, past experiences, and contraindications before choosing a specific relaxation strategy.
Provide a detailed description of the chosen relaxation technique.
Create a calm, interruption-free environment with dim lights and comfortable temperature.
Suggest that the individual assume a comfortable position, wearing loose clothing and eyes closed.
Individualize the content of the relaxation intervention (e.g., by soliciting suggestions for changes).
Trigger behaviors that are conditioned to produce relaxation, such as deep breaths, yawns, abdominal breathing, or use of peaceful imagery.
Invite the patient to relax and let sensations happen.
Use a soft tone of voice with a slow rhythm of words.
Demonstrate and practice relaxation techniques with the patient.
Encourage demonstrations of the techniques by the patient if possible.
Anticipate the need for relaxation use.
Provide written information on the preparation and participation in relaxation techniques.
Encourage frequent repetition or practice of selected techniques.
Provide an uninterrupted time because the patient may sleep.
Encourage control when the relaxation technique is performed.
Regularly evaluate the individual's information on relaxation achieved and periodically monitor muscle tension, heart rate, blood pressure, and skin temperature as appropriate.
Create a tape for the relaxation technique for the individual to use as appropriate.
Use relaxation as an adjunctive strategy with pain medications or in conjunction with other measures as appropriate.
Assess and document the response to relaxation therapy.

definicao: Facilitating the safe and effective use of hormone replacement therapy.
atividades: Determine the reason for choosing hormone replacement therapy.
Analyze alternatives to hormone replacement therapy.
Monitor the patient for therapeutic effects.
Monitor adverse effects.
Review information on the benefits and adverse effects of different hormonal components (e.g., estrogen, progestogen, androgen).
Review information on interactions of adjuvant treatments (e.g., calcium and vitamin D supplementation, exercise, thiazide use).
Review information regarding different administration methods (e.g., continuous oral combination, sequential oral, dermal, vaginal).
Facilitate the decision to continue/discontinue.
Facilitate changes in hormone replacement therapy with the healthcare professional as appropriate.
Recommend the patient make short-term decisions annually about continuation.
Adjust medications or dose as appropriate.

definicao: Assisting family members to lead their family to a more productive way of life.
atividades: Use family history to motivate family discussion.
Determine communication patterns in the family.
Identify how the family solves problems.
Determine how the family makes decisions.
Determine if abuse is occurring in the family.
Identify family strengths/resources.
Identify usual roles in the family system.
Identify specific disturbances related to role expectations.
Determine if any family member is abusing substances.
Identify family alliances.
Identify areas of dissatisfaction and/or conflict.
Determine recent or imminent events threatening the family.
Assist family members in communicating more effectively.
Facilitate family discussion.
Assist family members in prioritizing and selecting the most urgent family problems to solve.
Assist family members in clarifying what they need and expect from each other.
Facilitate stress reduction strategies.
Provide guidance and information.
Assist the family in improving existing positive coping strategies.
Share the therapeutic plan with the family.
Ask family members to participate in experiential homework assignments, such as having some meals together.
Provide challenges in family discussion to motivate new behaviors.
Discuss hierarchical relationships of family subsystems.
Assist family members in changing how they relate to each other.
Facilitate restructuring of family subsystems as appropriate.
Assist the family in setting goals toward a more competent way of dealing with dysfunctional behavior.
Monitor family boundaries.
Monitor adverse therapeutic responses.
Plan conclusion and follow-up strategies.

definicao: Administration and monitoring of intravenous fluids and medications.
atividades: Verify the prescription for IV therapy.
Instruct the patient about the procedure.
Follow strict aseptic technique.
Examine the solution for type, amount, expiration date, character of the solution, and container integrity.
Follow the 'five rights' before initiating infusion or medication administration (e.g., right medication, dose, patient, route, and frequency).
Select and prepare the IV infusion pump when indicated.
Puncture the container with the appropriate tubing.
Administer IV fluids at room temperature unless otherwise prescribed.
Identify if the patient is using medications incompatible with the prescribed one.
Administer IV medication as prescribed and monitor results.
Monitor the IV flow rate and IV site during infusion.
Monitor for fluid overload and physical reaction.
Monitor for IV patency before IV medication administration.
Replace IV cannula, tubing, and infusion supplies every 48 to 72 hours according to institutional protocol.
Maintain an occlusive dressing.
Check IV sites according to institutional protocol.
Care for IV sites according to institutional protocol.
Monitor vital signs.
Monitor the amount of intravenous potassium to not exceed 200 mEq per 24 hours for adults as appropriate.
Flush intravenous tubing between solution administrations.

definicao: Administration of food and fluids to support metabolic processes of a malnourished patient or one at high risk for malnutrition.
atividades: Perform a nutritional assessment as appropriate.
Monitor food/fluid intake and calculate daily caloric intake as appropriate.
Monitor the adequacy of the diet prescription to meet daily nutritional needs as appropriate.
Determine, in collaboration with the dietitian, the amount of calories and type of nutrients needed to meet nutritional requirements, as appropriate.
Determine food preferences considering cultural and religious diversity.
Select nutritional supplements as appropriate.
Encourage the patient to select soft foods if saliva deficiency makes swallowing difficult.
Encourage intake of calcium-rich foods as appropriate.
Encourage intake of potassium-rich foods and fluids as appropriate.
Ensure the diet includes high-fiber foods to prevent constipation.
Provide the patient with high-protein, high-calorie, nutritious foods and fluids that can be easily consumed, as appropriate.
Assist the patient in selecting soft, smooth, non-acidic foods as appropriate.
Determine the need for enteral tube feeding.
Administer enteral tube feeding as appropriate.
Discontinue enteral tube feeding when oral intake is tolerated.
Administer hyperalimentation fluids as appropriate.
Ensure availability of progressive therapeutic diet.
Provide necessary feeding within the limits of the prescribed diet.
Encourage bringing home-prepared food to the institution as appropriate.
Suggest a trial elimination of lactose-containing foods as appropriate.
Offer herbs and spices as an alternative to salt.
Structure the environment to create a pleasant and relaxing atmosphere.
Present food attractively and pleasantly, considering color, texture, and variety.
Provide oral care before meals as needed.
Assist the patient in sitting before eating or feeding.
Monitor laboratory data as appropriate.
Instruct the patient and family on the prescribed diet.
Refer for diet teaching and planning as needed.
Give the patient and family written examples of the prescribed diet.

definicao: Prescription and assistance with specific physical, cognitive, social, and spiritual activities to increase the range, frequency, or duration of an individual or group activity.
atividades: Assess the patient's ability to participate in specific activities.
Collaborate with occupational therapists, physical therapists, or recreational therapists in planning and monitoring an activity program as appropriate.
Assess the patient's commitment to increasing the frequency and range of activity.
Assist the patient in exploring the personal meaning of a habitual activity (e.g., work) and favorite leisure activities.
Assist the patient in choosing activities and activity goals that are compatible with physical, psychological, and social capabilities.
Assist the patient in focusing on abilities, not disabilities.
Assist the patient in identifying and obtaining necessary resources for the desired activity.
Encourage creative activities as appropriate.
Assist the patient in obtaining transportation for activities as appropriate.
Assist the patient in identifying activity preferences.
Assist the patient in identifying meaningful activities.
Assist the patient in scheduling specific times for activities in the daily routine.
Assist the patient and family in identifying activity level deficits.
Identify strategies to promote patient participation in desired activities.
Instruct the patient and family on the role of physical, social, spiritual, and cognitive activities in maintaining function and health.
Instruct the patient and family on how to perform desired or prescribed activities.
Coordinate the selection of age-appropriate activities for the patient.
Assist the patient and family in adapting the environment to accommodate the desired activity.
Provide activities to increase the attention level in consultation with the occupational therapist.
Facilitate substitution of activities when the patient has time, energy, or movement limitations, in consultation with the occupational therapist, physical therapist, or recreational therapist.
Encourage participation in group activities or therapies as appropriate.
Refer to community centers or activity programs as appropriate.

definicao: Use of an interactive helping process to resolve trauma experienced by a child.
atividades: Teach specific stress management techniques before trauma investigation to restore a sense of control over thoughts and feelings.
Investigate the trauma and its meaning for the child.
Use developmentally appropriate language to ask questions about the trauma.
Use relaxation and desensitization procedures to help the child describe the event.
Establish trust, safety, and the right to access care with stored material regarding trauma, monitoring reactions to disclosure.
Proceed with therapy at the child's own pace.
Establish a signal the child can make if trauma work overwhelms them.
Focus on self-regulation therapy and rebuilding a sense of safety.
Use art and play to promote expression.
Involve appropriate parents or caregivers in therapy as appropriate.
Educate parents about the child's reaction to trauma and the therapy process.
Assist parents in resolving their own emotional distress regarding the trauma.
Assist appropriate significant others in providing support.
Avoid involving parents or guardians if they are the cause of the trauma.
Assist the child in reconsidering statements made about the traumatic event with step-by-step analysis of perceptual and cognitive distortions.
Investigate and correct inaccurate attributions about the trauma, including fortune-telling and survivor guilt.
Assist in identifying and managing feelings.
Explain the grieving process to the child and parent and/or mother as appropriate.
Assist the child in examining distorted assumptions and conclusions.
Assist the child in re-establishing a sense of safety and predictability in their life.
Assist the child in integrating restructured trauma events into their life history and experience.
Address role functioning in family life, peer relationships, and post-trauma school performance.

definicao: Cleaning the blood of a patient with acute illness through a hemofilter controlled by the patient's hydrostatic pressure.
atividades: Determine baseline vital signs and weight.
Collect a blood sample and analyze blood biochemistry (e.g., BUN, serum creatinine, serum Na, Ca, K, and PO4 levels) before therapy.
Determine and record the patient's hemodynamic function.
Explain the procedure to the patient and related persons as appropriate.
Obtain written consent.
Adjust technology according to various patient system pathologies (e.g., place patient on a rotating air flow bed).
Use sterile technique to flush and prepare arterial tubing, venous tubing, and hemofilter with heparinized saline and connect to other tubing as needed.
Remove all air bubbles from the hemofiltration system.
Administer a heparin dose per protocol or medical order.
Use mask, gloves, and gown to prevent contact with blood.
Use sterile technique to initiate venous and arterial access per protocol.
Secure connections and tubing firmly.
Apply restraints as appropriate.
Monitor the ultrafiltration rate, adjusting the rate per protocol or medical order.
Monitor the hemofiltration system for leaks in connections and coagulation in the filter or tubing.
Monitor the patient's multiple systemic parameters by protocol.
Monitor and care for access points and lines according to protocol.
Monitor for signs and symptoms of infection.
Instruct patient/family on post-treatment precautions.

definicao: Intentional use of recreational activities to improve independence and social skills, as well as reduce or eliminate the effects of illness or disability.
atividades: Identify any deficits that limit participation in recreational activities (e.g., mobility, cognitive, economic).
Assist the patient in identifying meaningful recreational activities (e.g., sports, theater, games, arts, and crafts).
Assist the patient in exploring the personal meaning of favorite recreational activities.
Include the patient in planning recreational activities.
Assist the patient in choosing recreational activities compatible with physical, psychological, and social capability.
Assist in obtaining necessary resources for the recreational activity.
Provide safe recreational equipment.
Observe safety precautions.
Supervise recreational sessions when appropriate.
Monitor the patient's physical and mental capacities while participating in recreational activities.
Provide new recreational activities appropriate for age and ability.
Provide recreational activities aimed at reducing anxiety (e.g., card games, puzzles).
Assist in obtaining transportation for recreational activities.
Provide positive reinforcement for participation in activities.
Monitor the emotional, physical, and social response to the recreational activity.

definicao: Use of people, resources, and events in the patient's immediate environment to promote optimal psychosocial functioning.
atividades: Determine factors in the environment that contribute to patient behavior.
Regulate environmental factors that maximize adaptive behavior and minimize maladaptive behavior.
Consider the needs of others beyond the needs of the particular individual.
Provide self-care resources for patient use.
Enhance the normalcy of the environment through the use of clocks, calendars, handrails, and furniture.
Facilitate open communication between patient, nursing, and other staff.
Build a trusting therapeutic relationship with individuals by conveying genuine interest, respect, care, and compassion.
Model interpersonal skills, distress tolerance, and emotion regulation.
Avoid being too rigid or too flexible with rules.
Define clear and consistent rules and policies for patients, families, and staff.
Note and reinforce new adaptive behaviors.
Provide descriptions of problematic behavior in objective, impartial, non-pejorative terms, avoiding stigmatizing labels.
Include the patient in decisions about their own care.
Define treatment goals cooperatively with the patient, linking privileges to progress and goal achievement when appropriate.
Communicate the patient's individual treatment goals to all team members.
Provide individualized nursing care when appropriate.
Check on the patient regularly.
Provide support for formal and informal group activities to promote sharing, cooperation, commitment, and leadership.
Offer time and space for rehearsal of new behaviors to build mastery and adaptation to future environments.
Examine one's own attitudes toward patient rights, self-determination, social control, and deviance issues.
Use empathy when interpreting patient and colleague behaviors.
Ensure staff presence and supervision.
Minimize restrictions that diminish privacy or self-control when appropriate.
Encourage the use of personal property.
Minimize the use of locked doors and medications and rigid activity or property regulations as much as possible.
Offer a phone in a private space when appropriate.
Encourage appropriate patient-to-patient interactions.
Offer attractively arranged areas for private conversations with other patients, family, and friends.
Provide books, magazines, and craft materials according to the patient's recreational, cultural, and educational background and needs.
Monitor individual behavior that is disruptive or detrimental to the overall well-being of others.
Respond to disruptive or harmful behavior safely using the least restrictive measures possible.
Avoid the use of physical, mechanical, and chemical restraint when possible.
Limit the number of unmedicated psychotic patients at any time through controlled admissions and variable time periods of medication-free attempts when appropriate.

definicao: Performance of laboratory tests at the patient care site.
atividades: Obtain adequate training/orientation before performing the test.
Participate in assessment for color blindness as needed for specific tests and as required by the institution.
Participate in test proficiency programs as required by the institution.
Follow institutional procedures for specimen collection and preservation as appropriate.
Label specimens immediately to minimize specimen mix-ups as appropriate.
Use the appropriate specimen for the test to be performed.
Perform tests on collected specimens in a timely manner.

definicao: Providing comfort and communication through purposeful tactile contact.
atividades: Assess the individual's personal comfort with using touch with patients and families.
Assess the patient's readiness when offering touch.
Assess the environmental context before offering touch.
Determine which body part is best for touch and the duration of touch that produces the most positive responses from the recipient.
Observe cultural taboos about touch.
Hug to offer reassurance when appropriate.
Place an arm around the patient's shoulders as appropriate.
Hold the patient's hands for emotional support.
Apply light pressure to the wrist, hand, or shoulder of a critically ill patient.
Rub the back in synchrony with the patient's breathing as appropriate.
Stroke the body part slowly, rhythmically as appropriate.
Massage around the painful area as appropriate.
Elicit from parents actions commonly used to calm their child.
Hold the infant or child firmly and caringly.
Encourage parents to touch their newborn or ill child.
Swaddle the preterm infant with rolled blankets (nests).
Wrap the infant comfortably in a blanket to keep arms and legs close to the body.
Position the baby on the mother's body immediately after birth.
Encourage the mother to hold, touch, and examine the child while the umbilical cord is being cut.
Encourage parents to hold the infant.
Encourage parents to massage the infant.
Demonstrate techniques to calm infants.
Provide an appropriate pacifier for non-nutritive sucking in newborns.
Provide oral stimulation exercises before tube feeding in preterm infants.
Evaluate the effect of touch use.

definicao: Provision of a non-invasive biofield therapy using touch and compassionate intent to influence a person's energy system, affecting their physical, emotional, mental, and spiritual health and healing.
atividades: Create a comfortable, private environment free from distractions.
Determine the willingness to have the body touched.
Identify common goals for the session.
Advise the patient to ask questions whenever they arise.
Place the patient in a comfortable and safe position that facilitates relaxation (e.g., chair, recliner, or massage table can be used if the body can be supported safely).
Remove constricting items (e.g., glasses, shoes, and belt).
Keep the patient dressed comfortably.
Cover only to provide thermal comfort.
Center yourself by focusing on inner awareness.
Ground yourself by synchronizing with the Earth's energy.
Synchronize with the patient's energy field.
Establish the intention to work for the patient's highest good.
Conduct an assessment of the patient's energy field (aura) and energy centers to determine whether cleansing, balancing, or energizing techniques should be used.

Determine the specific therapeutic touch approach to promote healing (e.g., calming or softening the energy field, full body connection, etheric vitality, magnetic smoothness, magnetic pain drainage, spiral meditation, pyramid technique).
Use the hands to cleanse, balance, and energize the patient's field.
Continue until the field and energy center feel balanced, smooth, connected, symmetrical, and flowing.
Repeat an energy assessment to identify any changes

definicao: Tuning into the universal energy field, seeking to act as a healing influence through the use of natural sensitivity of the hands and passing them over the body to focus, direct, and gently modulate the human energy field.
atividades: Create a comfortable, distraction-free environment.
Determine willingness to undergo the intervention.
Identify mutual goals for the session.
Advise the patient to ask questions whenever they arise.
Place the patient in a comfortable chair or supine position.
Center by focusing on inner self-awareness.
Focus on the intention to facilitate fullness and healing at all levels of consciousness.
Position the palms facing the patient between 8 and 13 centimeters from the patient's body.
Begin the assessment for 1 to 2 minutes by moving hands slowly and firmly over the patient without touching, from head to toe and front to back.
Move hands in very gentle downward strokes through the patient's energy field, thinking of the patient as a whole and facilitating open and balanced energy flow.

Observe the overall pattern of energy flow, especially in areas of disturbance, such as congestion or irregularity, which may be perceived through very subtle signals in the hands, for example, temperature change, tingling, or other subtle sensations of movement.
Focus intention on facilitating symmetry and healing in disturbed areas.
Continue the treatment, very gently facilitating the flow of healing energy in the disturbed areas.
Finish when you judge an appropriate amount of change has occurred (i.e., for an infant, 1 to 2 minutes; for an adult, 5 to 10 minutes), keeping in mind the importance of gentleness.
Encourage the patient to rest for 20 minutes or more after the treatment.
Observe if the patient showed a relaxation response and any related changes.
Instruct the patient to move their body gently and stretch before standing.
Touch the patient's body to help them ground or connect to the Earth's energy if they feel dizzy upon standing.
Offer the patient a glass of water to replace water lost with energy movement.
Provide feedback to the patient regarding the energy work in terms they understand.
Ask the patient to describe what they felt or perceived during or after the session.
Record the characteristics of the energy work.
Record physical, mental, and emotional responses to the session.

definicao: Transfer of information from order sheets to the nursing care plan and documentation system.
atividades: Ensure the order sheet is stamped with patient identification.
Ensure the order sheet is in the correct patient's chart.
Ensure orders are written or signed by a licensed healthcare provider with clinical privileges.
Repeat the verbal order to the physician to ensure accuracy.
Avoid accepting verbal orders through other providers.
Ensure verbal orders are documented according to institutional policy before recording.
Assist the physician who lacks computer skills to enter the order into the computerized order entry system using the physician's login and confirmation that the order is correct.
Clarify confusing or illegible orders.
Clarify obscure abbreviations in orders.
Assess the appropriateness of the order and ensure all necessary information is provided.
Consult a pharmacist whenever uncertain about an unknown agent or a prescribed dose.
Document any disagreement with the physician's order after discussion with the physician and a supervisor.
Sign the name, title, date, and time of each transcribed order.
Transfer the order to the appropriate electronic document, flow sheet, medication sheet, laboratory requisition, care plan, or Kardex.
Schedule appointments as appropriate.
Note start and stop dates for medications according to institutional policy.
Note patient allergies when transcribing medication orders.
Inform team members to initiate treatment.
Participate in the development and advancement of computerized order entry systems for physicians and other professionals who write orders.

definicao: Movement of a patient with limited independent movement.
atividades: Review the chart for activity orders.
Determine the level of mobility and movement limitations.
Determine the level of consciousness and ability to cooperate.
Plan the type and method of movement.
Determine the amount and type of assistance needed.
Ensure equipment functions before use.
Discuss the need for relocation with the patient and/or family.
Discuss with the patient and helpers how the movement will be done.
Assist the patient in receiving all necessary care (e.g., personal hygiene, gathering belongings) before performing the transfer, as appropriate.

definicao: Movement of a patient from one institution to another.
atividades: Ensure a medical screening examination has been performed and documented.

Ensure the patient has been stabilized within the capabilities of the transferring institution or has met the conditions under which unstable patients may be transferred, considering the Emergency Treatment and Labor Act (EMTALA).
Determine the need for patient transfer, ensuring the patient requires treatment at the receiving institution and that the benefits of transfer outweigh the risks.
Obtain a written physician order for patient transport.
Identify the patient's or significant others' preference for institution and receiving physician as appropriate.
Document that the institution accepts the patient and has the necessary equipment and staff to handle the clinical situation.
Obtain written consent for transfer from the patient or significant others.
Obtain written consent for transfer of minors as appropriate.
Obtain written consent for release of patient information to the receiving institution.
Facilitate contact between the receiving physician and the attending physician as indicated by EMTALA, and document this contact.
Arrange the required type of transport.
Conduct a nurse-to-nurse handoff report about the patient and institution and document this contact.
Mobilize and provide necessary personnel, transfer equipment, and pharmaceutical products.
Copy medical records for the receiving institution, including the record of current events.
Ensure medical records accompany the patient during transport.
Complete the transfer certification, signed by the physician with time and date.
Document the medical reason for transfer, as well as the medical benefits and risks of transfer, as indicated by EMTALA.
Document all information regarding patients who refuse transfer.
Attempt to secure a written statement of refusal of transfer from the patient if indicated.
Continue to treat the patient who refuses transfer within the capacity of the institution.

definicao: Movement of a patient from one area of the facility to another.
atividades: Facilitate coordination and communication before transport.
Obtain a medical order before transport as appropriate.
Determine the amount and type of assistance needed.
Provide adequate personnel to assist with transport.
Provide adequate equipment to assist with transport.
Discuss the need for relocation with the patient and significant others.
Assist the patient in receiving all necessary care (e.g., personal hygiene, gathering belongings) before performing the transfer, as appropriate.
Ensure the patient's new accommodations are ready.
Move the patient using required equipment if needed.
Use an incubator, stretcher, or bed to move a weak, injured, or surgical patient from one area to another.
Use a wheelchair to move a patient unable to walk.
Carry an infant or young child.
Assist the patient to ambulate using your body as a human crutch as appropriate.
Provide companionship during transport as needed.
Monitor as appropriate during transport.
Conduct a clinical handoff report about the patient to the receiving location as appropriate.
Document pertinent information related to transport.
Remove patients in emergency situations, such as fire, hurricane, or tornado, according to the institution's disaster plan.

definicao: Management of symptoms and related states associated with increased body temperature mediated by endogenous pyrogens.
atividades: Monitor temperature and other vital signs.
Monitor skin color and temperature.
Monitor intake and output, being aware of changes in insensible fluid losses.
Administer medications or IV fluids (e.g., antipyretics, antibacterials, and anti-shivering agents).
Do not administer acetylsalicylic acid to children.

Cover the patient with a blanket or light clothing depending on the phase of fever (provide a warm blanket for the chill phase and light personal clothing or bed linens for the fever and flush phases).
Encourage fluid intake.
Facilitate rest by applying activity restrictions if needed.
Administer oxygen as appropriate.
Administer a tepid sponge bath with caution (i.e., administer to a patient with a very high temperature, do not administer during the chill phase, and avoid making the patient feel cold).
Increase air circulation.

Monitor complications related to fever and signs and symptoms of the condition causing the fever (e.g., seizure, decreased level of consciousness, abnormal electrolyte condition, acid-base imbalance, cardiac arrhythmia, and abnormal cellular changes).
Ensure other signs of infection are monitored in the elderly, as they may manifest only low-grade fever or no fever in case of infection.
Ensure safety measures are available if the patient becomes agitated or delirious.
Moisturize dry lips and nasal mucosa.

definicao: Management of symptoms and conditions related to increased body temperature due to thermoregulatory dysfunction.
atividades: Ensure a clear airway.
Monitor vital signs.
Administer oxygen if needed.

Discontinue the presumptive causative medication (e.g., selective serotonin reuptake inhibitors [SSRIs], monoamine oxidase inhibitors [MAOIs], or tricyclic antidepressants) for the patient with neuroleptic malignant syndrome.
Stop physical activity.
Remove the patient from the heat source, moving them to a cooler environment.
Loosen or remove clothing.
Apply external cooling methods (e.g., cold compresses to the neck, chest, abdomen, scalp, axillae, and groin; cooling blanket), as appropriate.
Position the patient in cool water as tolerated to prevent shivering.
Moisten the body surface and fan the patient.
Avoid alcohol sponge baths.
Provide oral rehydration solution (e.g., sports drinks) or other chilled fluids.
Do not offer broths or fluids orally to patients with neurological impairment.
Do not administer salt tablets.
Establish IV access.
Administer IV fluids using cooled solutions as appropriate.
Apply internal cooling methods (e.g., chilled gastric, bladder, peritoneal, or thoracic lavage), as appropriate.
Administer anti-shivering medication if needed.
Do not administer acetylsalicylic acid or other antipyretics.
Insert a nasogastric tube as appropriate.
Insert a urinary catheter.
Stop cooling activities when core body temperature reaches 39°C.
Monitor for abnormalities in mental status (e.g., confusion, bizarre behavior, anxiety, loss of coordination, agitation, seizure, and coma).
Monitor core body temperature using an appropriate device (e.g., rectal or esophageal probe).
Obtain laboratory values of serum electrolytes, urinalysis, cardiac enzymes, liver enzymes, and complete blood count and monitor results.
Monitor urine output.
Monitor arterial blood gases.
Monitor for hypoglycemia.
Monitor electrocardiographic results.
Monitor complications (e.g., renal dysfunction, acid-base imbalance, coagulopathy, pulmonary edema, cerebral edema, and multiple organ dysfunction syndrome).
Provide or arrange transport to the hospital for further treatment.

Instruct the patient on risk factors for heat-related illness (e.g., high ambient temperature, high humidity, dehydration, excessive physical exertion, obesity, extremes of age, certain medications, and heart disease).

Instruct the patient on measures to prevent heat-related illness (e.g., prevent overexposure to sun; ensure adequate intake of nutritious food and fluids before, during, and after physical activity; seek facilities where air conditioning is available; and wear light-colored, loose-fitting clothing).
Instruct the patient on early signs and symptoms of heat-related illness and when to seek healthcare professional assistance.

definicao: Prevention of heat loss, rewarming, and monitoring of a patient whose core body temperature is abnormally low due to non-induced circumstances.
atividades: Monitor the patient's temperature using appropriate devices and measurement sites.
Remove the patient from the cold environment.

definicao: Providing immediate emotional and physical support following a report of rape.
atividades: Allow the support person to remain with the patient.
Explain available legal procedures to the patient.
Explain the rape protocol and obtain consent to proceed with the protocol.
Document if the patient has bathed, washed, or rinsed their private parts after the incident.
Document mental status, physical status (clothing, soiled, debris), history of the incident, evidence of violence, and previous gynecological history.
Determine the presence of cuts, bruises, bleeding, lacerations, or other signs of physical injury.
Implement a rape protocol (e.g., label and store soiled clothing, vaginal secretions, and materials collected from vaginal hair).
Store samples for legal evidence.
Implement crisis intervention counseling.
Offer pregnancy prevention medications as appropriate.
Offer prophylactic antibiotics against venereal diseases.
Inform the patient about HIV testing as appropriate.
Provide clear written information about medication use, crisis support services, and legal support.
Refer the patient to a legal defense program.
Document according to institutional policy.

definicao: Care of the patient and family demonstrating dysfunction resulting from substance abuse or dependence.
atividades: Establish a trusting relationship while setting clear limits (i.e., provide gentle but firm evidence of dysfunction; stay focused on the substance abuse or dependence; and promote hope).
Consider the presence of comorbidity or concomitant medical or psychiatric disorder, making congruent modifications in treatment.
Assist the patient in understanding the disorder as a disease related to multiple factors (e.g., genetic, psychological, and situational circumstances).
Instruct the patient that the amount and frequency of substance use leading to dependence vary widely among people.
Instruct the patient on the effects of the substance used (e.g., physical, psychological, and social).
Discuss treatment needs for associated medical, psychological, social, vocational, housing, and legal difficulties.
Encourage or praise the patient's efforts toward accepting responsibility for substance-related dependence and its treatment.
Provide symptom management during the detoxification period.
Administer medications (e.g., disulfiram, acamprosate, methadone, naltrexone, nicotine patches or gum, or buprenorphine) as indicated.
Instruct the patient or family about medications used in treatment.
Provide therapy (e.g., cognitive, motivational, counseling, family support, family therapy, or community reinforcement approach for adolescents) as indicated.
Establish multidisciplinary programs (e.g., short-term residential inpatient therapy, detoxification program, or community residential therapeutic treatment) when appropriate.
Encourage the patient to participate in a self-help support program during and after treatment (e.g., 12-step programs, Women for Sobriety, or Rational Recovery).
Discuss the importance of abstaining from substance use, identifying the most appropriate therapeutic goal (e.g., total abstinence, sobriety

definicao: Care of the patient undergoing detoxification due to substance abuse.
atividades: Monitor vital signs.
Monitor respiratory and cardiac changes (e.g., hypertension, tachycardia, and bradypnea).
Monitor changes in level of consciousness.
Monitor intake and output.
Monitor suicidal tendencies.
Implement precautions for the patient at risk for suicide.

Monitor withdrawal symptoms (e.g., fatigue, sensory disturbances, irritability, violence, depression, panic attacks, 'craving,' insomnia, agitation, muscle pain, appetite changes, yawning, weakness, headache, rhinorrhea, dilated pupils, chills, anxiety, diaphoresis, nausea, vomiting, tremors, psychosis, and ataxia).
Promote symptom management.

Administer medications (e.g., benzodiazepines, chlorpromazine, diazepam, nicotine replacement, phenobarbital, clonidine, trazodone, methadone, alpha2-adrenergic agonists, and antipsychotics), always keeping in mind the possibility of cross-tolerance.
Implement precautions for patients at risk for seizures.
Provide adequate nutrition (e.g., small, frequent amounts of fluid and high-calorie foods).
Assist with activities of daily living.
Implement precautions for patients at risk for falls.
Maintain a low-stimulation environment (e.g., speak in a low, calm voice; give reassurance of safety; and ensure a comfortable, dark, quiet, non-threatening environment).
Reorient the patient to reality.
Encourage the patient to participate in follow-up support (e.g., peer group therapy, individual or family counseling, and educational recovery programs).
Offer supportive assistance (e.g., provision of food and shelter, structured psychotherapy).
Make referral.
Facilitate support from family and relevant others.
Provide support to the family or relevant others as appropriate.
Instruct the patient and family about the process of substance use and dependence.

definicao: Care of the patient undergoing sudden cessation of alcohol consumption.
atividades: Create a low-stimulation environment for detoxification.
Monitor vital signs during withdrawal.
Monitor for delirium tremens.
Administer anticonvulsants or sedatives as appropriate.
Medicate to alleviate physical discomfort if needed.
Address abusive patient behavior neutrally.
Treat hallucinations therapeutically.
Maintain adequate nutrient and fluid intake.
Administer vitamin therapy as appropriate.
Monitor hidden alcohol consumption during detoxification.
Listen to the patient's concerns about alcohol withdrawal.
Provide emotional support to the patient/family as appropriate.
Offer reassurance through speech as appropriate.
Provide reality orientation as appropriate.
Reassure the patient that depression and fatigue commonly occur during withdrawal.

definicao: Management of the patient's extracorporeal blood passage through the dialyzer.
atividades: Collect a blood sample and review blood chemistry (e.g., blood urea, serum creatinine, serum Na, K, and PO4 levels) pre-treatment.
Record baseline vital signs: weight, temperature, pulse, respirations, and blood pressure.
Explain the hemodialysis procedure and its purpose.
Check equipment and solutions according to protocol.
Use sterile technique to initiate hemodialysis and for needle insertion and catheter connections.
Use gloves, eye protection, and gowns to prevent direct contact with blood.
Initiate hemodialysis according to protocol.
Secure connections and tubing firmly.
Check the monitor system (e.g., flow rate, pressure, temperature, pH level, conductivity, clots, air detector, negative pressure for ultrafiltration, and blood sensor) to ensure patient safety.
Monitor blood pressure, pulse, respirations, temperature, and patient response during hemodialysis.
Administer heparin according to protocol.
Monitor coagulation times and adjust heparin administration accordingly.
Adjust filtration pressures to remove an appropriate amount of fluid.
Institute appropriate protocol if the patient becomes hypotensive.
Discontinue hemodialysis according to protocol.
Compare post-dialysis vital signs and blood chemistry to pre-dialysis values.
Avoid blood pressure measurement or intravenous punctures in the arm with the fistula.
Provide catheter or fistula care according to protocol.
Work collaboratively with the patient to adjust dietary regulations, fluid limitations, and medications to regulate fluid and electrolyte deviations between treatments.
Teach the patient to self-monitor signs and symptoms indicating the need for medical treatment (e.g., fever, bleeding, clotted fistula, thrombophlebitis, and irregular pulse).

Work collaboratively with the patient to alleviate discomfort from side effects of disease and treatment (e.g., cramps, fatigue, headaches, pruritus, anemia, bone demineralization, body image changes, and role disruption).
Work collaboratively with the patient to adjust dialysis duration, dietary regulations, and pain and distraction needs to achieve maximum treatment benefit.

definicao: Assistance in effectively expressing feelings, needs, and ideas while respecting the rights of others.
atividades: Assess barriers to assertiveness (e.g., developmental stage, chronic clinical or psychiatric illness, lack of knowledge, and female socialization).
Assist the patient in recognizing and reducing cognitive distortions that

definicao: Facilitation of memory.
atividades: Discuss with the patient/family any practical memory problems experienced.
Stimulate memory by repeating the patient's last expressed thought as appropriate.
Talk about past experiences with the patient as appropriate.
Implement appropriate memory techniques, such as visual imagery, mnemonic devices, memory games, memory cues, association techniques, list making, computer use, name tags, or information rehearsal.
Assist in associated learning tasks, such as practical learning and recall of presented verbal and pictorial information as appropriate.
Provide orientation training, such as the patient rehearsing personal information and dates as appropriate.
Provide opportunity for concentration, such as a memory game as appropriate.
Provide opportunity to use memory for recent facts, such as questioning the patient about a recent outing as appropriate.
Teach new learning, such as geographic location on a map as appropriate.
Establish image recognition memory as appropriate.
Structure teaching methods according to the patient's information organization.
Refer to occupational therapy as appropriate.
Encourage the patient to participate in group memory training programs as appropriate.
Monitor the patient's behavior during treatment.
Identify and correct the patient's orientation errors.
Monitor changes in memory with training.

definicao: Assisting in self-suggestion of feelings of heaviness and warmth for the purpose of inducing relaxation.
atividades: Choose a calm, comfortable environment.
Prepare a quiet environment.
Take precautions to prevent interruptions.
Instruct the patient on the purpose of the intervention.
Seat the patient in a reclining chair or place them in a recumbent position.
Ask the patient to wear comfortable clothing that does not restrict movement.
Read a prepared script to the patient, pausing long enough for the phrases to be repeated internally.
Use statements in the script that evoke feelings of heaviness, lightness, or floating of specific body parts.
Instruct the patient to repeat the statements to themselves and evoke the sensations in the body part to which they are directed.
Rehearse the script for about 15 to 20 minutes.
Encourage the patient to remain relaxed for an additional 15 to 20 minutes.
Proceed to evoke sensations of warmth after sensations of heaviness have been mastered.
Follow the procedure for evoking the feeling of heaviness, using a prepared script or audio recording to evoke warmth.
Provide instructions for the patient to use at home, with a script or audio recording.
Encourage the patient to practice the session 3 times per day.
Instruct the patient to keep a diary to document progress achieved with each session.

definicao: Establishing an individualized toileting schedule to prevent involuntary bladder emptying for people with limited cognitive or physical ability.
atividades: Involve caregivers in the process of developing the individualized toileting schedule.
Maintain a 3-day continence record to establish a voiding pattern.
Use an electronic monitoring device to establish incontinence episodes as appropriate.
Establish the initial interval of the toileting schedule based on the voiding pattern and usual routine (meals, waking, and retiring).

definicao: Assisting the patient in training the bowel to evacuate at specific intervals.
atividades: Individualize the bowel program with the patient and other appropriate persons.
Consult with the physician and patient regarding the use of suppositories.
Instruct the patient/family on the principles of bowel training.
Instruct the patient on foods high in fiber.
Provide high-fiber foods and/or those identified as helpful by the patient.
Ensure adequate fluid intake.
Ensure adequate exercise.
Initiate training at a consistent, uninterrupted time for defecation.
Ensure privacy.
Administer a suppository as appropriate.
Use a bisacodyl suppository in a polyethylene glycol vehicle if tolerated.
Use a small enema as appropriate.
Instruct the patient on digital rectal dilation as appropriate.
Perform digital rectal dilation as needed.
Determine bowel status regularly.
Modify the bowel training program as needed.

definicao: Assisting the patient in mediating impulsive behavior through the application of problem-solving strategies in social and interpersonal situations.
atividades: Choose a problem-solving strategy appropriate for the patient's developmental level and cognitive functioning.
Use a behavior modification plan to reinforce the problem-solving strategy being taught as appropriate.
Assist the patient in identifying the problem or situation requiring thoughtful action.
Teach the patient to prompt themselves to 'stop and think' before acting impulsively.
Instruct the patient to consider their own thoughts and feelings before acting impulsively.
Demonstrate the steps of the problem-solving strategy in the context of situations meaningful to the patient.
Assist the patient in identifying possible courses of action and their costs/benefits.
Assist the patient in choosing the most beneficial course of action.
Provide positive reinforcement (e.g., praise, rewards) for successful outcomes.
Encourage the patient to self-reward for good outcomes.
Assist the patient in evaluating how unsuccessful outcomes could have been avoided by different behavioral choices.
Offer opportunities for the patient to practice problem-solving (role-playing) within the therapeutic environment.
Encourage the patient to practice problem-solving in social and interpersonal situations outside the therapeutic environment, followed by evaluation of outcomes.

definicao: Improving bladder function for people with urge incontinence by increasing bladder capacity to retain urine and the patient's ability to suppress micturition.
atividades: Determine the ability to recognize urinary urgency.
Encourage the patient to maintain a voiding diary.
Maintain a 3-day continence record to establish a voiding pattern.
Assist the patient in identifying incontinence patterns.
Review the voiding diary with the patient.
Establish the initial interval of the toileting schedule based on the voiding pattern.
Establish the start and end times for the toileting routine if training is not to last 24 hours.
Establish the toileting interval at no less than 1 hour and preferably no less than 2 hours.
Take the patient to the toilet or remind them to void at prescribed intervals.

definicao: Establishing patient care priorities for urgent treatment while allocating scarce resources.
atividades: Prepare area and equipment for triage.
Acquire information about the nature of the problem, emergency, accident, or disaster.
Consider available resources.
Contact appropriate personnel.
Assess critically ill patients at the scene first.
Evacuate the injured as appropriate.
Participate in prioritizing patients for treatment.
Monitor and treat life-threatening injuries or acute needs.
Identify the patient's chief complaint.
Obtain information about the patient's medical history.
Check for medical alert bracelets as appropriate.
Perform a primary survey of all body systems as appropriate.
Initiate appropriate emergency measures as indicated.
Perform a secondary survey of body systems as appropriate.
Attach appropriate identification as indicated by patient status.
Assist in performing diagnostic tests as indicated by the patient's condition.

definicao: Establishing priorities and initiating treatment for patients in the emergency center.
atividades: Monitor breathing and circulation.
Perform crisis intervention as appropriate.
Reduce escalation of violence as appropriate.
Take patients requiring urgent care to the immediate treatment area.
Assess and transfer mothers in labor.
Explain the triage process to those presenting to the service.
Monitor vital signs.
Perform a physical examination relevant to the chief complaint.
Obtain pertinent medical history.
Identify current medications.
Classify according to the severity of the condition.
Refer non-urgent patients to clinics, other primary care providers, or the health department.
Contact poison control resources and initiate treatment as appropriate.
Immobilize potentially fractured extremities as appropriate.
Perform initial burn care as appropriate.
Control bleeding.
Dress wounds.
Preserve amputated parts.
Initiate treatment protocols.
Order diagnostic tests as appropriate.
Assign patients to physicians and/or treatment teams.
Provide information to the receiving caregiver.
Monitor patients awaiting evaluation.
Serve as a liaison between the healthcare team and people in the waiting area.
Answer patient and family questions.
Reassure patients and families.
Perform grief counseling.
Answer phone calls from people requesting information.
Control the flow of visitors and patients.

definicao: Determination of the nature and urgency of problem(s) and provision of guidance on the level of care needed via telephone.
atividades: Identify yourself by name and credentials, organization; inform the person if the call is being recorded (e.g., for quality monitoring).
Demonstrate willingness to help (e.g., 'How can I help?').
Obtain information about the purpose of the call (e.g., medical diagnosis, if applicable, past health history, and current treatment method).
Consider cultural and socioeconomic barriers to patient responses.
Identify patient concerns about health status.
Speak directly with the patient whenever possible.
Direct, facilitate, and calm the listener by giving simple action instructions as needed.

definicao: Providing information about patient care to other healthcare professionals.
atividades: Identify yourself or another referring professional and location.
Obtain a medical order for referral if needed.
Identify essential patient demographic data.
Describe pertinent past health history.
Identify current nursing and medical diagnoses.
Identify resolved nursing and medical diagnoses as appropriate.
Describe the care plan, including diet, medications, and exercise.
Describe nursing interventions being implemented.
Identify equipment and supplies needed for care.
Summarize patient progress toward goals.
Identify expected discharge or transfer date as appropriate.
Identify planned return appointment for follow-up care as appropriate.
Discuss patient strengths and resources.
Describe the family's role in continuity of care.
Identify other institutions providing care.
Request information from healthcare professionals of other institutions.
Coordinate care with other healthcare professionals.
Share patient or family concerns with other healthcare professionals.
Share information from other healthcare professionals with the patient and family as appropriate.
Transfer information via electronic technology if available.
Participate in the development and improvement of electronic health records.
Maintain education related to knowledge of health information technology.

definicao: Performance of ultrasound examinations to determine ovarian, endometrial, uterine, or fetal status.
atividades: Determine the clinical indication for ultrasound (obstetric or gynecologic).
Set up necessary equipment for non-invasive or invasive procedure (e.g., transducer, vaginal transducer).
Instruct the patient and family about the indications and procedure of the examination, its purpose, and limitations.
Prepare the patient physically and emotionally for the procedure.
Apply the appropriate protocol for ultrasound evaluation (e.g., pregnancy evaluation, postmenopausal bleeding evaluation, specialized obstetric procedure evaluation).
Warm the transducer gel for patient comfort.
Instruct the patient to empty the bladder before transvaginal ultrasound.
Position the transducer on the abdomen or in the vagina as appropriate.
Allow the patient to insert the transvaginal transducer if desired.
Obtain a clear image of anatomical structures on the monitor.
Identify the position and size of the uterus and endometrial thickness as appropriate.

definicao: Systematic review and maintenance of the contents of an emergency cart at established time intervals.
atividades: Ensure ease of use of equipment and supplies through appropriate cart layout and proper location of supplies during initial cart setup and with all checks.
Compare the cart's equipment with a list of designated equipment.
Locate all designated equipment and supplies on the cart.
Replace missing or expired supplies and equipment.
Ensure latex-free products are stocked when available according to institutional protocol.
Ensure equipment is functioning by testing it (e.g., assemble the laryngoscope and check bulb function) as indicated.
Ensure the defibrillator remains plugged in and charging between uses.
Test the defibrillator according to device and institutional protocols, including a low-voltage test shock (less than 200).
Clean equipment as needed.
Check the expiration date of all supplies and medications.
Document the cart check according to institutional policy.
Replace equipment, supplies, and medications as technology and guidelines are updated.

Ensure secure storage of supplies, equipment, and patient information according to institutional protocols and government regulations (e.g., Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act [HIPAA]).
Instruct new nursing staff on proper emergency cart check procedures.

definicao: Choosing, putting on, and removing clothing for a person who cannot perform such activities alone.
atividades: Identify areas where the patient needs assistance with dressing.
Monitor the patient's ability to dress.
Dress the patient after personal hygiene is completed.
Encourage participation in clothing selection.
Encourage the use of self-care devices as appropriate.
Dress the affected extremity first as appropriate.
Dress in non-restrictive clothing as appropriate.
Dress in personal clothing as appropriate.

definicao: Empathetic listening to truly understand an individual's situation and work collaboratively over a number of home visits to identify and generate solutions to reduce depressive symptoms.
atividades: Screen for depression by establishing a baseline.
Establish the goal and number of visits.
Establish a pleasant place and time for visits.
Demonstrate interest in the patient.
Maintain patient confidentiality and privacy.
Use personal strengths to establish relationships with the patient.
Use open-ended questions to encourage expression of thoughts, feelings, and concerns.
Use silence to encourage expression of thoughts, feelings, and concerns.
Avoid barriers to active listening (e.g., minimizing feelings, offering easy solutions, interrupting, talking about oneself, and closing prematurely).
Focus completely on the interaction by suppressing prejudice, bias, assumptions, personal concerns, and other distractions.
Use non-verbal behavior to facilitate communication (i.e., be aware that physical posture conveys non-verbal messages).
Listen to the unexpressed message and feeling, as well as the content of the conversation.
Explore the patient's behavior, feelings, and cognition about a situation.
Assist the patient in naming feelings and emotions associated with the situation.
Clarify the message through the use of questions and feedback.
Identify predominant themes.
Assist the patient in generating a comprehensive list of current problems.
Assist the patient in identifying the most important problem.
Assist the patient in generating a list of solutions.
Assist the patient in evaluating the disadvantages and advantages of the generated list.
Encourage the patient to select desired solutions.
Assist the patient in developing a plan to solve the problem.
Explore progress toward problem resolution at the subsequent visit.
Assess depression symptoms at appropriate intervals.
Refer the patient to other healthcare professionals when needed.
